27//! ðŸ¥§ Savoury implementation of the QUIC transport protocol and HTTP/3.
28//!
29//! [quiche] is an implementation of the QUIC transport protocol and HTTP/3 as
30//! specified by the [IETF]. It provides a low level API for processing QUIC
31//! packets and handling connection state. The application is responsible for
32//! providing I/O (e.g. sockets handling) as well as an event loop with support
33//! for timers.
34//!
35//! [quiche]: https://github.com/cloudflare/quiche/
36//! [ietf]: https://quicwg.org/
37//!
38//! ## Configuring connections
39//!
40//! The first step in establishing a QUIC connection using quiche is creating a
41//! [`Config`] object:
42//!
43//! ```
44//! let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
45//! config.set_application_protos(&[b"example-proto"]);
46//!
47//! // Additional configuration specific to application and use case...
48//! # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
49//! ```
50//!
51//! The [`Config`] object controls important aspects of the QUIC connection such
52//! as QUIC version, ALPN IDs, flow control, congestion control, idle timeout
53//! and other properties or features.
54//!
55//! QUIC is a general-purpose transport protocol and there are several
56//! configuration properties where there is no reasonable default value. For
57//! example, the permitted number of concurrent streams of any particular type
58//! is dependent on the application running over QUIC, and other use-case
59//! specific concerns.
60//!
61//! quiche defaults several properties to zero, applications most likely need
62//! to set these to something else to satisfy their needs using the following:
63//!
64//! - [`set_initial_max_streams_bidi()`]
65//! - [`set_initial_max_streams_uni()`]
66//! - [`set_initial_max_data()`]
67//! - [`set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local()`]
68//! - [`set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote()`]
69//! - [`set_initial_max_stream_data_uni()`]
70//!
71//! [`Config`] also holds TLS configuration. This can be changed by mutators on
72//! the an existing object, or by constructing a TLS context manually and
73//! creating a configuration using [`with_boring_ssl_ctx_builder()`].
74//!
75//! A configuration object can be shared among multiple connections.
76//!
77//! ### Connection setup
78//!
79//! On the client-side the [`connect()`] utility function can be used to create
80//! a new connection, while [`accept()`] is for servers:
81//!
82//! ```
83//! # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
84//! # let server_name = "quic.tech";
85//! # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
86//! # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
87//! # let local = "127.0.0.1:4321".parse().unwrap();
88//! // Client connection.
89//! let conn =
90//!     quiche::connect(Some(&server_name), &scid, local, peer, &mut config)?;
91//!
92//! // Server connection.
93//! # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
94//! # let local = "127.0.0.1:4321".parse().unwrap();
95//! let conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
96//! # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
97//! ```
98//!
99//! In both cases, the application is responsible for generating a new source
100//! connection ID that will be used to identify the new connection.
101//!
102//! The application also need to pass the address of the remote peer of the
103//! connection: in the case of a client that would be the address of the server
104//! it is trying to connect to, and for a server that is the address of the
105//! client that initiated the connection.
106//!
107//! ## Handling incoming packets
108//!
109//! Using the connection's [`recv()`] method the application can process
110//! incoming packets that belong to that connection from the network:
111//!
112//! ```no_run
113//! # let mut buf = [0; 512];
114//! # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
115//! # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
116//! # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
117//! # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
118//! # let local = "127.0.0.1:4321".parse().unwrap();
119//! # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
120//! let to = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
121//!
122//! loop {
123//!     let (read, from) = socket.recv_from(&mut buf).unwrap();
124//!
125//!     let recv_info = quiche::RecvInfo { from, to };
126//!
127//!     let read = match conn.recv(&mut buf[..read], recv_info) {
128//!         Ok(v) => v,
129//!
130//!         Err(quiche::Error::Done) => {
131//!             // Done reading.
132//!             break;
133//!         },
134//!
135//!         Err(e) => {
136//!             // An error occurred, handle it.
137//!             break;
138//!         },
139//!     };
140//! }
141//! # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
142//! ```
143//!
144//! The application has to pass a [`RecvInfo`] structure in order to provide
145//! additional information about the received packet (such as the address it
146//! was received from).
147//!
148//! ## Generating outgoing packets
149//!
150//! Outgoing packet are generated using the connection's [`send()`] method
151//! instead:
152//!
153//! ```no_run
154//! # let mut out = [0; 512];
155//! # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
156//! # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
157//! # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
158//! # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
159//! # let local = "127.0.0.1:4321".parse().unwrap();
160//! # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
161//! loop {
162//!     let (write, send_info) = match conn.send(&mut out) {
163//!         Ok(v) => v,
164//!
165//!         Err(quiche::Error::Done) => {
166//!             // Done writing.
167//!             break;
168//!         },
169//!
170//!         Err(e) => {
171//!             // An error occurred, handle it.
172//!             break;
173//!         },
174//!     };
175//!
176//!     socket.send_to(&out[..write], &send_info.to).unwrap();
177//! }
178//! # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
179//! ```
180//!
181//! The application will be provided with a [`SendInfo`] structure providing
182//! additional information about the newly created packet (such as the address
183//! the packet should be sent to).
184//!
185//! When packets are sent, the application is responsible for maintaining a
186//! timer to react to time-based connection events. The timer expiration can be
187//! obtained using the connection's [`timeout()`] method.
188//!
189//! ```
190//! # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
191//! # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
192//! # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
193//! # let local = "127.0.0.1:4321".parse().unwrap();
194//! # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
195//! let timeout = conn.timeout();
196//! # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
197//! ```
198//!
199//! The application is responsible for providing a timer implementation, which
200//! can be specific to the operating system or networking framework used. When
201//! a timer expires, the connection's [`on_timeout()`] method should be called,
202//! after which additional packets might need to be sent on the network:
203//!
204//! ```no_run
205//! # let mut out = [0; 512];
206//! # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
207//! # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
208//! # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
209//! # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
210//! # let local = "127.0.0.1:4321".parse().unwrap();
211//! # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
212//! // Timeout expired, handle it.
213//! conn.on_timeout();
214//!
215//! // Send more packets as needed after timeout.
216//! loop {
217//!     let (write, send_info) = match conn.send(&mut out) {
218//!         Ok(v) => v,
219//!
220//!         Err(quiche::Error::Done) => {
221//!             // Done writing.
222//!             break;
223//!         },
224//!
225//!         Err(e) => {
226//!             // An error occurred, handle it.
227//!             break;
228//!         },
229//!     };
230//!
231//!     socket.send_to(&out[..write], &send_info.to).unwrap();
232//! }
233//! # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
234//! ```
235//!
236//! ### Pacing
237//!
238//! It is recommended that applications [pace] sending of outgoing packets to
239//! avoid creating packet bursts that could cause short-term congestion and
240//! losses in the network.
241//!
242//! quiche exposes pacing hints for outgoing packets through the [`at`] field
243//! of the [`SendInfo`] structure that is returned by the [`send()`] method.
244//! This field represents the time when a specific packet should be sent into
245//! the network.
246//!
247//! Applications can use these hints by artificially delaying the sending of
248//! packets through platform-specific mechanisms (such as the [`SO_TXTIME`]
249//! socket option on Linux), or custom methods (for example by using user-space
250//! timers).
251//!
252//! [pace]: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9002#section-7.7
253//! [`SO_TXTIME`]: https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man8/tc-etf.8.html
254//!
255//! ## Sending and receiving stream data
256//!
257//! After some back and forth, the connection will complete its handshake and
258//! will be ready for sending or receiving application data.
259//!
260//! Data can be sent on a stream by using the [`stream_send()`] method:
261//!
262//! ```no_run
263//! # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
264//! # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
265//! # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
266//! # let local = "127.0.0.1:4321".parse().unwrap();
267//! # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
268//! if conn.is_established() {
269//!     // Handshake completed, send some data on stream 0.
270//!     conn.stream_send(0, b"hello", true)?;
271//! }
272//! # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
273//! ```
274//!
275//! The application can check whether there are any readable streams by using
276//! the connection's [`readable()`] method, which returns an iterator over all
277//! the streams that have outstanding data to read.
278//!
279//! The [`stream_recv()`] method can then be used to retrieve the application
280//! data from the readable stream:
281//!
282//! ```no_run
283//! # let mut buf = [0; 512];
284//! # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
285//! # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
286//! # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
287//! # let local = "127.0.0.1:4321".parse().unwrap();
288//! # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
289//! if conn.is_established() {
290//!     // Iterate over readable streams.
291//!     for stream_id in conn.readable() {
292//!         // Stream is readable, read until there's no more data.
293//!         while let Ok((read, fin)) = conn.stream_recv(stream_id, &mut buf) {
294//!             println!("Got {} bytes on stream {}", read, stream_id);
295//!         }
296//!     }
297//! }
298//! # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
299//! ```
300//!
301//! ## HTTP/3
302//!
303//! The quiche [HTTP/3 module] provides a high level API for sending and
304//! receiving HTTP requests and responses on top of the QUIC transport protocol.
305//!
306//! [`Config`]: https://docs.quic.tech/quiche/struct.Config.html
307//! [`set_initial_max_streams_bidi()`]: https://docs.rs/quiche/latest/quiche/struct.Config.html#method.set_initial_max_streams_bidi
308//! [`set_initial_max_streams_uni()`]: https://docs.rs/quiche/latest/quiche/struct.Config.html#method.set_initial_max_streams_uni
309//! [`set_initial_max_data()`]: https://docs.rs/quiche/latest/quiche/struct.Config.html#method.set_initial_max_data
310//! [`set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local()`]: https://docs.rs/quiche/latest/quiche/struct.Config.html#method.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local
311//! [`set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote()`]: https://docs.rs/quiche/latest/quiche/struct.Config.html#method.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote
312//! [`set_initial_max_stream_data_uni()`]: https://docs.rs/quiche/latest/quiche/struct.Config.html#method.set_initial_max_stream_data_uni
313//! [`with_boring_ssl_ctx_builder()`]: https://docs.quic.tech/quiche/struct.Config.html#method.with_boring_ssl_ctx_builder
314//! [`connect()`]: fn.connect.html
315//! [`accept()`]: fn.accept.html
316//! [`recv()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.recv
317//! [`RecvInfo`]: struct.RecvInfo.html
318//! [`send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.send
319//! [`SendInfo`]: struct.SendInfo.html
320//! [`at`]: struct.SendInfo.html#structfield.at
321//! [`timeout()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.timeout
322//! [`on_timeout()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.on_timeout
323//! [`stream_send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_send
324//! [`readable()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.readable
325//! [`stream_recv()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_recv
326//! [HTTP/3 module]: h3/index.html
327//!
328//! ## Congestion Control
329//!
330//! The quiche library provides a high-level API for configuring which
331//! congestion control algorithm to use throughout the QUIC connection.
332//!
333//! When a QUIC connection is created, the application can optionally choose
334//! which CC algorithm to use. See [`CongestionControlAlgorithm`] for currently
335//! available congestion control algorithms.
336//!
337//! For example:
338//!
339//! ```
340//! let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
341//! config.set_cc_algorithm(quiche::CongestionControlAlgorithm::Reno);
342//! ```
343//!
344//! Alternatively, you can configure the congestion control algorithm to use
345//! by its name.
346//!
347//! ```
348//! let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
349//! config.set_cc_algorithm_name("reno").unwrap();
350//! ```
351//!
352//! Note that the CC algorithm should be configured before calling [`connect()`]
353//! or [`accept()`]. Otherwise the connection will use a default CC algorithm.
354//!
355//! [`CongestionControlAlgorithm`]: enum.CongestionControlAlgorithm.html
356//!
357//! ## Feature flags
358//!
359//! quiche defines a number of [feature flags] to reduce the amount of compiled
360//! code and dependencies:
361//!
362//! * `boringssl-vendored` (default): Build the vendored BoringSSL library.
363//!
364//! * `boringssl-boring-crate`: Use the BoringSSL library provided by the
365//!   [boring] crate. It takes precedence over `boringssl-vendored` if both
366//!   features are enabled.
367//!
368//! * `pkg-config-meta`: Generate pkg-config metadata file for libquiche.
369//!
370//! * `ffi`: Build and expose the FFI API.
371//!
372//! * `qlog`: Enable support for the [qlog] logging format.
373//!
374//! [feature flags]: https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/manifest.html#the-features-section
375//! [boring]: https://crates.io/crates/boring
376//! [qlog]: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-quic-qlog-main-schema
377
378#![allow(clippy::upper_case_acronyms)]
379#![warn(missing_docs)]
380#![cfg_attr(docsrs, feature(doc_cfg))]
381

392#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
393use qlog::events::quic::TransportEventType;
394#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
395use qlog::events::DataRecipient;
396#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
397use qlog::events::Event;
398#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
399use qlog::events::EventData;
400#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
401use qlog::events::EventImportance;
402#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
403use qlog::events::EventType;
404#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
405use qlog::events::RawInfo;
406use stream::StreamPriorityKey;
407
408use std::cmp;
409use std::convert::TryInto;
410use std::time;
411
412use std::sync::Arc;
413
414use std::net::SocketAddr;
415
416use std::str::FromStr;

426/// The current QUIC wire version.
427pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: u32 = PROTOCOL_VERSION_V1;
428
429/// Supported QUIC versions.
430const PROTOCOL_VERSION_V1: u32 = 0x0000_0001;
431
432/// The maximum length of a connection ID.
433pub const MAX_CONN_ID_LEN: usize = crate::packet::MAX_CID_LEN as usize;
434
435/// The minimum length of Initial packets sent by a client.
436pub const MIN_CLIENT_INITIAL_LEN: usize = 1200;
437
438#[cfg(not(feature = "fuzzing"))]
439const PAYLOAD_MIN_LEN: usize = 4;
440
441#[cfg(feature = "fuzzing")]
442// Due to the fact that in fuzzing mode we use a zero-length AEAD tag (which
443// would normally be 16 bytes), we need to adjust the minimum payload size to
444// account for that.
445const PAYLOAD_MIN_LEN: usize = 20;
446
447// PATH_CHALLENGE (9 bytes) + AEAD tag (16 bytes).
448const MIN_PROBING_SIZE: usize = 25;
449
450const MAX_AMPLIFICATION_FACTOR: usize = 3;
451
452// The maximum number of tracked packet number ranges that need to be acked.
453//
454// This represents more or less how many ack blocks can fit in a typical packet.
455const MAX_ACK_RANGES: usize = 68;
456
457// The highest possible stream ID allowed.
458const MAX_STREAM_ID: u64 = 1 << 60;
459
460// The default max_datagram_size used in congestion control.
461const MAX_SEND_UDP_PAYLOAD_SIZE: usize = 1200;
462
463// The default length of DATAGRAM queues.
464const DEFAULT_MAX_DGRAM_QUEUE_LEN: usize = 0;
465
466// The default length of PATH_CHALLENGE receive queue.
467const DEFAULT_MAX_PATH_CHALLENGE_RX_QUEUE_LEN: usize = 3;
468
469// The DATAGRAM standard recommends either none or 65536 as maximum DATAGRAM
470// frames size. We enforce the recommendation for forward compatibility.
471const MAX_DGRAM_FRAME_SIZE: u64 = 65536;
472
473// The length of the payload length field.
474const PAYLOAD_LENGTH_LEN: usize = 2;
475
476// The number of undecryptable that can be buffered.
477const MAX_UNDECRYPTABLE_PACKETS: usize = 10;
478
479const RESERVED_VERSION_MASK: u32 = 0xfafafafa;
480
481// The default size of the receiver connection flow control window.
482const DEFAULT_CONNECTION_WINDOW: u64 = 48 * 1024;
483
484// The maximum size of the receiver connection flow control window.
485const MAX_CONNECTION_WINDOW: u64 = 24 * 1024 * 1024;
486
487// How much larger the connection flow control window need to be larger than
488// the stream flow control window.
489const CONNECTION_WINDOW_FACTOR: f64 = 1.5;
490
491// How many probing packet timeouts do we tolerate before considering the path
492// validation as failed.
493const MAX_PROBING_TIMEOUTS: usize = 3;
494
495// The default initial congestion window size in terms of packet count.
496const DEFAULT_INITIAL_CONGESTION_WINDOW_PACKETS: usize = 10;
497
498// The maximum data offset that can be stored in a crypto stream.
499const MAX_CRYPTO_STREAM_OFFSET: u64 = 1 << 16;
500
501/// A specialized [`Result`] type for quiche operations.
502///
503/// This type is used throughout quiche's public API for any operation that
504/// can produce an error.
505///
506/// [`Result`]: https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/result/enum.Result.html
507pub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, Error>;
508
509/// A QUIC error.
510#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
511pub enum Error {
512    /// There is no more work to do.
513    Done,
514
515    /// The provided buffer is too short.
516    BufferTooShort,
517
518    /// The provided packet cannot be parsed because its version is unknown.
519    UnknownVersion,
520
521    /// The provided packet cannot be parsed because it contains an invalid
522    /// frame.
523    InvalidFrame,
524
525    /// The provided packet cannot be parsed.
526    InvalidPacket,
527
528    /// The operation cannot be completed because the connection is in an
529    /// invalid state.
530    InvalidState,
531
532    /// The operation cannot be completed because the stream is in an
533    /// invalid state.
534    ///
535    /// The stream ID is provided as associated data.
536    InvalidStreamState(u64),
537
538    /// The peer's transport params cannot be parsed.
539    InvalidTransportParam,
540
541    /// A cryptographic operation failed.
542    CryptoFail,
543
544    /// The TLS handshake failed.
545    TlsFail,
546
547    /// The peer violated the local flow control limits.
548    FlowControl,
549
550    /// The peer violated the local stream limits.
551    StreamLimit,
552
553    /// The specified stream was stopped by the peer.
554    ///
555    /// The error code sent as part of the `STOP_SENDING` frame is provided as
556    /// associated data.
557    StreamStopped(u64),
558
559    /// The specified stream was reset by the peer.
560    ///
561    /// The error code sent as part of the `RESET_STREAM` frame is provided as
562    /// associated data.
563    StreamReset(u64),
564
565    /// The received data exceeds the stream's final size.
566    FinalSize,
567
568    /// Error in congestion control.
569    CongestionControl,
570
571    /// Too many identifiers were provided.
572    IdLimit,
573
574    /// Not enough available identifiers.
575    OutOfIdentifiers,
576
577    /// Error in key update.
578    KeyUpdate,
579
580    /// The peer sent more data in CRYPTO frames than we can buffer.
581    CryptoBufferExceeded,
582}
583
584/// QUIC error codes sent on the wire.
585///
586/// As defined in [RFC9000](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#name-error-codes).
587#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]
588pub enum WireErrorCode {
589    /// An endpoint uses this with CONNECTION_CLOSE to signal that the
590    /// connection is being closed abruptly in the absence of any error.
591    NoError              = 0x0,
592    /// The endpoint encountered an internal error and cannot continue with the
593    /// connection.
594    InternalError        = 0x1,
595    /// The server refused to accept a new connection.
596    ConnectionRefused    = 0x2,
597    /// An endpoint received more data than it permitted in its advertised data
598    /// limits; see Section 4.
599    FlowControlError     = 0x3,
600    /// An endpoint received a frame for a stream identifier that exceeded its
601    /// advertised stream limit for the corresponding stream type.
602    StreamLimitError     = 0x4,
603    /// An endpoint received a frame for a stream that was not in a state that
604    /// permitted that frame.
605    StreamStateError     = 0x5,
606    /// (1) An endpoint received a STREAM frame containing data that exceeded
607    /// the previously established final size, (2) an endpoint received a
608    /// STREAM frame or a RESET_STREAM frame containing a final size that
609    /// was lower than the size of stream data that was already received, or
610    /// (3) an endpoint received a STREAM frame or a RESET_STREAM frame
611    /// containing a different final size to the one already established.
612    FinalSizeError       = 0x6,
613    /// An endpoint received a frame that was badly formatted -- for instance, a
614    /// frame of an unknown type or an ACK frame that has more
615    /// acknowledgment ranges than the remainder of the packet could carry.
616    FrameEncodingError   = 0x7,
617    /// An endpoint received transport parameters that were badly formatted,
618    /// included an invalid value, omitted a mandatory transport parameter,
619    /// included a forbidden transport parameter, or were otherwise in
620    /// error.
621    TransportParameterError = 0x8,
622    /// An endpoint received transport parameters that were badly formatted,
623    /// included an invalid value, omitted a mandatory transport parameter,
624    /// included a forbidden transport parameter, or were otherwise in
625    /// error.
626    ConnectionIdLimitError = 0x9,
627    /// An endpoint detected an error with protocol compliance that was not
628    /// covered by more specific error codes.
629    ProtocolViolation    = 0xa,
630    /// A server received a client Initial that contained an invalid Token
631    /// field.
632    InvalidToken         = 0xb,
633    /// The application or application protocol caused the connection to be
634    /// closed.
635    ApplicationError     = 0xc,
636    /// An endpoint has received more data in CRYPTO frames than it can buffer.
637    CryptoBufferExceeded = 0xd,
638    /// An endpoint detected errors in performing key updates.
639    KeyUpdateError       = 0xe,
640    /// An endpoint has reached the confidentiality or integrity limit for the
641    /// AEAD algorithm used by the given connection.
642    AeadLimitReached     = 0xf,
643    /// An endpoint has determined that the network path is incapable of
644    /// supporting QUIC. An endpoint is unlikely to receive a
645    /// CONNECTION_CLOSE frame carrying this code except when the path does
646    /// not support a large enough MTU.
647    NoViablePath         = 0x10,
648}
649
650impl Error {
651    fn to_wire(self) -> u64 {
652        match self {
653            Error::Done => WireErrorCode::NoError as u64,
654            Error::InvalidFrame => WireErrorCode::FrameEncodingError as u64,
655            Error::InvalidStreamState(..) =>
656                WireErrorCode::StreamStateError as u64,
657            Error::InvalidTransportParam =>
658                WireErrorCode::TransportParameterError as u64,
659            Error::FlowControl => WireErrorCode::FlowControlError as u64,
660            Error::StreamLimit => WireErrorCode::StreamLimitError as u64,
661            Error::IdLimit => WireErrorCode::ConnectionIdLimitError as u64,
662            Error::FinalSize => WireErrorCode::FinalSizeError as u64,
663            Error::CryptoBufferExceeded =>
664                WireErrorCode::CryptoBufferExceeded as u64,
665            Error::KeyUpdate => WireErrorCode::KeyUpdateError as u64,
666            _ => WireErrorCode::ProtocolViolation as u64,
667        }
668    }
669
670    #[cfg(feature = "ffi")]
671    fn to_c(self) -> libc::ssize_t {
672        match self {
673            Error::Done => -1,
674            Error::BufferTooShort => -2,
675            Error::UnknownVersion => -3,
676            Error::InvalidFrame => -4,
677            Error::InvalidPacket => -5,
678            Error::InvalidState => -6,
679            Error::InvalidStreamState(_) => -7,
680            Error::InvalidTransportParam => -8,
681            Error::CryptoFail => -9,
682            Error::TlsFail => -10,
683            Error::FlowControl => -11,
684            Error::StreamLimit => -12,
685            Error::FinalSize => -13,
686            Error::CongestionControl => -14,
687            Error::StreamStopped { .. } => -15,
688            Error::StreamReset { .. } => -16,
689            Error::IdLimit => -17,
690            Error::OutOfIdentifiers => -18,
691            Error::KeyUpdate => -19,
692            Error::CryptoBufferExceeded => -20,
693        }
694    }
695}
696
697impl std::fmt::Display for Error {
698    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter) -> std::fmt::Result {
699        write!(f, "{self:?}")
700    }
701}
702
703impl std::error::Error for Error {
704    fn source(&self) -> Option<&(dyn std::error::Error + 'static)> {
705        None
706    }
707}
708
709impl std::convert::From<octets::BufferTooShortError> for Error {
710    fn from(_err: octets::BufferTooShortError) -> Self {
711        Error::BufferTooShort
712    }
713}
714
715/// Ancillary information about incoming packets.
716#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
717pub struct RecvInfo {
718    /// The remote address the packet was received from.
719    pub from: SocketAddr,
720
721    /// The local address the packet was received on.
722    pub to: SocketAddr,
723}
724
725/// Ancillary information about outgoing packets.
726#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
727pub struct SendInfo {
728    /// The local address the packet should be sent from.
729    pub from: SocketAddr,
730
731    /// The remote address the packet should be sent to.
732    pub to: SocketAddr,
733
734    /// The time to send the packet out.
735    ///
736    /// See [Pacing] for more details.
737    ///
738    /// [Pacing]: index.html#pacing
739    pub at: time::Instant,
740}
741
742/// Represents information carried by `CONNECTION_CLOSE` frames.
743#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
744pub struct ConnectionError {
745    /// Whether the error came from the application or the transport layer.
746    pub is_app: bool,
747
748    /// The error code carried by the `CONNECTION_CLOSE` frame.
749    pub error_code: u64,
750
751    /// The reason carried by the `CONNECTION_CLOSE` frame.
752    pub reason: Vec<u8>,
753}
754
755/// The side of the stream to be shut down.
756///
757/// This should be used when calling [`stream_shutdown()`].
758///
759/// [`stream_shutdown()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_shutdown
760#[repr(C)]
761#[derive(PartialEq, Eq)]
762pub enum Shutdown {
763    /// Stop receiving stream data.
764    Read  = 0,
765
766    /// Stop sending stream data.
767    Write = 1,
768}
769
770/// Qlog logging level.
771#[repr(C)]
772#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
773#[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "qlog")))]
774pub enum QlogLevel {
775    /// Logs any events of Core importance.
776    Core  = 0,
777
778    /// Logs any events of Core and Base importance.
779    Base  = 1,
780
781    /// Logs any events of Core, Base and Extra importance
782    Extra = 2,
783}
784
785/// Stores configuration shared between multiple connections.
786pub struct Config {
787    local_transport_params: TransportParams,
788
789    version: u32,
790
791    tls_ctx: tls::Context,
792
793    application_protos: Vec<Vec<u8>>,
794
795    grease: bool,
796
797    cc_algorithm: CongestionControlAlgorithm,
798    initial_congestion_window_packets: usize,
799
800    pmtud: bool,
801
802    hystart: bool,
803
804    pacing: bool,
805    /// Send rate limit in Mbps
806    max_pacing_rate: Option<u64>,
807
808    dgram_recv_max_queue_len: usize,
809    dgram_send_max_queue_len: usize,
810
811    path_challenge_recv_max_queue_len: usize,
812
813    max_send_udp_payload_size: usize,
814
815    max_connection_window: u64,
816    max_stream_window: u64,
817
818    max_amplification_factor: usize,
819
820    disable_dcid_reuse: bool,
821
822    track_unknown_transport_params: Option<usize>,
823}
824
825// See https://quicwg.org/base-drafts/rfc9000.html#section-15
826fn is_reserved_version(version: u32) -> bool {
827    version & RESERVED_VERSION_MASK == version
828}
829
830impl Config {
831    /// Creates a config object with the given version.
832    ///
833    /// ## Examples:
834    ///
835    /// ```
836    /// let config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
837    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
838    /// ```
839    pub fn new(version: u32) -> Result<Config> {
840        Self::with_tls_ctx(version, tls::Context::new()?)
841    }
842
843    /// Creates a config object with the given version and
844    /// [`SslContextBuilder`].
845    ///
846    /// This is useful for applications that wish to manually configure
847    /// [`SslContextBuilder`].
848    ///
849    /// [`SslContextBuilder`]: https://docs.rs/boring/latest/boring/ssl/struct.SslContextBuilder.html
850    #[cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")]
851    #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")))]
852    pub fn with_boring_ssl_ctx_builder(
853        version: u32, tls_ctx_builder: boring::ssl::SslContextBuilder,
854    ) -> Result<Config> {
855        Self::with_tls_ctx(version, tls::Context::from_boring(tls_ctx_builder))
856    }
857
858    fn with_tls_ctx(version: u32, tls_ctx: tls::Context) -> Result<Config> {
859        if !is_reserved_version(version) && !version_is_supported(version) {
860            return Err(Error::UnknownVersion);
861        }
862
863        Ok(Config {
864            local_transport_params: TransportParams::default(),
865            version,
866            tls_ctx,
867            application_protos: Vec::new(),
868            grease: true,
869            cc_algorithm: CongestionControlAlgorithm::CUBIC,
870            initial_congestion_window_packets:
871                DEFAULT_INITIAL_CONGESTION_WINDOW_PACKETS,
872            pmtud: false,
873            hystart: true,
874            pacing: true,
875            max_pacing_rate: None,
876
877            dgram_recv_max_queue_len: DEFAULT_MAX_DGRAM_QUEUE_LEN,
878            dgram_send_max_queue_len: DEFAULT_MAX_DGRAM_QUEUE_LEN,
879
880            path_challenge_recv_max_queue_len:
881                DEFAULT_MAX_PATH_CHALLENGE_RX_QUEUE_LEN,
882
883            max_send_udp_payload_size: MAX_SEND_UDP_PAYLOAD_SIZE,
884
885            max_connection_window: MAX_CONNECTION_WINDOW,
886            max_stream_window: stream::MAX_STREAM_WINDOW,
887
888            max_amplification_factor: MAX_AMPLIFICATION_FACTOR,
889
890            disable_dcid_reuse: false,
891
892            track_unknown_transport_params: None,
893        })
894    }
895
896    /// Configures the given certificate chain.
897    ///
898    /// The content of `file` is parsed as a PEM-encoded leaf certificate,
899    /// followed by optional intermediate certificates.
900    ///
901    /// ## Examples:
902    ///
903    /// ```no_run
904    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(0xbabababa)?;
905    /// config.load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("/path/to/cert.pem")?;
906    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
907    /// ```
908    pub fn load_cert_chain_from_pem_file(&mut self, file: &str) -> Result<()> {
909        self.tls_ctx.use_certificate_chain_file(file)
910    }
911
912    /// Configures the given private key.
913    ///
914    /// The content of `file` is parsed as a PEM-encoded private key.
915    ///
916    /// ## Examples:
917    ///
918    /// ```no_run
919    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(0xbabababa)?;
920    /// config.load_priv_key_from_pem_file("/path/to/key.pem")?;
921    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
922    /// ```
923    pub fn load_priv_key_from_pem_file(&mut self, file: &str) -> Result<()> {
924        self.tls_ctx.use_privkey_file(file)
925    }
926
927    /// Specifies a file where trusted CA certificates are stored for the
928    /// purposes of certificate verification.
929    ///
930    /// The content of `file` is parsed as a PEM-encoded certificate chain.
931    ///
932    /// ## Examples:
933    ///
934    /// ```no_run
935    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(0xbabababa)?;
936    /// config.load_verify_locations_from_file("/path/to/cert.pem")?;
937    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
938    /// ```
939    pub fn load_verify_locations_from_file(&mut self, file: &str) -> Result<()> {
940        self.tls_ctx.load_verify_locations_from_file(file)
941    }
942
943    /// Specifies a directory where trusted CA certificates are stored for the
944    /// purposes of certificate verification.
945    ///
946    /// The content of `dir` a set of PEM-encoded certificate chains.
947    ///
948    /// ## Examples:
949    ///
950    /// ```no_run
951    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(0xbabababa)?;
952    /// config.load_verify_locations_from_directory("/path/to/certs")?;
953    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
954    /// ```
955    pub fn load_verify_locations_from_directory(
956        &mut self, dir: &str,
957    ) -> Result<()> {
958        self.tls_ctx.load_verify_locations_from_directory(dir)
959    }
960
961    /// Configures whether to verify the peer's certificate.
962    ///
963    /// This should usually be `true` for client-side connections and `false`
964    /// for server-side ones.
965    ///
966    /// Note that by default, no verification is performed.
967    ///
968    /// Also note that on the server-side, enabling verification of the peer
969    /// will trigger a certificate request and make authentication errors
970    /// fatal, but will still allow anonymous clients (i.e. clients that
971    /// don't present a certificate at all). Servers can check whether a
972    /// client presented a certificate by calling [`peer_cert()`] if they
973    /// need to.
974    ///
975    /// [`peer_cert()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.peer_cert
976    pub fn verify_peer(&mut self, verify: bool) {
977        self.tls_ctx.set_verify(verify);
978    }
979
980    /// Configures whether to do path MTU discovery.
981    ///
982    /// The default value is `false`.
983    pub fn discover_pmtu(&mut self, discover: bool) {
984        self.pmtud = discover;
985    }
986
987    /// Configures whether to send GREASE values.
988    ///
989    /// The default value is `true`.
990    pub fn grease(&mut self, grease: bool) {
991        self.grease = grease;
992    }
993
994    /// Enables logging of secrets.
995    ///
996    /// When logging is enabled, the [`set_keylog()`] method must be called on
997    /// the connection for its cryptographic secrets to be logged in the
998    /// [keylog] format to the specified writer.
999    ///
1000    /// [`set_keylog()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.set_keylog
1001    /// [keylog]: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Mozilla/Projects/NSS/Key_Log_Format
1002    pub fn log_keys(&mut self) {
1003        self.tls_ctx.enable_keylog();
1004    }
1005
1006    /// Configures the session ticket key material.
1007    ///
1008    /// On the server this key will be used to encrypt and decrypt session
1009    /// tickets, used to perform session resumption without server-side state.
1010    ///
1011    /// By default a key is generated internally, and rotated regularly, so
1012    /// applications don't need to call this unless they need to use a
1013    /// specific key (e.g. in order to support resumption across multiple
1014    /// servers), in which case the application is also responsible for
1015    /// rotating the key to provide forward secrecy.
1016    pub fn set_ticket_key(&mut self, key: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
1017        self.tls_ctx.set_ticket_key(key)
1018    }
1019
1020    /// Enables sending or receiving early data.
1021    pub fn enable_early_data(&mut self) {
1022        self.tls_ctx.set_early_data_enabled(true);
1023    }
1024
1025    /// Configures the list of supported application protocols.
1026    ///
1027    /// On the client this configures the list of protocols to send to the
1028    /// server as part of the ALPN extension.
1029    ///
1030    /// On the server this configures the list of supported protocols to match
1031    /// against the client-supplied list.
1032    ///
1033    /// Applications must set a value, but no default is provided.
1034    ///
1035    /// ## Examples:
1036    ///
1037    /// ```
1038    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(0xbabababa)?;
1039    /// config.set_application_protos(&[b"http/1.1", b"http/0.9"]);
1040    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
1041    /// ```
1042    pub fn set_application_protos(
1043        &mut self, protos_list: &[&[u8]],
1044    ) -> Result<()> {
1045        self.application_protos =
1046            protos_list.iter().map(|s| s.to_vec()).collect();
1047
1048        self.tls_ctx.set_alpn(protos_list)
1049    }
1050
1051    /// Configures the list of supported application protocols using wire
1052    /// format.
1053    ///
1054    /// The list of protocols `protos` must be a series of non-empty, 8-bit
1055    /// length-prefixed strings.
1056    ///
1057    /// See [`set_application_protos`](Self::set_application_protos) for more
1058    /// background about application protocols.
1059    ///
1060    /// ## Examples:
1061    ///
1062    /// ```
1063    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(0xbabababa)?;
1064    /// config.set_application_protos_wire_format(b"\x08http/1.1\x08http/0.9")?;
1065    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
1066    /// ```
1067    pub fn set_application_protos_wire_format(
1068        &mut self, protos: &[u8],
1069    ) -> Result<()> {
1070        let mut b = octets::Octets::with_slice(protos);
1071
1072        let mut protos_list = Vec::new();
1073
1074        while let Ok(proto) = b.get_bytes_with_u8_length() {
1075            protos_list.push(proto.buf());
1076        }
1077
1078        self.set_application_protos(&protos_list)
1079    }
1080
1081    /// Sets the anti-amplification limit factor.
1082    ///
1083    /// The default value is `3`.
1084    pub fn set_max_amplification_factor(&mut self, v: usize) {
1085        self.max_amplification_factor = v;
1086    }
1087
1088    /// Sets the `max_idle_timeout` transport parameter, in milliseconds.
1089    ///
1090    /// The default value is infinite, that is, no timeout is used.
1091    pub fn set_max_idle_timeout(&mut self, v: u64) {
1092        self.local_transport_params.max_idle_timeout = v;
1093    }
1094
1095    /// Sets the `max_udp_payload_size transport` parameter.
1096    ///
1097    /// The default value is `65527`.
1098    pub fn set_max_recv_udp_payload_size(&mut self, v: usize) {
1099        self.local_transport_params.max_udp_payload_size = v as u64;
1100    }
1101
1102    /// Sets the maximum outgoing UDP payload size.
1103    ///
1104    /// The default and minimum value is `1200`.
1105    pub fn set_max_send_udp_payload_size(&mut self, v: usize) {
1106        self.max_send_udp_payload_size = cmp::max(v, MAX_SEND_UDP_PAYLOAD_SIZE);
1107    }
1108
1109    /// Sets the `initial_max_data` transport parameter.
1110    ///
1111    /// When set to a non-zero value quiche will only allow at most `v` bytes of
1112    /// incoming stream data to be buffered for the whole connection (that is,
1113    /// data that is not yet read by the application) and will allow more data
1114    /// to be received as the buffer is consumed by the application.
1115    ///
1116    /// When set to zero, either explicitly or via the default, quiche will not
1117    /// give any flow control to the peer, preventing it from sending any stream
1118    /// data.
1119    ///
1120    /// The default value is `0`.
1121    pub fn set_initial_max_data(&mut self, v: u64) {
1122        self.local_transport_params.initial_max_data = v;
1123    }
1124
1125    /// Sets the `initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local` transport parameter.
1126    ///
1127    /// When set to a non-zero value quiche will only allow at most `v` bytes
1128    /// of incoming stream data to be buffered for each locally-initiated
1129    /// bidirectional stream (that is, data that is not yet read by the
1130    /// application) and will allow more data to be received as the buffer is
1131    /// consumed by the application.
1132    ///
1133    /// When set to zero, either explicitly or via the default, quiche will not
1134    /// give any flow control to the peer, preventing it from sending any stream
1135    /// data.
1136    ///
1137    /// The default value is `0`.
1138    pub fn set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(&mut self, v: u64) {
1139        self.local_transport_params
1140            .initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local = v;
1141    }
1142
1143    /// Sets the `initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote` transport parameter.
1144    ///
1145    /// When set to a non-zero value quiche will only allow at most `v` bytes
1146    /// of incoming stream data to be buffered for each remotely-initiated
1147    /// bidirectional stream (that is, data that is not yet read by the
1148    /// application) and will allow more data to be received as the buffer is
1149    /// consumed by the application.
1150    ///
1151    /// When set to zero, either explicitly or via the default, quiche will not
1152    /// give any flow control to the peer, preventing it from sending any stream
1153    /// data.
1154    ///
1155    /// The default value is `0`.
1156    pub fn set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(&mut self, v: u64) {
1157        self.local_transport_params
1158            .initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote = v;
1159    }
1160
1161    /// Sets the `initial_max_stream_data_uni` transport parameter.
1162    ///
1163    /// When set to a non-zero value quiche will only allow at most `v` bytes
1164    /// of incoming stream data to be buffered for each unidirectional stream
1165    /// (that is, data that is not yet read by the application) and will allow
1166    /// more data to be received as the buffer is consumed by the application.
1167    ///
1168    /// When set to zero, either explicitly or via the default, quiche will not
1169    /// give any flow control to the peer, preventing it from sending any stream
1170    /// data.
1171    ///
1172    /// The default value is `0`.
1173    pub fn set_initial_max_stream_data_uni(&mut self, v: u64) {
1174        self.local_transport_params.initial_max_stream_data_uni = v;
1175    }
1176
1177    /// Sets the `initial_max_streams_bidi` transport parameter.
1178    ///
1179    /// When set to a non-zero value quiche will only allow `v` number of
1180    /// concurrent remotely-initiated bidirectional streams to be open at any
1181    /// given time and will increase the limit automatically as streams are
1182    /// completed.
1183    ///
1184    /// When set to zero, either explicitly or via the default, quiche will not
1185    /// not allow the peer to open any bidirectional streams.
1186    ///
1187    /// A bidirectional stream is considered completed when all incoming data
1188    /// has been read by the application (up to the `fin` offset) or the
1189    /// stream's read direction has been shutdown, and all outgoing data has
1190    /// been acked by the peer (up to the `fin` offset) or the stream's write
1191    /// direction has been shutdown.
1192    ///
1193    /// The default value is `0`.
1194    pub fn set_initial_max_streams_bidi(&mut self, v: u64) {
1195        self.local_transport_params.initial_max_streams_bidi = v;
1196    }
1197
1198    /// Sets the `initial_max_streams_uni` transport parameter.
1199    ///
1200    /// When set to a non-zero value quiche will only allow `v` number of
1201    /// concurrent remotely-initiated unidirectional streams to be open at any
1202    /// given time and will increase the limit automatically as streams are
1203    /// completed.
1204    ///
1205    /// When set to zero, either explicitly or via the default, quiche will not
1206    /// not allow the peer to open any unidirectional streams.
1207    ///
1208    /// A unidirectional stream is considered completed when all incoming data
1209    /// has been read by the application (up to the `fin` offset) or the
1210    /// stream's read direction has been shutdown.
1211    ///
1212    /// The default value is `0`.
1213    pub fn set_initial_max_streams_uni(&mut self, v: u64) {
1214        self.local_transport_params.initial_max_streams_uni = v;
1215    }
1216
1217    /// Sets the `ack_delay_exponent` transport parameter.
1218    ///
1219    /// The default value is `3`.
1220    pub fn set_ack_delay_exponent(&mut self, v: u64) {
1221        self.local_transport_params.ack_delay_exponent = v;
1222    }
1223
1224    /// Sets the `max_ack_delay` transport parameter.
1225    ///
1226    /// The default value is `25`.
1227    pub fn set_max_ack_delay(&mut self, v: u64) {
1228        self.local_transport_params.max_ack_delay = v;
1229    }
1230
1231    /// Sets the `active_connection_id_limit` transport parameter.
1232    ///
1233    /// The default value is `2`. Lower values will be ignored.
1234    pub fn set_active_connection_id_limit(&mut self, v: u64) {
1235        if v >= 2 {
1236            self.local_transport_params.active_conn_id_limit = v;
1237        }
1238    }
1239
1240    /// Sets the `disable_active_migration` transport parameter.
1241    ///
1242    /// The default value is `false`.
1243    pub fn set_disable_active_migration(&mut self, v: bool) {
1244        self.local_transport_params.disable_active_migration = v;
1245    }
1246
1247    /// Sets the congestion control algorithm used.
1248    ///
1249    /// The default value is `CongestionControlAlgorithm::CUBIC`.
1250    pub fn set_cc_algorithm(&mut self, algo: CongestionControlAlgorithm) {
1251        self.cc_algorithm = algo;
1252    }
1253
1254    /// Sets the congestion control algorithm used by string.
1255    ///
1256    /// The default value is `cubic`. On error `Error::CongestionControl`
1257    /// will be returned.
1258    ///
1259    /// ## Examples:
1260    ///
1261    /// ```
1262    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(0xbabababa)?;
1263    /// config.set_cc_algorithm_name("reno");
1264    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
1265    /// ```
1266    pub fn set_cc_algorithm_name(&mut self, name: &str) -> Result<()> {
1267        self.cc_algorithm = CongestionControlAlgorithm::from_str(name)?;
1268
1269        Ok(())
1270    }
1271
1272    /// Sets initial congestion window size in terms of packet count.
1273    ///
1274    /// The default value is 10.
1275    pub fn set_initial_congestion_window_packets(&mut self, packets: usize) {
1276        self.initial_congestion_window_packets = packets;
1277    }
1278
1279    /// Configures whether to enable HyStart++.
1280    ///
1281    /// The default value is `true`.
1282    pub fn enable_hystart(&mut self, v: bool) {
1283        self.hystart = v;
1284    }
1285
1286    /// Configures whether to enable pacing.
1287    ///
1288    /// The default value is `true`.
1289    pub fn enable_pacing(&mut self, v: bool) {
1290        self.pacing = v;
1291    }
1292
1293    /// Sets the max value for pacing rate.
1294    ///
1295    /// By default pacing rate is not limited.
1296    pub fn set_max_pacing_rate(&mut self, v: u64) {
1297        self.max_pacing_rate = Some(v);
1298    }
1299
1300    /// Configures whether to enable receiving DATAGRAM frames.
1301    ///
1302    /// When enabled, the `max_datagram_frame_size` transport parameter is set
1303    /// to 65536 as recommended by draft-ietf-quic-datagram-01.
1304    ///
1305    /// The default is `false`.
1306    pub fn enable_dgram(
1307        &mut self, enabled: bool, recv_queue_len: usize, send_queue_len: usize,
1308    ) {
1309        self.local_transport_params.max_datagram_frame_size = if enabled {
1310            Some(MAX_DGRAM_FRAME_SIZE)
1311        } else {
1312            None
1313        };
1314        self.dgram_recv_max_queue_len = recv_queue_len;
1315        self.dgram_send_max_queue_len = send_queue_len;
1316    }
1317
1318    /// Configures the max number of queued received PATH_CHALLENGE frames.
1319    ///
1320    /// When an endpoint receives a PATH_CHALLENGE frame and the queue is full,
1321    /// the frame is discarded.
1322    ///
1323    /// The default is 3.
1324    pub fn set_path_challenge_recv_max_queue_len(&mut self, queue_len: usize) {
1325        self.path_challenge_recv_max_queue_len = queue_len;
1326    }
1327
1328    /// Sets the maximum size of the connection window.
1329    ///
1330    /// The default value is MAX_CONNECTION_WINDOW (24MBytes).
1331    pub fn set_max_connection_window(&mut self, v: u64) {
1332        self.max_connection_window = v;
1333    }
1334
1335    /// Sets the maximum size of the stream window.
1336    ///
1337    /// The default value is MAX_STREAM_WINDOW (16MBytes).
1338    pub fn set_max_stream_window(&mut self, v: u64) {
1339        self.max_stream_window = v;
1340    }
1341
1342    /// Sets the initial stateless reset token.
1343    ///
1344    /// This value is only advertised by servers. Setting a stateless retry
1345    /// token as a client has no effect on the connection.
1346    ///
1347    /// The default value is `None`.
1348    pub fn set_stateless_reset_token(&mut self, v: Option<u128>) {
1349        self.local_transport_params.stateless_reset_token = v;
1350    }
1351
1352    /// Sets whether the QUIC connection should avoid reusing DCIDs over
1353    /// different paths.
1354    ///
1355    /// When set to `true`, it ensures that a destination Connection ID is never
1356    /// reused on different paths. Such behaviour may lead to connection stall
1357    /// if the peer performs a non-voluntary migration (e.g., NAT rebinding) and
1358    /// does not provide additional destination Connection IDs to handle such
1359    /// event.
1360    ///
1361    /// The default value is `false`.
1362    pub fn set_disable_dcid_reuse(&mut self, v: bool) {
1363        self.disable_dcid_reuse = v;
1364    }
1365
1366    /// Enables tracking unknown transport parameters.
1367    ///
1368    /// Specify the maximum number of bytes used to track unknown transport
1369    /// parameters. The size includes the identifier and its value. If storing a
1370    /// transport parameter would cause the limit to be exceeded, it is quietly
1371    /// dropped.
1372    ///
1373    /// The default is that the feature is disabled.
1374    pub fn enable_track_unknown_transport_parameters(&mut self, size: usize) {
1375        self.track_unknown_transport_params = Some(size);
1376    }
1377}
1378
1379/// A QUIC connection.
1380pub struct Connection {
1381    /// QUIC wire version used for the connection.
1382    version: u32,
1383
1384    /// Connection Identifiers.
1385    ids: cid::ConnectionIdentifiers,
1386
1387    /// Unique opaque ID for the connection that can be used for logging.
1388    trace_id: String,
1389
1390    /// Packet number spaces.
1391    pkt_num_spaces: [packet::PktNumSpace; packet::Epoch::count()],
1392
1393    /// Next packet number.
1394    next_pkt_num: u64,
1395
1396    /// Peer's transport parameters.
1397    peer_transport_params: TransportParams,
1398
1399    /// If tracking unknown transport parameters from a peer, how much space to
1400    /// use in bytes.
1401    peer_transport_params_track_unknown: Option<usize>,
1402
1403    /// Local transport parameters.
1404    local_transport_params: TransportParams,
1405
1406    /// TLS handshake state.
1407    handshake: tls::Handshake,
1408
1409    /// Serialized TLS session buffer.
1410    ///
1411    /// This field is populated when a new session ticket is processed on the
1412    /// client. On the server this is empty.
1413    session: Option<Vec<u8>>,
1414
1415    /// The configuration for recovery.
1416    recovery_config: recovery::RecoveryConfig,
1417
1418    /// The path manager.
1419    paths: path::PathMap,
1420
1421    /// PATH_CHALLENGE receive queue max length.
1422    path_challenge_recv_max_queue_len: usize,
1423
1424    /// Total number of received PATH_CHALLENGE frames.
1425    path_challenge_rx_count: u64,
1426
1427    /// List of supported application protocols.
1428    application_protos: Vec<Vec<u8>>,
1429
1430    /// Total number of received packets.
1431    recv_count: usize,
1432
1433    /// Total number of sent packets.
1434    sent_count: usize,
1435
1436    /// Total number of lost packets.
1437    lost_count: usize,
1438
1439    /// Total number of packets sent with data retransmitted.
1440    retrans_count: usize,
1441
1442    /// Total number of sent DATAGRAM frames.
1443    dgram_sent_count: usize,
1444
1445    /// Total number of received DATAGRAM frames.
1446    dgram_recv_count: usize,
1447
1448    /// Total number of bytes received from the peer.
1449    rx_data: u64,
1450
1451    /// Receiver flow controller.
1452    flow_control: flowcontrol::FlowControl,
1453
1454    /// Whether we send MAX_DATA frame.
1455    almost_full: bool,
1456
1457    /// Number of stream data bytes that can be buffered.
1458    tx_cap: usize,
1459
1460    // Number of bytes buffered in the send buffer.
1461    tx_buffered: usize,
1462
1463    /// Total number of bytes sent to the peer.
1464    tx_data: u64,
1465
1466    /// Peer's flow control limit for the connection.
1467    max_tx_data: u64,
1468
1469    /// Last tx_data before running a full send() loop.
1470    last_tx_data: u64,
1471
1472    /// Total number of bytes retransmitted over the connection.
1473    /// This counts only STREAM and CRYPTO data.
1474    stream_retrans_bytes: u64,
1475
1476    /// Total number of bytes sent over the connection.
1477    sent_bytes: u64,
1478
1479    /// Total number of bytes received over the connection.
1480    recv_bytes: u64,
1481
1482    /// Total number of bytes sent acked over the connection.
1483    acked_bytes: u64,
1484
1485    /// Total number of bytes sent lost over the connection.
1486    lost_bytes: u64,
1487
1488    /// Streams map, indexed by stream ID.
1489    streams: stream::StreamMap,
1490
1491    /// Peer's original destination connection ID. Used by the client to
1492    /// validate the server's transport parameter.
1493    odcid: Option<ConnectionId<'static>>,
1494
1495    /// Peer's retry source connection ID. Used by the client during stateless
1496    /// retry to validate the server's transport parameter.
1497    rscid: Option<ConnectionId<'static>>,
1498
1499    /// Received address verification token.
1500    token: Option<Vec<u8>>,
1501
1502    /// Error code and reason to be sent to the peer in a CONNECTION_CLOSE
1503    /// frame.
1504    local_error: Option<ConnectionError>,
1505
1506    /// Error code and reason received from the peer in a CONNECTION_CLOSE
1507    /// frame.
1508    peer_error: Option<ConnectionError>,
1509
1510    /// The connection-level limit at which send blocking occurred.
1511    blocked_limit: Option<u64>,
1512
1513    /// Idle timeout expiration time.
1514    idle_timer: Option<time::Instant>,
1515
1516    /// Draining timeout expiration time.
1517    draining_timer: Option<time::Instant>,
1518
1519    /// List of raw packets that were received before they could be decrypted.
1520    undecryptable_pkts: VecDeque<(Vec<u8>, RecvInfo)>,
1521
1522    /// The negotiated ALPN protocol.
1523    alpn: Vec<u8>,
1524
1525    /// Whether this is a server-side connection.
1526    is_server: bool,
1527
1528    /// Whether the initial secrets have been derived.
1529    derived_initial_secrets: bool,
1530
1531    /// Whether a version negotiation packet has already been received. Only
1532    /// relevant for client connections.
1533    did_version_negotiation: bool,
1534
1535    /// Whether stateless retry has been performed.
1536    did_retry: bool,
1537
1538    /// Whether the peer already updated its connection ID.
1539    got_peer_conn_id: bool,
1540
1541    /// Whether the peer verified our initial address.
1542    peer_verified_initial_address: bool,
1543
1544    /// Whether the peer's transport parameters were parsed.
1545    parsed_peer_transport_params: bool,
1546
1547    /// Whether the connection handshake has been completed.
1548    handshake_completed: bool,
1549
1550    /// Whether the HANDSHAKE_DONE frame has been sent.
1551    handshake_done_sent: bool,
1552
1553    /// Whether the HANDSHAKE_DONE frame has been acked.
1554    handshake_done_acked: bool,
1555
1556    /// Whether the connection handshake has been confirmed.
1557    handshake_confirmed: bool,
1558
1559    /// Key phase bit used for outgoing protected packets.
1560    key_phase: bool,
1561
1562    /// Whether an ack-eliciting packet has been sent since last receiving a
1563    /// packet.
1564    ack_eliciting_sent: bool,
1565
1566    /// Whether the connection is closed.
1567    closed: bool,
1568
1569    /// Whether the connection was timed out.
1570    timed_out: bool,
1571
1572    /// Whether to send GREASE.
1573    grease: bool,
1574
1575    /// TLS keylog writer.
1576    keylog: Option<Box<dyn std::io::Write + Send + Sync>>,
1577
1578    #[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
1579    qlog: QlogInfo,
1580
1581    /// DATAGRAM queues.
1582    dgram_recv_queue: dgram::DatagramQueue,
1583    dgram_send_queue: dgram::DatagramQueue,
1584
1585    /// Whether to emit DATAGRAM frames in the next packet.
1586    emit_dgram: bool,
1587
1588    /// Whether the connection should prevent from reusing destination
1589    /// Connection IDs when the peer migrates.
1590    disable_dcid_reuse: bool,
1591
1592    /// The number of streams reset by local.
1593    reset_stream_local_count: u64,
1594
1595    /// The number of streams stopped by local.
1596    stopped_stream_local_count: u64,
1597
1598    /// The number of streams reset by remote.
1599    reset_stream_remote_count: u64,
1600
1601    /// The number of streams stopped by remote.
1602    stopped_stream_remote_count: u64,
1603
1604    /// The anti-amplification limit factor.
1605    max_amplification_factor: usize,
1606}
1607
1608/// Creates a new server-side connection.
1609///
1610/// The `scid` parameter represents the server's source connection ID, while
1611/// the optional `odcid` parameter represents the original destination ID the
1612/// client sent before a stateless retry (this is only required when using
1613/// the [`retry()`] function).
1614///
1615/// [`retry()`]: fn.retry.html
1616///
1617/// ## Examples:
1618///
1619/// ```no_run
1620/// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(0xbabababa)?;
1621/// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
1622/// # let local = "127.0.0.1:0".parse().unwrap();
1623/// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
1624/// let conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
1625/// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
1626/// ```
1627#[inline]
1628pub fn accept(
1629    scid: &ConnectionId, odcid: Option<&ConnectionId>, local: SocketAddr,
1630    peer: SocketAddr, config: &mut Config,
1631) -> Result<Connection> {
1632    let conn = Connection::new(scid, odcid, local, peer, config, true)?;
1633
1634    Ok(conn)
1635}
1636
1637/// Creates a new client-side connection.
1638///
1639/// The `scid` parameter is used as the connection's source connection ID,
1640/// while the optional `server_name` parameter is used to verify the peer's
1641/// certificate.
1642///
1643/// ## Examples:
1644///
1645/// ```no_run
1646/// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(0xbabababa)?;
1647/// # let server_name = "quic.tech";
1648/// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
1649/// # let local = "127.0.0.1:4321".parse().unwrap();
1650/// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
1651/// let conn =
1652///     quiche::connect(Some(&server_name), &scid, local, peer, &mut config)?;
1653/// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
1654/// ```
1655#[inline]
1656pub fn connect(
1657    server_name: Option<&str>, scid: &ConnectionId, local: SocketAddr,
1658    peer: SocketAddr, config: &mut Config,
1659) -> Result<Connection> {
1660    let mut conn = Connection::new(scid, None, local, peer, config, false)?;
1661
1662    if let Some(server_name) = server_name {
1663        conn.handshake.set_host_name(server_name)?;
1664    }
1665
1666    Ok(conn)
1667}
1668
1669/// Writes a version negotiation packet.
1670///
1671/// The `scid` and `dcid` parameters are the source connection ID and the
1672/// destination connection ID extracted from the received client's Initial
1673/// packet that advertises an unsupported version.
1674///
1675/// ## Examples:
1676///
1677/// ```no_run
1678/// # let mut buf = [0; 512];
1679/// # let mut out = [0; 512];
1680/// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
1681/// let (len, src) = socket.recv_from(&mut buf).unwrap();
1682///
1683/// let hdr =
1684///     quiche::Header::from_slice(&mut buf[..len], quiche::MAX_CONN_ID_LEN)?;
1685///
1686/// if hdr.version != quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION {
1687///     let len = quiche::negotiate_version(&hdr.scid, &hdr.dcid, &mut out)?;
1688///     socket.send_to(&out[..len], &src).unwrap();
1689/// }
1690/// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
1691/// ```
1692#[inline]
1693pub fn negotiate_version(
1694    scid: &ConnectionId, dcid: &ConnectionId, out: &mut [u8],
1695) -> Result<usize> {
1696    packet::negotiate_version(scid, dcid, out)
1697}
1698
1699/// Writes a stateless retry packet.
1700///
1701/// The `scid` and `dcid` parameters are the source connection ID and the
1702/// destination connection ID extracted from the received client's Initial
1703/// packet, while `new_scid` is the server's new source connection ID and
1704/// `token` is the address validation token the client needs to echo back.
1705///
1706/// The application is responsible for generating the address validation
1707/// token to be sent to the client, and verifying tokens sent back by the
1708/// client. The generated token should include the `dcid` parameter, such
1709/// that it can be later extracted from the token and passed to the
1710/// [`accept()`] function as its `odcid` parameter.
1711///
1712/// [`accept()`]: fn.accept.html
1713///
1714/// ## Examples:
1715///
1716/// ```no_run
1717/// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(0xbabababa)?;
1718/// # let mut buf = [0; 512];
1719/// # let mut out = [0; 512];
1720/// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
1721/// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
1722/// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
1723/// # fn mint_token(hdr: &quiche::Header, src: &std::net::SocketAddr) -> Vec<u8> {
1724/// #     vec![]
1725/// # }
1726/// # fn validate_token<'a>(src: &std::net::SocketAddr, token: &'a [u8]) -> Option<quiche::ConnectionId<'a>> {
1727/// #     None
1728/// # }
1729/// let (len, peer) = socket.recv_from(&mut buf).unwrap();
1730///
1731/// let hdr = quiche::Header::from_slice(&mut buf[..len], quiche::MAX_CONN_ID_LEN)?;
1732///
1733/// let token = hdr.token.as_ref().unwrap();
1734///
1735/// // No token sent by client, create a new one.
1736/// if token.is_empty() {
1737///     let new_token = mint_token(&hdr, &peer);
1738///
1739///     let len = quiche::retry(
1740///         &hdr.scid, &hdr.dcid, &scid, &new_token, hdr.version, &mut out,
1741///     )?;
1742///
1743///     socket.send_to(&out[..len], &peer).unwrap();
1744///     return Ok(());
1745/// }
1746///
1747/// // Client sent token, validate it.
1748/// let odcid = validate_token(&peer, token);
1749///
1750/// if odcid.is_none() {
1751///     // Invalid address validation token.
1752///     return Ok(());
1753/// }
1754///
1755/// let conn = quiche::accept(&scid, odcid.as_ref(), local, peer, &mut config)?;
1756/// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
1757/// ```
1758#[inline]
1759pub fn retry(
1760    scid: &ConnectionId, dcid: &ConnectionId, new_scid: &ConnectionId,
1761    token: &[u8], version: u32, out: &mut [u8],
1762) -> Result<usize> {
1763    packet::retry(scid, dcid, new_scid, token, version, out)
1764}
1765
1766/// Returns true if the given protocol version is supported.
1767#[inline]
1768pub fn version_is_supported(version: u32) -> bool {
1769    matches!(version, PROTOCOL_VERSION_V1)
1770}
1771
1772/// Pushes a frame to the output packet if there is enough space.
1773///
1774/// Returns `true` on success, `false` otherwise. In case of failure it means
1775/// there is no room to add the frame in the packet. You may retry to add the
1776/// frame later.
1777macro_rules! push_frame_to_pkt {
1778    ($out:expr, $frames:expr, $frame:expr, $left:expr) => {{
1779        if $frame.wire_len() <= $left {
1780            $left -= $frame.wire_len();
1781
1782            $frame.to_bytes(&mut $out)?;
1783
1784            $frames.push($frame);
1785
1786            true
1787        } else {
1788            false
1789        }
1790    }};
1791}
1792
1793/// Executes the provided body if the qlog feature is enabled, quiche has been
1794/// configured with a log writer, the event's importance is within the
1795/// configured level.
1796macro_rules! qlog_with_type {
1797    ($ty:expr, $qlog:expr, $qlog_streamer_ref:ident, $body:block) => {{
1798        #[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
1799        {
1800            if EventImportance::from($ty).is_contained_in(&$qlog.level) {
1801                if let Some($qlog_streamer_ref) = &mut $qlog.streamer {
1802                    $body
1803                }
1804            }
1805        }
1806    }};
1807}
1808
1809#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
1810const QLOG_PARAMS_SET: EventType =
1811    EventType::TransportEventType(TransportEventType::ParametersSet);
1812
1813#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
1814const QLOG_PACKET_RX: EventType =
1815    EventType::TransportEventType(TransportEventType::PacketReceived);
1816
1817#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
1818const QLOG_PACKET_TX: EventType =
1819    EventType::TransportEventType(TransportEventType::PacketSent);
1820
1821#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
1822const QLOG_DATA_MV: EventType =
1823    EventType::TransportEventType(TransportEventType::DataMoved);
1824
1825#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
1826const QLOG_METRICS: EventType =
1827    EventType::RecoveryEventType(RecoveryEventType::MetricsUpdated);
1828
1829#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
1830const QLOG_CONNECTION_CLOSED: EventType =
1831    EventType::ConnectivityEventType(ConnectivityEventType::ConnectionClosed);
1832
1833#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
1834struct QlogInfo {
1835    streamer: Option<qlog::streamer::QlogStreamer>,
1836    logged_peer_params: bool,
1837    level: EventImportance,
1838}
1839
1840#[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
1841impl Default for QlogInfo {
1842    fn default() -> Self {
1843        QlogInfo {
1844            streamer: None,
1845            logged_peer_params: false,
1846            level: EventImportance::Base,
1847        }
1848    }
1849}
1850
1851impl Connection {
1852    fn new(
1853        scid: &ConnectionId, odcid: Option<&ConnectionId>, local: SocketAddr,
1854        peer: SocketAddr, config: &mut Config, is_server: bool,
1855    ) -> Result<Connection> {
1856        let tls = config.tls_ctx.new_handshake()?;
1857        Connection::with_tls(scid, odcid, local, peer, config, tls, is_server)
1858    }
1859
1860    fn with_tls(
1861        scid: &ConnectionId, odcid: Option<&ConnectionId>, local: SocketAddr,
1862        peer: SocketAddr, config: &Config, tls: tls::Handshake, is_server: bool,
1863    ) -> Result<Connection> {
1864        let max_rx_data = config.local_transport_params.initial_max_data;
1865
1866        let scid_as_hex: Vec<String> =
1867            scid.iter().map(|b| format!("{b:02x}")).collect();
1868
1869        let reset_token = if is_server {
1870            config.local_transport_params.stateless_reset_token
1871        } else {
1872            None
1873        };
1874
1875        let recovery_config = recovery::RecoveryConfig::from_config(config);
1876
1877        let mut path = path::Path::new(
1878            local,
1879            peer,
1880            &recovery_config,
1881            config.path_challenge_recv_max_queue_len,
1882            MIN_CLIENT_INITIAL_LEN,
1883            true,
1884        );
1885
1886        // If we did stateless retry assume the peer's address is verified.
1887        path.verified_peer_address = odcid.is_some();
1888        // Assume clients validate the server's address implicitly.
1889        path.peer_verified_local_address = is_server;
1890
1891        // Do not allocate more than the number of active CIDs.
1892        let paths = path::PathMap::new(
1893            path,
1894            config.local_transport_params.active_conn_id_limit as usize,
1895            is_server,
1896            config.pmtud,
1897            config.max_send_udp_payload_size,
1898        );
1899
1900        let active_path_id = paths.get_active_path_id()?;
1901
1902        let ids = cid::ConnectionIdentifiers::new(
1903            config.local_transport_params.active_conn_id_limit as usize,
1904            scid,
1905            active_path_id,
1906            reset_token,
1907        );
1908
1909        let mut conn = Connection {
1910            version: config.version,
1911
1912            ids,
1913
1914            trace_id: scid_as_hex.join(""),
1915
1916            pkt_num_spaces: [
1917                packet::PktNumSpace::new(),
1918                packet::PktNumSpace::new(),
1919                packet::PktNumSpace::new(),
1920            ],
1921
1922            next_pkt_num: 0,
1923
1924            peer_transport_params: TransportParams::default(),
1925
1926            peer_transport_params_track_unknown: config
1927                .track_unknown_transport_params,
1928
1929            local_transport_params: config.local_transport_params.clone(),
1930
1931            handshake: tls,
1932
1933            session: None,
1934
1935            recovery_config,
1936
1937            paths,
1938            path_challenge_recv_max_queue_len: config
1939                .path_challenge_recv_max_queue_len,
1940            path_challenge_rx_count: 0,
1941
1942            application_protos: config.application_protos.clone(),
1943
1944            recv_count: 0,
1945            sent_count: 0,
1946            lost_count: 0,
1947            retrans_count: 0,
1948            dgram_sent_count: 0,
1949            dgram_recv_count: 0,
1950            sent_bytes: 0,
1951            recv_bytes: 0,
1952            acked_bytes: 0,
1953            lost_bytes: 0,
1954
1955            rx_data: 0,
1956            flow_control: flowcontrol::FlowControl::new(
1957                max_rx_data,
1958                cmp::min(max_rx_data / 2 * 3, DEFAULT_CONNECTION_WINDOW),
1959                config.max_connection_window,
1960            ),
1961            almost_full: false,
1962
1963            tx_cap: 0,
1964
1965            tx_buffered: 0,
1966
1967            tx_data: 0,
1968            max_tx_data: 0,
1969            last_tx_data: 0,
1970
1971            stream_retrans_bytes: 0,
1972
1973            streams: stream::StreamMap::new(
1974                config.local_transport_params.initial_max_streams_bidi,
1975                config.local_transport_params.initial_max_streams_uni,
1976                config.max_stream_window,
1977            ),
1978
1979            odcid: None,
1980
1981            rscid: None,
1982
1983            token: None,
1984
1985            local_error: None,
1986
1987            peer_error: None,
1988
1989            blocked_limit: None,
1990
1991            idle_timer: None,
1992
1993            draining_timer: None,
1994
1995            undecryptable_pkts: VecDeque::new(),
1996
1997            alpn: Vec::new(),
1998
1999            is_server,
2000
2001            derived_initial_secrets: false,
2002
2003            did_version_negotiation: false,
2004
2005            did_retry: false,
2006
2007            got_peer_conn_id: false,
2008
2009            // Assume clients validate the server's address implicitly.
2010            peer_verified_initial_address: is_server,
2011
2012            parsed_peer_transport_params: false,
2013
2014            handshake_completed: false,
2015
2016            handshake_done_sent: false,
2017            handshake_done_acked: false,
2018
2019            handshake_confirmed: false,
2020
2021            key_phase: false,
2022
2023            ack_eliciting_sent: false,
2024
2025            closed: false,
2026
2027            timed_out: false,
2028
2029            grease: config.grease,
2030
2031            keylog: None,
2032
2033            #[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
2034            qlog: Default::default(),
2035
2036            dgram_recv_queue: dgram::DatagramQueue::new(
2037                config.dgram_recv_max_queue_len,
2038            ),
2039
2040            dgram_send_queue: dgram::DatagramQueue::new(
2041                config.dgram_send_max_queue_len,
2042            ),
2043
2044            emit_dgram: true,
2045
2046            disable_dcid_reuse: config.disable_dcid_reuse,
2047
2048            reset_stream_local_count: 0,
2049            stopped_stream_local_count: 0,
2050            reset_stream_remote_count: 0,
2051            stopped_stream_remote_count: 0,
2052
2053            max_amplification_factor: config.max_amplification_factor,
2054        };
2055
2056        if let Some(odcid) = odcid {
2057            conn.local_transport_params
2058                .original_destination_connection_id = Some(odcid.to_vec().into());
2059
2060            conn.local_transport_params.retry_source_connection_id =
2061                Some(conn.ids.get_scid(0)?.cid.to_vec().into());
2062
2063            conn.did_retry = true;
2064        }
2065
2066        conn.local_transport_params.initial_source_connection_id =
2067            Some(conn.ids.get_scid(0)?.cid.to_vec().into());
2068
2069        conn.handshake.init(is_server)?;
2070
2071        conn.handshake
2072            .use_legacy_codepoint(config.version != PROTOCOL_VERSION_V1);
2073
2074        conn.encode_transport_params()?;
2075
2076        // Derive initial secrets for the client. We can do this here because
2077        // we already generated the random destination connection ID.
2078        if !is_server {
2079            let mut dcid = [0; 16];
2080            rand::rand_bytes(&mut dcid[..]);
2081
2082            let (aead_open, aead_seal) = crypto::derive_initial_key_material(
2083                &dcid,
2084                conn.version,
2085                conn.is_server,
2086                false,
2087            )?;
2088
2089            let reset_token = conn.peer_transport_params.stateless_reset_token;
2090            conn.set_initial_dcid(
2091                dcid.to_vec().into(),
2092                reset_token,
2093                active_path_id,
2094            )?;
2095
2096            conn.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Initial].crypto_open =
2097                Some(aead_open);
2098            conn.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Initial].crypto_seal =
2099                Some(aead_seal);
2100
2101            conn.derived_initial_secrets = true;
2102        }
2103
2104        Ok(conn)
2105    }
2106
2107    /// Sets keylog output to the designated [`Writer`].
2108    ///
2109    /// This needs to be called as soon as the connection is created, to avoid
2110    /// missing some early logs.
2111    ///
2112    /// [`Writer`]: https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/io/trait.Write.html
2113    #[inline]
2114    pub fn set_keylog(&mut self, writer: Box<dyn std::io::Write + Send + Sync>) {
2115        self.keylog = Some(writer);
2116    }
2117
2118    /// Sets qlog output to the designated [`Writer`].
2119    ///
2120    /// Only events included in `QlogLevel::Base` are written. The serialization
2121    /// format is JSON-SEQ.
2122    ///
2123    /// This needs to be called as soon as the connection is created, to avoid
2124    /// missing some early logs.
2125    ///
2126    /// [`Writer`]: https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/io/trait.Write.html
2127    #[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
2128    #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "qlog")))]
2129    pub fn set_qlog(
2130        &mut self, writer: Box<dyn std::io::Write + Send + Sync>, title: String,
2131        description: String,
2132    ) {
2133        self.set_qlog_with_level(writer, title, description, QlogLevel::Base)
2134    }
2135
2136    /// Sets qlog output to the designated [`Writer`].
2137    ///
2138    /// Only qlog events included in the specified `QlogLevel` are written. The
2139    /// serialization format is JSON-SEQ.
2140    ///
2141    /// This needs to be called as soon as the connection is created, to avoid
2142    /// missing some early logs.
2143    ///
2144    /// [`Writer`]: https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/io/trait.Write.html
2145    #[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
2146    #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "qlog")))]
2147    pub fn set_qlog_with_level(
2148        &mut self, writer: Box<dyn std::io::Write + Send + Sync>, title: String,
2149        description: String, qlog_level: QlogLevel,
2150    ) {
2151        let vp = if self.is_server {
2152            qlog::VantagePointType::Server
2153        } else {
2154            qlog::VantagePointType::Client
2155        };
2156
2157        let level = match qlog_level {
2158            QlogLevel::Core => EventImportance::Core,
2159
2160            QlogLevel::Base => EventImportance::Base,
2161
2162            QlogLevel::Extra => EventImportance::Extra,
2163        };
2164
2165        self.qlog.level = level;
2166
2167        let trace = qlog::TraceSeq::new(
2168            qlog::VantagePoint {
2169                name: None,
2170                ty: vp,
2171                flow: None,
2172            },
2173            Some(title.to_string()),
2174            Some(description.to_string()),
