2175            Some(qlog::Configuration {
2176                time_offset: Some(0.0),
2177                original_uris: None,
2178            }),
2179            None,
2180        );
2181
2182        let mut streamer = qlog::streamer::QlogStreamer::new(
2183            qlog::QLOG_VERSION.to_string(),
2184            Some(title),
2185            Some(description),
2186            None,
2187            time::Instant::now(),
2188            trace,
2189            self.qlog.level,
2190            writer,
2191        );
2192
2193        streamer.start_log().ok();
2194
2195        let ev_data = self
2196            .local_transport_params
2197            .to_qlog(TransportOwner::Local, self.handshake.cipher());
2198
2199        // This event occurs very early, so just mark the relative time as 0.0.
2200        streamer.add_event(Event::with_time(0.0, ev_data)).ok();
2201
2202        self.qlog.streamer = Some(streamer);
2203    }
2204
2205    /// Returns a mutable reference to the QlogStreamer, if it exists.
2206    #[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
2207    #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "qlog")))]
2208    pub fn qlog_streamer(&mut self) -> Option<&mut qlog::streamer::QlogStreamer> {
2209        self.qlog.streamer.as_mut()
2210    }
2211
2212    /// Configures the given session for resumption.
2213    ///
2214    /// On the client, this can be used to offer the given serialized session,
2215    /// as returned by [`session()`], for resumption.
2216    ///
2217    /// This must only be called immediately after creating a connection, that
2218    /// is, before any packet is sent or received.
2219    ///
2220    /// [`session()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.session
2221    #[inline]
2222    pub fn set_session(&mut self, session: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
2223        let mut b = octets::Octets::with_slice(session);
2224
2225        let session_len = b.get_u64()? as usize;
2226        let session_bytes = b.get_bytes(session_len)?;
2227
2228        self.handshake.set_session(session_bytes.as_ref())?;
2229
2230        let raw_params_len = b.get_u64()? as usize;
2231        let raw_params_bytes = b.get_bytes(raw_params_len)?;
2232
2233        let peer_params = TransportParams::decode(
2234            raw_params_bytes.as_ref(),
2235            self.is_server,
2236            self.peer_transport_params_track_unknown,
2237        )?;
2238
2239        self.process_peer_transport_params(peer_params)?;
2240
2241        Ok(())
2242    }
2243
2244    /// Sets the `max_idle_timeout` transport parameter, in milliseconds.
2245    ///
2246    /// This must only be called immediately after creating a connection, that
2247    /// is, before any packet is sent or received.
2248    ///
2249    /// The default value is infinite, that is, no timeout is used unless
2250    /// already configured when creating the connection.
2251    pub fn set_max_idle_timeout(&mut self, v: u64) -> Result<()> {
2252        self.local_transport_params.max_idle_timeout = v;
2253
2254        self.encode_transport_params()
2255    }
2256
2257    /// Sets the congestion control algorithm used.
2258    ///
2259    /// This function can only be called inside one of BoringSSL's handshake
2260    /// callbacks, before any packet has been sent. Calling this function any
2261    /// other time will have no effect.
2262    ///
2263    /// See [`Config::set_cc_algorithm()`].
2264    ///
2265    /// [`Config::set_cc_algorithm()`]: struct.Config.html#method.set_cc_algorithm
2266    #[cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")]
2267    #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")))]
2268    pub fn set_cc_algorithm_in_handshake(
2269        ssl: &mut boring::ssl::SslRef, algo: CongestionControlAlgorithm,
2270    ) -> Result<()> {
2271        let ex_data = tls::ExData::from_ssl_ref(ssl).ok_or(Error::TlsFail)?;
2272
2273        ex_data.recovery_config.cc_algorithm = algo;
2274
2275        Ok(())
2276    }
2277
2278    /// Sets the congestion control algorithm used by string.
2279    ///
2280    /// This function can only be called inside one of BoringSSL's handshake
2281    /// callbacks, before any packet has been sent. Calling this function any
2282    /// other time will have no effect.
2283    ///
2284    /// See [`Config::set_cc_algorithm_name()`].
2285    ///
2286    /// [`Config::set_cc_algorithm_name()`]: struct.Config.html#method.set_cc_algorithm_name
2287    #[cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")]
2288    #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")))]
2289    pub fn set_cc_algorithm_name_in_handshake(
2290        ssl: &mut boring::ssl::SslRef, name: &str,
2291    ) -> Result<()> {
2292        let cc_algo = CongestionControlAlgorithm::from_str(name)?;
2293        Self::set_cc_algorithm_in_handshake(ssl, cc_algo)
2294    }
2295
2296    /// Sets initial congestion window size in terms of packet count.
2297    ///
2298    /// This function can only be called inside one of BoringSSL's handshake
2299    /// callbacks, before any packet has been sent. Calling this function any
2300    /// other time will have no effect.
2301    ///
2302    /// See [`Config::set_initial_congestion_window_packets()`].
2303    ///
2304    /// [`Config::set_initial_congestion_window_packets()`]: struct.Config.html#method.set_initial_congestion_window_packets
2305    #[cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")]
2306    #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")))]
2307    pub fn set_initial_congestion_window_packets_in_handshake(
2308        ssl: &mut boring::ssl::SslRef, packets: usize,
2309    ) -> Result<()> {
2310        let ex_data = tls::ExData::from_ssl_ref(ssl).ok_or(Error::TlsFail)?;
2311
2312        ex_data.recovery_config.initial_congestion_window_packets = packets;
2313
2314        Ok(())
2315    }
2316
2317    /// Configures whether to enable HyStart++.
2318    ///
2319    /// This function can only be called inside one of BoringSSL's handshake
2320    /// callbacks, before any packet has been sent. Calling this function any
2321    /// other time will have no effect.
2322    ///
2323    /// See [`Config::enable_hystart()`].
2324    ///
2325    /// [`Config::enable_hystart()`]: struct.Config.html#method.enable_hystart
2326    #[cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")]
2327    #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")))]
2328    pub fn set_hystart_in_handshake(
2329        ssl: &mut boring::ssl::SslRef, v: bool,
2330    ) -> Result<()> {
2331        let ex_data = tls::ExData::from_ssl_ref(ssl).ok_or(Error::TlsFail)?;
2332
2333        ex_data.recovery_config.hystart = v;
2334
2335        Ok(())
2336    }
2337
2338    /// Configures whether to enable pacing.
2339    ///
2340    /// This function can only be called inside one of BoringSSL's handshake
2341    /// callbacks, before any packet has been sent. Calling this function any
2342    /// other time will have no effect.
2343    ///
2344    /// See [`Config::enable_pacing()`].
2345    ///
2346    /// [`Config::enable_pacing()`]: struct.Config.html#method.enable_pacing
2347    #[cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")]
2348    #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")))]
2349    pub fn set_pacing_in_handshake(
2350        ssl: &mut boring::ssl::SslRef, v: bool,
2351    ) -> Result<()> {
2352        let ex_data = tls::ExData::from_ssl_ref(ssl).ok_or(Error::TlsFail)?;
2353
2354        ex_data.recovery_config.pacing = v;
2355
2356        Ok(())
2357    }
2358
2359    /// Sets the max value for pacing rate.
2360    ///
2361    /// This function can only be called inside one of BoringSSL's handshake
2362    /// callbacks, before any packet has been sent. Calling this function any
2363    /// other time will have no effect.
2364    ///
2365    /// See [`Config::set_max_pacing_rate()`].
2366    ///
2367    /// [`Config::set_max_pacing_rate()`]: struct.Config.html#method.set_max_pacing_rate
2368    #[cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")]
2369    #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")))]
2370    pub fn set_max_pacing_rate_in_handshake(
2371        ssl: &mut boring::ssl::SslRef, v: Option<u64>,
2372    ) -> Result<()> {
2373        let ex_data = tls::ExData::from_ssl_ref(ssl).ok_or(Error::TlsFail)?;
2374
2375        ex_data.recovery_config.max_pacing_rate = v;
2376
2377        Ok(())
2378    }
2379
2380    /// Sets the maximum outgoing UDP payload size.
2381    ///
2382    /// This function can only be called inside one of BoringSSL's handshake
2383    /// callbacks, before any packet has been sent. Calling this function any
2384    /// other time will have no effect.
2385    ///
2386    /// See [`Config::set_max_send_udp_payload_size()`].
2387    ///
2388    /// [`Config::set_max_send_udp_payload_size()`]: struct.Config.html#method.set_max_send_udp_payload_size
2389    #[cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")]
2390    #[cfg_attr(docsrs, doc(cfg(feature = "boringssl-boring-crate")))]
2391    pub fn set_max_send_udp_payload_size_in_handshake(
2392        ssl: &mut boring::ssl::SslRef, v: usize,
2393    ) -> Result<()> {
2394        let ex_data = tls::ExData::from_ssl_ref(ssl).ok_or(Error::TlsFail)?;
2395
2396        ex_data.recovery_config.max_send_udp_payload_size = v;
2397
2398        Ok(())
2399    }
2400
2401    /// Processes QUIC packets received from the peer.
2402    ///
2403    /// On success the number of bytes processed from the input buffer is
2404    /// returned. On error the connection will be closed by calling [`close()`]
2405    /// with the appropriate error code.
2406    ///
2407    /// Coalesced packets will be processed as necessary.
2408    ///
2409    /// Note that the contents of the input buffer `buf` might be modified by
2410    /// this function due to, for example, in-place decryption.
2411    ///
2412    /// [`close()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.close
2413    ///
2414    /// ## Examples:
2415    ///
2416    /// ```no_run
2417    /// # let mut buf = [0; 512];
2418    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
2419    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
2420    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
2421    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
2422    /// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
2423    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
2424    /// loop {
2425    ///     let (read, from) = socket.recv_from(&mut buf).unwrap();
2426    ///
2427    ///     let recv_info = quiche::RecvInfo {
2428    ///         from,
2429    ///         to: local,
2430    ///     };
2431    ///
2432    ///     let read = match conn.recv(&mut buf[..read], recv_info) {
2433    ///         Ok(v) => v,
2434    ///
2435    ///         Err(e) => {
2436    ///             // An error occurred, handle it.
2437    ///             break;
2438    ///         },
2439    ///     };
2440    /// }
2441    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
2442    /// ```
2443    pub fn recv(&mut self, buf: &mut [u8], info: RecvInfo) -> Result<usize> {
2444        let len = buf.len();
2445
2446        if len == 0 {
2447            return Err(Error::BufferTooShort);
2448        }
2449
2450        let recv_pid = self.paths.path_id_from_addrs(&(info.to, info.from));
2451
2452        if let Some(recv_pid) = recv_pid {
2453            let recv_path = self.paths.get_mut(recv_pid)?;
2454
2455            // Keep track of how many bytes we received from the client, so we
2456            // can limit bytes sent back before address validation, to a
2457            // multiple of this. The limit needs to be increased early on, so
2458            // that if there is an error there is enough credit to send a
2459            // CONNECTION_CLOSE.
2460            //
2461            // It doesn't matter if the packets received were valid or not, we
2462            // only need to track the total amount of bytes received.
2463            //
2464            // Note that we also need to limit the number of bytes we sent on a
2465            // path if we are not the host that initiated its usage.
2466            if self.is_server && !recv_path.verified_peer_address {
2467                recv_path.max_send_bytes += len * self.max_amplification_factor;
2468            }
2469        } else if !self.is_server {
2470            // If a client receives packets from an unknown server address,
2471            // the client MUST discard these packets.
2472            trace!(
2473                "{} client received packet from unknown address {:?}, dropping",
2474                self.trace_id,
2475                info,
2476            );
2477
2478            return Ok(len);
2479        }
2480
2481        let mut done = 0;
2482        let mut left = len;
2483
2484        // Process coalesced packets.
2485        while left > 0 {
2486            let read = match self.recv_single(
2487                &mut buf[len - left..len],
2488                &info,
2489                recv_pid,
2490            ) {
2491                Ok(v) => v,
2492
2493                Err(Error::Done) => {
2494                    // If the packet can't be processed or decrypted, check if
2495                    // it's a stateless reset.
2496                    if self.is_stateless_reset(&buf[len - left..len]) {
2497                        trace!("{} packet is a stateless reset", self.trace_id);
2498
2499                        self.mark_closed();
2500                    }
2501
2502                    left
2503                },
2504
2505                Err(e) => {
2506                    // In case of error processing the incoming packet, close
2507                    // the connection.
2508                    self.close(false, e.to_wire(), b"").ok();
2509                    return Err(e);
2510                },
2511            };
2512
2513            done += read;
2514            left -= read;
2515        }
2516
2517        // Even though the packet was previously "accepted", it
2518        // should be safe to forward the error, as it also comes
2519        // from the `recv()` method.
2520        self.process_undecrypted_0rtt_packets()?;
2521
2522        Ok(done)
2523    }
2524
2525    fn process_undecrypted_0rtt_packets(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
2526        // Process previously undecryptable 0-RTT packets if the decryption key
2527        // is now available.
2528        if self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Application]
2529            .crypto_0rtt_open
2530            .is_some()
2531        {
2532            while let Some((mut pkt, info)) = self.undecryptable_pkts.pop_front()
2533            {
2534                if let Err(e) = self.recv(&mut pkt, info) {
2535                    self.undecryptable_pkts.clear();
2536
2537                    return Err(e);
2538                }
2539            }
2540        }
2541        Ok(())
2542    }
2543
2544    /// Returns true if a QUIC packet is a stateless reset.
2545    fn is_stateless_reset(&self, buf: &[u8]) -> bool {
2546        // If the packet is too small, then we just throw it away.
2547        let buf_len = buf.len();
2548        if buf_len < 21 {
2549            return false;
2550        }
2551
2552        // TODO: we should iterate over all active destination connection IDs
2553        // and check against their reset token.
2554        match self.peer_transport_params.stateless_reset_token {
2555            Some(token) => {
2556                let token_len = 16;
2557
2558                crypto::verify_slices_are_equal(
2559                    &token.to_be_bytes(),
2560                    &buf[buf_len - token_len..buf_len],
2561                )
2562                .is_ok()
2563            },
2564
2565            None => false,
2566        }
2567    }
2568
2569    /// Processes a single QUIC packet received from the peer.
2570    ///
2571    /// On success the number of bytes processed from the input buffer is
2572    /// returned. When the [`Done`] error is returned, processing of the
2573    /// remainder of the incoming UDP datagram should be interrupted.
2574    ///
2575    /// Note that a server might observe a new 4-tuple, preventing to
2576    /// know in advance to which path the incoming packet belongs to (`recv_pid`
2577    /// is `None`). As a client, packets from unknown 4-tuple are dropped
2578    /// beforehand (see `recv()`).
2579    ///
2580    /// On error, an error other than [`Done`] is returned.
2581    ///
2582    /// [`Done`]: enum.Error.html#variant.Done
2583    fn recv_single(
2584        &mut self, buf: &mut [u8], info: &RecvInfo, recv_pid: Option<usize>,
2585    ) -> Result<usize> {
2586        let now = time::Instant::now();
2587
2588        if buf.is_empty() {
2589            return Err(Error::Done);
2590        }
2591
2592        if self.is_closed() || self.is_draining() {
2593            return Err(Error::Done);
2594        }
2595
2596        let is_closing = self.local_error.is_some();
2597
2598        if is_closing {
2599            return Err(Error::Done);
2600        }
2601
2602        let buf_len = buf.len();
2603
2604        let mut b = octets::OctetsMut::with_slice(buf);
2605
2606        let mut hdr = Header::from_bytes(&mut b, self.source_id().len())
2607            .map_err(|e| {
2608                drop_pkt_on_err(
2609                    e,
2610                    self.recv_count,
2611                    self.is_server,
2612                    &self.trace_id,
2613                )
2614            })?;
2615
2616        if hdr.ty == packet::Type::VersionNegotiation {
2617            // Version negotiation packets can only be sent by the server.
2618            if self.is_server {
2619                return Err(Error::Done);
2620            }
2621
2622            // Ignore duplicate version negotiation.
2623            if self.did_version_negotiation {
2624                return Err(Error::Done);
2625            }
2626
2627            // Ignore version negotiation if any other packet has already been
2628            // successfully processed.
2629            if self.recv_count > 0 {
2630                return Err(Error::Done);
2631            }
2632
2633            if hdr.dcid != self.source_id() {
2634                return Err(Error::Done);
2635            }
2636
2637            if hdr.scid != self.destination_id() {
2638                return Err(Error::Done);
2639            }
2640
2641            trace!("{} rx pkt {:?}", self.trace_id, hdr);
2642
2643            let versions = hdr.versions.ok_or(Error::Done)?;
2644
2645            // Ignore version negotiation if the version already selected is
2646            // listed.
2647            if versions.contains(&self.version) {
2648                return Err(Error::Done);
2649            }
2650
2651            let supported_versions =
2652                versions.iter().filter(|&&v| version_is_supported(v));
2653
2654            let mut found_version = false;
2655
2656            for &v in supported_versions {
2657                found_version = true;
2658
2659                // The final version takes precedence over draft ones.
2660                if v == PROTOCOL_VERSION_V1 {
2661                    self.version = v;
2662                    break;
2663                }
2664
2665                self.version = cmp::max(self.version, v);
2666            }
2667
2668            if !found_version {
2669                // We don't support any of the versions offered.
2670                //
2671                // While a man-in-the-middle attacker might be able to
2672                // inject a version negotiation packet that triggers this
2673                // failure, the window of opportunity is very small and
2674                // this error is quite useful for debugging, so don't just
2675                // ignore the packet.
2676                return Err(Error::UnknownVersion);
2677            }
2678
2679            self.did_version_negotiation = true;
2680
2681            // Derive Initial secrets based on the new version.
2682            let (aead_open, aead_seal) = crypto::derive_initial_key_material(
2683                &self.destination_id(),
2684                self.version,
2685                self.is_server,
2686                true,
2687            )?;
2688
2689            // Reset connection state to force sending another Initial packet.
2690            self.drop_epoch_state(packet::Epoch::Initial, now);
2691            self.got_peer_conn_id = false;
2692            self.handshake.clear()?;
2693
2694            self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Initial].crypto_open =
2695                Some(aead_open);
2696            self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Initial].crypto_seal =
2697                Some(aead_seal);
2698
2699            self.handshake
2700                .use_legacy_codepoint(self.version != PROTOCOL_VERSION_V1);
2701
2702            // Encode transport parameters again, as the new version might be
2703            // using a different format.
2704            self.encode_transport_params()?;
2705
2706            return Err(Error::Done);
2707        }
2708
2709        if hdr.ty == packet::Type::Retry {
2710            // Retry packets can only be sent by the server.
2711            if self.is_server {
2712                return Err(Error::Done);
2713            }
2714
2715            // Ignore duplicate retry.
2716            if self.did_retry {
2717                return Err(Error::Done);
2718            }
2719
2720            // Check if Retry packet is valid.
2721            if packet::verify_retry_integrity(
2722                &b,
2723                &self.destination_id(),
2724                self.version,
2725            )
2726            .is_err()
2727            {
2728                return Err(Error::Done);
2729            }
2730
2731            trace!("{} rx pkt {:?}", self.trace_id, hdr);
2732
2733            self.token = hdr.token;
2734            self.did_retry = true;
2735
2736            // Remember peer's new connection ID.
2737            self.odcid = Some(self.destination_id().into_owned());
2738
2739            self.set_initial_dcid(
2740                hdr.scid.clone(),
2741                None,
2742                self.paths.get_active_path_id()?,
2743            )?;
2744
2745            self.rscid = Some(self.destination_id().into_owned());
2746
2747            // Derive Initial secrets using the new connection ID.
2748            let (aead_open, aead_seal) = crypto::derive_initial_key_material(
2749                &hdr.scid,
2750                self.version,
2751                self.is_server,
2752                true,
2753            )?;
2754
2755            // Reset connection state to force sending another Initial packet.
2756            self.drop_epoch_state(packet::Epoch::Initial, now);
2757            self.got_peer_conn_id = false;
2758            self.handshake.clear()?;
2759
2760            self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Initial].crypto_open =
2761                Some(aead_open);
2762            self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Initial].crypto_seal =
2763                Some(aead_seal);
2764
2765            return Err(Error::Done);
2766        }
2767
2768        if self.is_server && !self.did_version_negotiation {
2769            if !version_is_supported(hdr.version) {
2770                return Err(Error::UnknownVersion);
2771            }
2772
2773            self.version = hdr.version;
2774            self.did_version_negotiation = true;
2775
2776            self.handshake
2777                .use_legacy_codepoint(self.version != PROTOCOL_VERSION_V1);
2778
2779            // Encode transport parameters again, as the new version might be
2780            // using a different format.
2781            self.encode_transport_params()?;
2782        }
2783
2784        if hdr.ty != packet::Type::Short && hdr.version != self.version {
2785            // At this point version negotiation was already performed, so
2786            // ignore packets that don't match the connection's version.
2787            return Err(Error::Done);
2788        }
2789
2790        // Long header packets have an explicit payload length, but short
2791        // packets don't so just use the remaining capacity in the buffer.
2792        let payload_len = if hdr.ty == packet::Type::Short {
2793            b.cap()
2794        } else {
2795            b.get_varint().map_err(|e| {
2796                drop_pkt_on_err(
2797                    e.into(),
2798                    self.recv_count,
2799                    self.is_server,
2800                    &self.trace_id,
2801                )
2802            })? as usize
2803        };
2804
2805        // Make sure the buffer is same or larger than an explicit
2806        // payload length.
2807        if payload_len > b.cap() {
2808            return Err(drop_pkt_on_err(
2809                Error::InvalidPacket,
2810                self.recv_count,
2811                self.is_server,
2812                &self.trace_id,
2813            ));
2814        }
2815
2816        // Derive initial secrets on the server.
2817        if !self.derived_initial_secrets {
2818            let (aead_open, aead_seal) = crypto::derive_initial_key_material(
2819                &hdr.dcid,
2820                self.version,
2821                self.is_server,
2822                false,
2823            )?;
2824
2825            self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Initial].crypto_open =
2826                Some(aead_open);
2827            self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Initial].crypto_seal =
2828                Some(aead_seal);
2829
2830            self.derived_initial_secrets = true;
2831        }
2832
2833        // Select packet number space epoch based on the received packet's type.
2834        let epoch = hdr.ty.to_epoch()?;
2835
2836        // Select AEAD context used to open incoming packet.
2837        let aead = if hdr.ty == packet::Type::ZeroRTT {
2838            // Only use 0-RTT key if incoming packet is 0-RTT.
2839            self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].crypto_0rtt_open.as_ref()
2840        } else {
2841            // Otherwise use the packet number space's main key.
2842            self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].crypto_open.as_ref()
2843        };
2844
2845        // Finally, discard packet if no usable key is available.
2846        let mut aead = match aead {
2847            Some(v) => v,
2848
2849            None => {
2850                if hdr.ty == packet::Type::ZeroRTT &&
2851                    self.undecryptable_pkts.len() < MAX_UNDECRYPTABLE_PACKETS &&
2852                    !self.is_established()
2853                {
2854                    // Buffer 0-RTT packets when the required read key is not
2855                    // available yet, and process them later.
2856                    //
2857                    // TODO: in the future we might want to buffer other types
2858                    // of undecryptable packets as well.
2859                    let pkt_len = b.off() + payload_len;
2860                    let pkt = (b.buf()[..pkt_len]).to_vec();
2861
2862                    self.undecryptable_pkts.push_back((pkt, *info));
2863                    return Ok(pkt_len);
2864                }
2865
2866                let e = drop_pkt_on_err(
2867                    Error::CryptoFail,
2868                    self.recv_count,
2869                    self.is_server,
2870                    &self.trace_id,
2871                );
2872
2873                return Err(e);
2874            },
2875        };
2876
2877        let aead_tag_len = aead.alg().tag_len();
2878
2879        packet::decrypt_hdr(&mut b, &mut hdr, aead).map_err(|e| {
2880            drop_pkt_on_err(e, self.recv_count, self.is_server, &self.trace_id)
2881        })?;
2882
2883        let pn = packet::decode_pkt_num(
2884            self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].largest_rx_pkt_num,
2885            hdr.pkt_num,
2886            hdr.pkt_num_len,
2887        );
2888
2889        let pn_len = hdr.pkt_num_len;
2890
2891        trace!(
2892            "{} rx pkt {:?} len={} pn={} {}",
2893            self.trace_id,
2894            hdr,
2895            payload_len,
2896            pn,
2897            AddrTupleFmt(info.from, info.to)
2898        );
2899
2900        #[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
2901        let mut qlog_frames = vec![];
2902
2903        // Check for key update.
2904        let mut aead_next = None;
2905
2906        if self.handshake_confirmed &&
2907            hdr.ty != Type::ZeroRTT &&
2908            hdr.key_phase != self.key_phase
2909        {
2910            // Check if this packet arrived before key update.
2911            if let Some(key_update) = self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch]
2912                .key_update
2913                .as_ref()
2914                .and_then(|key_update| {
2915                    (pn < key_update.pn_on_update).then_some(key_update)
2916                })
2917            {
2918                aead = &key_update.crypto_open;
2919            } else {
2920                trace!("{} peer-initiated key update", self.trace_id);
2921
2922                aead_next = Some((
2923                    self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch]
2924                        .crypto_open
2925                        .as_ref()
2926                        .unwrap()
2927                        .derive_next_packet_key()?,
2928                    self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch]
2929                        .crypto_seal
2930                        .as_ref()
2931                        .unwrap()
2932                        .derive_next_packet_key()?,
2933                ));
2934
2935                // `aead_next` is always `Some()` at this point, so the `unwrap()`
2936                // will never fail.
2937                aead = &aead_next.as_ref().unwrap().0;
2938            }
2939        }
2940
2941        let mut payload = packet::decrypt_pkt(
2942            &mut b,
2943            pn,
2944            pn_len,
2945            payload_len,
2946            aead,
2947        )
2948        .map_err(|e| {
2949            drop_pkt_on_err(e, self.recv_count, self.is_server, &self.trace_id)
2950        })?;
2951
2952        if self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].recv_pkt_num.contains(pn) {
2953            trace!("{} ignored duplicate packet {}", self.trace_id, pn);
2954            return Err(Error::Done);
2955        }
2956
2957        // Packets with no frames are invalid.
2958        if payload.cap() == 0 {
2959            return Err(Error::InvalidPacket);
2960        }
2961
2962        // Now that we decrypted the packet, let's see if we can map it to an
2963        // existing path.
2964        let recv_pid = if hdr.ty == packet::Type::Short && self.got_peer_conn_id {
2965            let pkt_dcid = ConnectionId::from_ref(&hdr.dcid);
2966            self.get_or_create_recv_path_id(recv_pid, &pkt_dcid, buf_len, info)?
2967        } else {
2968            // During handshake, we are on the initial path.
2969            self.paths.get_active_path_id()?
2970        };
2971
2972        // The key update is verified once a packet is successfully decrypted
2973        // using the new keys.
2974        if let Some((open_next, seal_next)) = aead_next {
2975            if !self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch]
2976                .key_update
2977                .as_ref()
2978                .map_or(true, |prev| prev.update_acked)
2979            {
2980                // Peer has updated keys twice without awaiting confirmation.
2981                return Err(Error::KeyUpdate);
2982            }
2983
2984            trace!("{} key update verified", self.trace_id);
2985
2986            let _ = self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].crypto_seal.replace(seal_next);
2987
2988            let open_prev = self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch]
2989                .crypto_open
2990                .replace(open_next)
2991                .unwrap();
2992
2993            let recv_path = self.paths.get_mut(recv_pid)?;
2994
2995            self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].key_update = Some(packet::KeyUpdate {
2996                crypto_open: open_prev,
2997                pn_on_update: pn,
2998                update_acked: false,
2999                timer: now + (recv_path.recovery.pto() * 3),
3000            });
3001
3002            self.key_phase = !self.key_phase;
3003
3004            qlog_with_type!(QLOG_PACKET_RX, self.qlog, q, {
3005                let trigger = Some(
3006                    qlog::events::security::KeyUpdateOrRetiredTrigger::RemoteUpdate,
3007                );
3008
3009                let ev_data_client =
3010                    EventData::KeyUpdated(qlog::events::security::KeyUpdated {
3011                        key_type:
3012                            qlog::events::security::KeyType::Client1RttSecret,
3013                        trigger: trigger.clone(),
3014                        ..Default::default()
3015                    });
3016
3017                q.add_event_data_with_instant(ev_data_client, now).ok();
3018
3019                let ev_data_server =
3020                    EventData::KeyUpdated(qlog::events::security::KeyUpdated {
3021                        key_type:
3022                            qlog::events::security::KeyType::Server1RttSecret,
3023                        trigger,
3024                        ..Default::default()
3025                    });
3026
3027                q.add_event_data_with_instant(ev_data_server, now).ok();
3028            });
3029        }
3030
3031        if !self.is_server && !self.got_peer_conn_id {
3032            if self.odcid.is_none() {
3033                self.odcid = Some(self.destination_id().into_owned());
3034            }
3035
3036            // Replace the randomly generated destination connection ID with
3037            // the one supplied by the server.
3038            self.set_initial_dcid(
3039                hdr.scid.clone(),
3040                self.peer_transport_params.stateless_reset_token,
3041                recv_pid,
3042            )?;
3043
3044            self.got_peer_conn_id = true;
3045        }
3046
3047        if self.is_server && !self.got_peer_conn_id {
3048            self.set_initial_dcid(hdr.scid.clone(), None, recv_pid)?;
3049
3050            if !self.did_retry {
3051                self.local_transport_params
3052                    .original_destination_connection_id =
3053                    Some(hdr.dcid.to_vec().into());
3054
3055                self.encode_transport_params()?;
3056            }
3057
3058            self.got_peer_conn_id = true;
3059        }
3060
3061        // To avoid sending an ACK in response to an ACK-only packet, we need
3062        // to keep track of whether this packet contains any frame other than
3063        // ACK and PADDING.
3064        let mut ack_elicited = false;
3065
3066        // Process packet payload. If a frame cannot be processed, store the
3067        // error and stop further packet processing.
3068        let mut frame_processing_err = None;
3069
3070        // To know if the peer migrated the connection, we need to keep track
3071        // whether this is a non-probing packet.
3072        let mut probing = true;
3073
3074        // Process packet payload.
3075        while payload.cap() > 0 {
3076            let frame = frame::Frame::from_bytes(&mut payload, hdr.ty)?;
3077
3078            qlog_with_type!(QLOG_PACKET_RX, self.qlog, _q, {
3079                qlog_frames.push(frame.to_qlog());
3080            });
3081
3082            if frame.ack_eliciting() {
3083                ack_elicited = true;
3084            }
3085
3086            if !frame.probing() {
3087                probing = false;
3088            }
3089
3090            if let Err(e) = self.process_frame(frame, &hdr, recv_pid, epoch, now)
3091            {
3092                frame_processing_err = Some(e);
3093                break;
3094            }
3095        }
3096
3097        qlog_with_type!(QLOG_PACKET_RX, self.qlog, q, {
3098            let packet_size = b.len();
3099
3100            let qlog_pkt_hdr = qlog::events::quic::PacketHeader::with_type(
3101                hdr.ty.to_qlog(),
3102                Some(pn),
3103                Some(hdr.version),
3104                Some(&hdr.scid),
3105                Some(&hdr.dcid),
3106            );
3107
3108            let qlog_raw_info = RawInfo {
3109                length: Some(packet_size as u64),
3110                payload_length: Some(payload_len as u64),
3111                data: None,
3112            };
3113
3114            let ev_data =
3115                EventData::PacketReceived(qlog::events::quic::PacketReceived {
3116                    header: qlog_pkt_hdr,
3117                    frames: Some(qlog_frames),
3118                    raw: Some(qlog_raw_info),
3119                    ..Default::default()
3120                });
3121
3122            q.add_event_data_with_instant(ev_data, now).ok();
3123        });
3124
3125        qlog_with_type!(QLOG_PACKET_RX, self.qlog, q, {
3126            let recv_path = self.paths.get_mut(recv_pid)?;
3127            if let Some(ev_data) = recv_path.recovery.maybe_qlog() {
3128                q.add_event_data_with_instant(ev_data, now).ok();
3129            }
3130        });
3131
3132        if let Some(e) = frame_processing_err {
3133            // Any frame error is terminal, so now just return.
3134            return Err(e);
3135        }
3136
3137        // Only log the remote transport parameters once the connection is
3138        // established (i.e. after frames have been fully parsed) and only
3139        // once per connection.
3140        if self.is_established() {
3141            qlog_with_type!(QLOG_PARAMS_SET, self.qlog, q, {
3142                if !self.qlog.logged_peer_params {
3143                    let ev_data = self
3144                        .peer_transport_params
3145                        .to_qlog(TransportOwner::Remote, self.handshake.cipher());
3146
3147                    q.add_event_data_with_instant(ev_data, now).ok();
3148
3149                    self.qlog.logged_peer_params = true;
3150                }
3151            });
3152        }
3153
3154        // Following flag used to upgrade datagram size, if probe is successful.
3155        let mut pmtud_probe = false;
3156
3157        // Process acked frames. Note that several packets from several paths
3158        // might have been acked by the received packet.
3159        for (_, p) in self.paths.iter_mut() {
3160            for acked in p.recovery.get_acked_frames(epoch) {
3161                match acked {
3162                    frame::Frame::Ping {
3163                        mtu_probe: Some(mtu_probe),
3164                    } => {
3165                        let pmtud_next = p.pmtud.get_current();
3166                        p.pmtud.set_current(cmp::max(pmtud_next, mtu_probe));
3167
3168                        // Stop sending path MTU probes after successful probe.
3169                        p.pmtud.should_probe(false);
3170                        pmtud_probe = true;
3171
3172                        trace!(
3173                            "{} pmtud acked; pmtu size {:?}",
3174                            self.trace_id,
3175                            p.pmtud.get_current()
3176                        );
3177                    },
3178
3179                    frame::Frame::ACK { ranges, .. } => {
3180                        // Stop acknowledging packets less than or equal to the
3181                        // largest acknowledged in the sent ACK frame that, in
3182                        // turn, got acked.
3183                        if let Some(largest_acked) = ranges.last() {
3184                            self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch]
3185                                .recv_pkt_need_ack
3186                                .remove_until(largest_acked);
3187                        }
3188                    },
3189
3190                    frame::Frame::CryptoHeader { offset, length } => {
3191                        self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch]
3192                            .crypto_stream
3193                            .send
3194                            .ack_and_drop(offset, length);
3195                    },
3196
3197                    frame::Frame::StreamHeader {
3198                        stream_id,
3199                        offset,
3200                        length,
3201                        ..
3202                    } => {
3203                        let stream = match self.streams.get_mut(stream_id) {
3204                            Some(v) => v,
3205
3206                            None => continue,
3207                        };
3208
3209                        stream.send.ack_and_drop(offset, length);
3210
3211                        self.tx_buffered =
3212                            self.tx_buffered.saturating_sub(length);
3213
3214                        qlog_with_type!(QLOG_DATA_MV, self.qlog, q, {
3215                            let ev_data = EventData::DataMoved(
3216                                qlog::events::quic::DataMoved {
3217                                    stream_id: Some(stream_id),
3218                                    offset: Some(offset),
3219                                    length: Some(length as u64),
3220                                    from: Some(DataRecipient::Transport),
3221                                    to: Some(DataRecipient::Dropped),
3222                                    ..Default::default()
3223                                },
3224                            );
3225
3226                            q.add_event_data_with_instant(ev_data, now).ok();
3227                        });
3228
3229                        // Only collect the stream if it is complete and not
3230                        // readable. If it is readable, it will get collected when
3231                        // stream_recv() is used.
3232                        if stream.is_complete() && !stream.is_readable() {
3233                            let local = stream.local;
3234                            self.streams.collect(stream_id, local);
3235                        }
3236                    },
3237
3238                    frame::Frame::HandshakeDone => {
3239                        // Explicitly set this to true, so that if the frame was
3240                        // already scheduled for retransmission, it is aborted.
3241                        self.handshake_done_sent = true;
3242
3243                        self.handshake_done_acked = true;
3244                    },
3245
3246                    frame::Frame::ResetStream { stream_id, .. } => {
3247                        let stream = match self.streams.get_mut(stream_id) {
3248                            Some(v) => v,
3249
3250                            None => continue,
3251                        };
3252
3253                        // Only collect the stream if it is complete and not
3254                        // readable. If it is readable, it will get collected when
3255                        // stream_recv() is used.
3256                        if stream.is_complete() && !stream.is_readable() {
3257                            let local = stream.local;
3258                            self.streams.collect(stream_id, local);
3259                        }
3260                    },
3261
3262                    _ => (),
3263                }
3264            }
3265
3266            // Update max datagram send size with newly acked probe size.
3267            if pmtud_probe {
3268                trace!(
3269                    "{} updating pmtu {:?}",
3270                    p.pmtud.get_current(),
3271                    self.trace_id
3272                );
3273
3274                qlog_with_type!(
3275                    EventType::ConnectivityEventType(
3276                        ConnectivityEventType::MtuUpdated
3277                    ),
3278                    self.qlog,
3279                    q,
3280                    {
3281                        let pmtu_data = EventData::MtuUpdated(
3282                            qlog::events::connectivity::MtuUpdated {
3283                                old: Some(p.recovery.max_datagram_size() as u16),
3284                                new: p.pmtud.get_current() as u16,
3285                                done: Some(pmtud_probe),
3286                            },
3287                        );
3288
3289                        q.add_event_data_with_instant(pmtu_data, now).ok();
3290                    }
3291                );
3292
3293                p.recovery
3294                    .pmtud_update_max_datagram_size(p.pmtud.get_current());
3295            }
3296        }
3297
3298        // Now that we processed all the frames, if there is a path that has no
3299        // Destination CID, try to allocate one.
3300        let no_dcid = self
3301            .paths
3302            .iter_mut()
3303            .filter(|(_, p)| p.active_dcid_seq.is_none());
3304
3305        for (pid, p) in no_dcid {
3306            if self.ids.zero_length_dcid() {
3307                p.active_dcid_seq = Some(0);
3308                continue;
3309            }
3310
3311            let dcid_seq = match self.ids.lowest_available_dcid_seq() {
3312                Some(seq) => seq,
3313                None => break,
3314            };
3315
3316            self.ids.link_dcid_to_path_id(dcid_seq, pid)?;
3317
3318            p.active_dcid_seq = Some(dcid_seq);
3319        }
3320
3321        // We only record the time of arrival of the largest packet number
3322        // that still needs to be acked, to be used for ACK delay calculation.
3323        if self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].recv_pkt_need_ack.last() < Some(pn) {
3324            self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].largest_rx_pkt_time = now;
3325        }
3326
3327        self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].recv_pkt_num.insert(pn);
3328
3329        self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].recv_pkt_need_ack.push_item(pn);
3330
3331        self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].ack_elicited =
3332            cmp::max(self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].ack_elicited, ack_elicited);
3333
3334        self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].largest_rx_pkt_num =
3335            cmp::max(self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].largest_rx_pkt_num, pn);
3336
3337        if !probing {
3338            self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].largest_rx_non_probing_pkt_num = cmp::max(
3339                self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].largest_rx_non_probing_pkt_num,
3340                pn,
3341            );
3342
3343            // Did the peer migrated to another path?
3344            let active_path_id = self.paths.get_active_path_id()?;
3345
3346            if self.is_server &&
3347                recv_pid != active_path_id &&
3348                self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].largest_rx_non_probing_pkt_num == pn
3349            {
3350                self.on_peer_migrated(recv_pid, self.disable_dcid_reuse, now)?;
3351            }
3352        }
3353
3354        if let Some(idle_timeout) = self.idle_timeout() {
3355            self.idle_timer = Some(now + idle_timeout);
3356        }
3357
3358        // Update send capacity.
3359        self.update_tx_cap();
3360
3361        self.recv_count += 1;
3362        self.paths.get_mut(recv_pid)?.recv_count += 1;
3363
3364        let read = b.off() + aead_tag_len;
3365
3366        self.recv_bytes += read as u64;
3367        self.paths.get_mut(recv_pid)?.recv_bytes += read as u64;
3368
3369        // An Handshake packet has been received from the client and has been
3370        // successfully processed, so we can drop the initial state and consider
3371        // the client's address to be verified.
3372        if self.is_server && hdr.ty == packet::Type::Handshake {
3373            self.drop_epoch_state(packet::Epoch::Initial, now);
3374
3375            self.paths.get_mut(recv_pid)?.verified_peer_address = true;
3376        }
3377
3378        self.ack_eliciting_sent = false;
3379
3380        Ok(read)
3381    }
3382
3383    /// Writes a single QUIC packet to be sent to the peer.
3384    ///
3385    /// On success the number of bytes written to the output buffer is
3386    /// returned, or [`Done`] if there was nothing to write.
3387    ///
3388    /// The application should call `send()` multiple times until [`Done`] is
3389    /// returned, indicating that there are no more packets to send. It is
3390    /// recommended that `send()` be called in the following cases:
3391    ///
3392    ///  * When the application receives QUIC packets from the peer (that is,
3393    ///    any time [`recv()`] is also called).
3394    ///
3395    ///  * When the connection timer expires (that is, any time [`on_timeout()`]
3396    ///    is also called).
3397    ///
3398    ///  * When the application sends data to the peer (for example, any time
3399    ///    [`stream_send()`] or [`stream_shutdown()`] are called).
3400    ///
3401    ///  * When the application receives data from the peer (for example any
3402    ///    time [`stream_recv()`] is called).
3403    ///
3404    /// Once [`is_draining()`] returns `true`, it is no longer necessary to call
3405    /// `send()` and all calls will return [`Done`].
3406    ///
3407    /// [`Done`]: enum.Error.html#variant.Done
3408    /// [`recv()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.recv
3409    /// [`on_timeout()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.on_timeout
3410    /// [`stream_send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_send
3411    /// [`stream_shutdown()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_shutdown
3412    /// [`stream_recv()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_recv
3413    /// [`is_draining()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.is_draining
3414    ///
3415    /// ## Examples:
3416    ///
3417    /// ```no_run
3418    /// # let mut out = [0; 512];
3419    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
3420    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
3421    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
3422    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
3423    /// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
3424    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
3425    /// loop {
3426    ///     let (write, send_info) = match conn.send(&mut out) {
3427    ///         Ok(v) => v,
3428    ///
3429    ///         Err(quiche::Error::Done) => {
3430    ///             // Done writing.
3431    ///             break;
3432    ///         },
3433    ///
3434    ///         Err(e) => {
3435    ///             // An error occurred, handle it.
3436    ///             break;
3437    ///         },
3438    ///     };
3439    ///
3440    ///     socket.send_to(&out[..write], &send_info.to).unwrap();
3441    /// }
3442    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
3443    /// ```
3444    pub fn send(&mut self, out: &mut [u8]) -> Result<(usize, SendInfo)> {
3445        self.send_on_path(out, None, None)
3446    }
3447
3448    /// Writes a single QUIC packet to be sent to the peer from the specified
3449    /// local address `from` to the destination address `to`.
3450    ///
3451    /// The behavior of this method differs depending on the value of the `from`
3452    /// and `to` parameters:
3453    ///
3454    ///  * If both are `Some`, then the method only consider the 4-tuple
3455    ///    (`from`, `to`). Application can monitor the 4-tuple availability,
3456    ///    either by monitoring [`path_event_next()`] events or by relying on
3457    ///    the [`paths_iter()`] method. If the provided 4-tuple does not exist
3458    ///    on the connection (anymore), it returns an [`InvalidState`].
3459    ///
3460    ///  * If `from` is `Some` and `to` is `None`, then the method only
3461    ///    considers sending packets on paths having `from` as local address.
3462    ///
3463    ///  * If `to` is `Some` and `from` is `None`, then the method only
3464    ///    considers sending packets on paths having `to` as peer address.
3465    ///
3466    ///  * If both are `None`, all available paths are considered.
3467    ///
3468    /// On success the number of bytes written to the output buffer is
3469    /// returned, or [`Done`] if there was nothing to write.
3470    ///
3471    /// The application should call `send_on_path()` multiple times until
3472    /// [`Done`] is returned, indicating that there are no more packets to
3473    /// send. It is recommended that `send_on_path()` be called in the
3474    /// following cases:
3475    ///
3476    ///  * When the application receives QUIC packets from the peer (that is,
3477    ///    any time [`recv()`] is also called).
3478    ///
3479    ///  * When the connection timer expires (that is, any time [`on_timeout()`]
3480    ///    is also called).
3481    ///
3482    ///  * When the application sends data to the peer (for examples, any time
3483    ///    [`stream_send()`] or [`stream_shutdown()`] are called).
3484    ///
3485    ///  * When the application receives data from the peer (for example any
3486    ///    time [`stream_recv()`] is called).
3487    ///
3488    /// Once [`is_draining()`] returns `true`, it is no longer necessary to call
3489    /// `send_on_path()` and all calls will return [`Done`].
3490    ///
3491    /// [`Done`]: enum.Error.html#variant.Done
3492    /// [`InvalidState`]: enum.Error.html#InvalidState
3493    /// [`recv()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.recv
3494    /// [`on_timeout()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.on_timeout
3495    /// [`stream_send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_send
3496    /// [`stream_shutdown()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_shutdown
3497    /// [`stream_recv()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_recv
3498    /// [`path_event_next()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.path_event_next
3499    /// [`paths_iter()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.paths_iter
3500    /// [`is_draining()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.is_draining
3501    ///
3502    /// ## Examples:
3503    ///
3504    /// ```no_run
3505    /// # let mut out = [0; 512];
3506    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
3507    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
3508    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
3509    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
3510    /// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
3511    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
3512    /// loop {
3513    ///     let (write, send_info) = match conn.send_on_path(&mut out, Some(local), Some(peer)) {
3514    ///         Ok(v) => v,
3515    ///
3516    ///         Err(quiche::Error::Done) => {
3517    ///             // Done writing.
3518    ///             break;
3519    ///         },
3520    ///
3521    ///         Err(e) => {
3522    ///             // An error occurred, handle it.
3523    ///             break;
3524    ///         },
3525    ///     };
3526    ///
3527    ///     socket.send_to(&out[..write], &send_info.to).unwrap();
3528    /// }
3529    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
3530    /// ```
3531    pub fn send_on_path(
3532        &mut self, out: &mut [u8], from: Option<SocketAddr>,
3533        to: Option<SocketAddr>,
3534    ) -> Result<(usize, SendInfo)> {
3535        if out.is_empty() {
3536            return Err(Error::BufferTooShort);
3537        }
3538
3539        if self.is_closed() || self.is_draining() {
3540            return Err(Error::Done);
3541        }
3542
3543        let now = time::Instant::now();
3544
3545        if self.local_error.is_none() {
3546            self.do_handshake(now)?;
3547        }
3548
3549        // Forwarding the error value here could confuse
3550        // applications, as they may not expect getting a `recv()`
3551        // error when calling `send()`.
3552        //
3553        // We simply fall-through to sending packets, which should
3554        // take care of terminating the connection as needed.
3555        let _ = self.process_undecrypted_0rtt_packets();
3556
3557        // There's no point in trying to send a packet if the Initial secrets
3558        // have not been derived yet, so return early.
3559        if !self.derived_initial_secrets {
3560            return Err(Error::Done);
3561        }
3562
3563        let mut has_initial = false;
3564
3565        let mut done = 0;
3566
3567        // Limit output packet size to respect the sender and receiver's
3568        // maximum UDP payload size limit.
3569        let mut left = cmp::min(out.len(), self.max_send_udp_payload_size());
3570
3571        let send_pid = match (from, to) {
3572            (Some(f), Some(t)) => self
3573                .paths
3574                .path_id_from_addrs(&(f, t))
3575                .ok_or(Error::InvalidState)?,
3576
3577            _ => self.get_send_path_id(from, to)?,
3578        };
3579
3580        let send_path = self.paths.get_mut(send_pid)?;
3581
3582        // Update max datagram size to allow path MTU discovery probe to be sent.
3583        if send_path.pmtud.get_probe_status() {
3584            let size = if self.handshake_confirmed || self.handshake_done_sent {
3585                send_path.pmtud.get_probe_size()
3586            } else {
3587                send_path.pmtud.get_current()
3588            };
3589
3590            send_path.recovery.pmtud_update_max_datagram_size(size);
3591
3592            left = cmp::min(out.len(), send_path.recovery.max_datagram_size());
3593        }
3594
3595        // Limit data sent by the server based on the amount of data received
3596        // from the client before its address is validated.
3597        if !send_path.verified_peer_address && self.is_server {
3598            left = cmp::min(left, send_path.max_send_bytes);
3599        }
3600
3601        // Generate coalesced packets.
3602        while left > 0 {
3603            let (ty, written) = match self.send_single(
3604                &mut out[done..done + left],
3605                send_pid,
3606                has_initial,
3607                now,
3608            ) {
3609                Ok(v) => v,
3610
3611                Err(Error::BufferTooShort) | Err(Error::Done) => break,
3612
3613                Err(e) => return Err(e),
3614            };
3615
3616            done += written;
3617            left -= written;
3618
3619            match ty {
3620                packet::Type::Initial => has_initial = true,
3621
3622                // No more packets can be coalesced after a 1-RTT.
3623                packet::Type::Short => break,
3624
3625                _ => (),
3626            };
3627
3628            // When sending multiple PTO probes, don't coalesce them together,
3629            // so they are sent on separate UDP datagrams.
3630            if let Ok(epoch) = ty.to_epoch() {
3631                if self.paths.get_mut(send_pid)?.recovery.loss_probes(epoch) > 0 {
3632                    break;
3633                }
3634            }
3635
3636            // Don't coalesce packets that must go on different paths.
3637            if !(from.is_some() && to.is_some()) &&
3638                self.get_send_path_id(from, to)? != send_pid
3639            {
3640                break;
3641            }
3642        }
3643
3644        if done == 0 {
3645            self.last_tx_data = self.tx_data;
3646
3647            return Err(Error::Done);
3648        }
3649
3650        // Pad UDP datagram if it contains a QUIC Initial packet.
3651        #[cfg(not(feature = "fuzzing"))]
3652        if has_initial && left > 0 && done < MIN_CLIENT_INITIAL_LEN {
3653            let pad_len = cmp::min(left, MIN_CLIENT_INITIAL_LEN - done);
3654
3655            // Fill padding area with null bytes, to avoid leaking information
3656            // in case the application reuses the packet buffer.
3657            out[done..done + pad_len].fill(0);
3658
3659            done += pad_len;
3660        }
3661
3662        let send_path = self.paths.get(send_pid)?;
3663
3664        let info = SendInfo {
3665            from: send_path.local_addr(),
3666            to: send_path.peer_addr(),
3667
3668            at: send_path.recovery.get_packet_send_time(),
3669        };
3670
3671        Ok((done, info))
3672    }
3673
3674    fn send_single(
3675        &mut self, out: &mut [u8], send_pid: usize, has_initial: bool,
3676        now: time::Instant,
3677    ) -> Result<(packet::Type, usize)> {
3678        if out.is_empty() {
3679            return Err(Error::BufferTooShort);
3680        }
3681
3682        if self.is_draining() {
3683            return Err(Error::Done);
3684        }
3685
3686        let is_closing = self.local_error.is_some();
3687
3688        let out_len = out.len();
3689
3690        let mut b = octets::OctetsMut::with_slice(out);
3691
3692        let pkt_type = self.write_pkt_type(send_pid)?;
3693
3694        let max_dgram_len = if !self.dgram_send_queue.is_empty() {
3695            self.dgram_max_writable_len()
3696        } else {
3697            None
3698        };
3699
3700        let epoch = pkt_type.to_epoch()?;
3701        let pkt_space = &mut self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch];
3702
3703        // Process lost frames. There might be several paths having lost frames.
3704        for (_, p) in self.paths.iter_mut() {
3705            for lost in p.recovery.get_lost_frames(epoch) {
3706                match lost {
3707                    frame::Frame::CryptoHeader { offset, length } => {
3708                        pkt_space.crypto_stream.send.retransmit(offset, length);
3709
3710                        self.stream_retrans_bytes += length as u64;
3711                        p.stream_retrans_bytes += length as u64;
3712
3713                        self.retrans_count += 1;
3714                        p.retrans_count += 1;
3715                    },
3716
3717                    frame::Frame::StreamHeader {
3718                        stream_id,
3719                        offset,
3720                        length,
3721                        fin,
3722                    } => {
3723                        let stream = match self.streams.get_mut(stream_id) {
3724                            Some(v) => v,
3725
3726                            None => continue,
3727                        };
3728
3729                        let was_flushable = stream.is_flushable();
3730
3731                        let empty_fin = length == 0 && fin;
3732
3733                        stream.send.retransmit(offset, length);
3734
3735                        // If the stream is now flushable push it to the
3736                        // flushable queue, but only if it wasn't already
3737                        // queued.
3738                        //
3739                        // Consider the stream flushable also when we are
3740                        // sending a zero-length frame that has the fin flag
3741                        // set.
3742                        if (stream.is_flushable() || empty_fin) && !was_flushable
3743                        {
3744                            let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
3745                            self.streams.insert_flushable(&priority_key);
3746                        }
3747
3748                        self.stream_retrans_bytes += length as u64;
3749                        p.stream_retrans_bytes += length as u64;
3750
3751                        self.retrans_count += 1;
3752                        p.retrans_count += 1;
3753                    },
3754
3755                    frame::Frame::ACK { .. } => {
3756                        pkt_space.ack_elicited = true;
3757                    },
3758
3759                    frame::Frame::ResetStream {
3760                        stream_id,
3761                        error_code,
3762                        final_size,
3763                    } =>
3764                        if self.streams.get(stream_id).is_some() {
3765                            self.streams
3766                                .insert_reset(stream_id, error_code, final_size);
3767                        },
3768
3769                    // Retransmit HANDSHAKE_DONE only if it hasn't been acked at
3770                    // least once already.
3771                    frame::Frame::HandshakeDone if !self.handshake_done_acked => {
3772                        self.handshake_done_sent = false;
3773                    },
3774
3775                    frame::Frame::MaxStreamData { stream_id, .. } => {
3776                        if self.streams.get(stream_id).is_some() {
3777                            self.streams.insert_almost_full(stream_id);
3778                        }
3779                    },
3780
3781                    frame::Frame::MaxData { .. } => {
3782                        self.almost_full = true;
3783                    },
3784
3785                    frame::Frame::NewConnectionId { seq_num, .. } => {
3786                        self.ids.mark_advertise_new_scid_seq(seq_num, true);
3787                    },
3788
3789                    frame::Frame::RetireConnectionId { seq_num } => {
3790                        self.ids.mark_retire_dcid_seq(seq_num, true)?;
3791                    },
3792
3793                    frame::Frame::Ping { mtu_probe } if mtu_probe.is_some() => {
3794                        p.pmtud.pmtu_probe_lost();
3795                    },
3796
3797                    _ => (),
3798                }
3799            }
3800        }
3801
3802        let is_app_limited = self.delivery_rate_check_if_app_limited();
3803        let n_paths = self.paths.len();
3804        let path = self.paths.get_mut(send_pid)?;
3805        let flow_control = &mut self.flow_control;
3806        let pkt_space = &mut self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch];
3807
3808        let mut left = if path.pmtud.is_enabled() {
3809            // Limit output buffer size by estimated path MTU.
3810            cmp::min(path.pmtud.get_current(), b.cap())
3811        } else {
3812            b.cap()
3813        };
3814
3815        let pn = self.next_pkt_num;
3816        let largest_acked_pkt =
3817            path.recovery.get_largest_acked_on_epoch(epoch).unwrap_or(0);
3818        let pn_len = packet::pkt_num_len(pn, largest_acked_pkt);
3819
3820        // The AEAD overhead at the current encryption level.
3821        let crypto_overhead = pkt_space.crypto_overhead().ok_or(Error::Done)?;
3822
3823        let dcid_seq = path.active_dcid_seq.ok_or(Error::OutOfIdentifiers)?;
3824
3825        let dcid =
3826            ConnectionId::from_ref(self.ids.get_dcid(dcid_seq)?.cid.as_ref());
3827
3828        let scid = if let Some(scid_seq) = path.active_scid_seq {
3829            ConnectionId::from_ref(self.ids.get_scid(scid_seq)?.cid.as_ref())
3830        } else if pkt_type == packet::Type::Short {
3831            ConnectionId::default()
3832        } else {
3833            return Err(Error::InvalidState);
3834        };
3835
3836        let hdr = Header {
3837            ty: pkt_type,
3838
3839            version: self.version,
3840
3841            dcid,
3842            scid,
3843
3844            pkt_num: 0,
3845            pkt_num_len: pn_len,
3846
3847            // Only clone token for Initial packets, as other packets don't have
3848            // this field (Retry doesn't count, as it's not encoded as part of
3849            // this code path).
3850            token: if pkt_type == packet::Type::Initial {
3851                self.token.clone()
3852            } else {
3853                None
3854            },
3855
3856            versions: None,
3857            key_phase: self.key_phase,
3858        };
3859
3860        hdr.to_bytes(&mut b)?;
3861
3862        let hdr_trace = if log::max_level() == log::LevelFilter::Trace {
3863            Some(format!("{hdr:?}"))
3864        } else {
3865            None
3866        };
3867
3868        let hdr_ty = hdr.ty;
3869
3870        #[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
3871        let qlog_pkt_hdr = self.qlog.streamer.as_ref().map(|_q| {
3872            qlog::events::quic::PacketHeader::with_type(
3873                hdr.ty.to_qlog(),
3874                Some(pn),
3875                Some(hdr.version),
3876                Some(&hdr.scid),
3877                Some(&hdr.dcid),
3878            )
3879        });
3880
3881        // Calculate the space required for the packet, including the header
3882        // the payload length, the packet number and the AEAD overhead.
3883        let mut overhead = b.off() + pn_len + crypto_overhead;
3884
3885        // We assume that the payload length, which is only present in long
3886        // header packets, can always be encoded with a 2-byte varint.
3887        if pkt_type != packet::Type::Short {
3888            overhead += PAYLOAD_LENGTH_LEN;
3889        }
3890
3891        // Make sure we have enough space left for the packet overhead.
3892        match left.checked_sub(overhead) {
3893            Some(v) => left = v,
3894
3895            None => {
3896                // We can't send more because there isn't enough space available
3897                // in the output buffer.
3898                //
3899                // This usually happens when we try to send a new packet but
3900                // failed because cwnd is almost full. In such case app_limited
3901                // is set to false here to make cwnd grow when ACK is received.
3902                path.recovery.update_app_limited(false);
3903                return Err(Error::Done);
3904            },
3905        }
3906
3907        // Make sure there is enough space for the minimum payload length.
3908        if left < PAYLOAD_MIN_LEN {
3909            path.recovery.update_app_limited(false);
3910            return Err(Error::Done);
3911        }
3912
3913        let mut frames: SmallVec<[frame::Frame; 1]> = SmallVec::new();
3914
3915        let mut ack_eliciting = false;
3916        let mut in_flight = false;
3917        // Foll. flag used to upgrade datagram size, if probe successful
3918        let mut pmtud_probe = false;
3919        let mut has_data = false;
3920
3921        // Whether or not we should explicitly elicit an ACK via PING frame if we
3922        // implicitly elicit one otherwise.
3923        let ack_elicit_required = path.recovery.should_elicit_ack(epoch);
3924
3925        let header_offset = b.off();
3926
3927        // Reserve space for payload length in advance. Since we don't yet know
3928        // what the final length will be, we reserve 2 bytes in all cases.
3929        //
3930        // Only long header packets have an explicit length field.
3931        if pkt_type != packet::Type::Short {
3932            b.skip(PAYLOAD_LENGTH_LEN)?;
3933        }
3934
3935        packet::encode_pkt_num(pn, pn_len, &mut b)?;
3936
3937        let payload_offset = b.off();
3938
3939        let cwnd_available =
3940            path.recovery.cwnd_available().saturating_sub(overhead);
3941
3942        let left_before_packing_ack_frame = left;
3943
3944        // Create ACK frame.
3945        //
3946        // When we need to explicitly elicit an ACK via PING later, go ahead and
3947        // generate an ACK (if there's anything to ACK) since we're going to
3948        // send a packet with PING anyways, even if we haven't received anything
3949        // ACK eliciting.
3950        if pkt_space.recv_pkt_need_ack.len() > 0 &&
3951            (pkt_space.ack_elicited || ack_elicit_required) &&
3952            (!is_closing ||
3953                (pkt_type == Type::Handshake &&
3954                    self.local_error
3955                        .as_ref()
3956                        .is_some_and(|le| le.is_app))) &&
3957            path.active()
3958        {
3959            let ack_delay = pkt_space.largest_rx_pkt_time.elapsed();
3960
3961            let ack_delay = ack_delay.as_micros() as u64 /
3962                2_u64
3963                    .pow(self.local_transport_params.ack_delay_exponent as u32);
3964
3965            let frame = frame::Frame::ACK {
3966                ack_delay,
3967                ranges: pkt_space.recv_pkt_need_ack.clone(),
3968                ecn_counts: None, // sending ECN is not supported at this time
3969            };
3970
3971            // When a PING frame needs to be sent, avoid sending the ACK if
3972            // there is not enough cwnd available for both (note that PING
3973            // frames are always 1 byte, so we just need to check that the
3974            // ACK's length is lower than cwnd).
3975            if pkt_space.ack_elicited || frame.wire_len() < cwnd_available {
3976                // ACK-only packets are not congestion controlled so ACKs must
3977                // be bundled considering the buffer capacity only, and not the
3978                // available cwnd.
3979                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
3980                    pkt_space.ack_elicited = false;
3981                }
3982            }
3983        }
3984
3985        // Limit output packet size by congestion window size.
3986        left = cmp::min(
3987            left,
3988            // Bytes consumed by ACK frames.
3989            cwnd_available.saturating_sub(left_before_packing_ack_frame - left),
3990        );
3991
3992        let mut challenge_data = None;
3993
3994        let active_path = self.paths.get_active_mut()?;
3995
3996        if pkt_type == packet::Type::Short {
3997            // Create PMTUD probe.
3998            //
3999            // In order to send a PMTUD probe the current `left` value, which was
4000            // already limited by the current PMTU measure, needs to be ignored,
4001            // but the outgoing packet still needs to be limited by
4002            // the output buffer size, as well as the congestion
4003            // window.
4004            //
4005            // In addition, the PMTUD probe is only generated when the handshake
4006            // is confirmed, to avoid interfering with the handshake
4007            // (e.g. due to the anti-amplification limits).
4008
4009            let pmtu_probe = active_path.should_send_pmtu_probe(
4010                self.handshake_confirmed,
4011                self.handshake_done_sent,
4012                out_len,
4013                is_closing,
4014                frames.is_empty(),
4015            );
4016
4017            trace!("{} pmtud probe status {} hs_con={} hs_sent={} cwnd_avail={} out_len={} left={}", self.trace_id, pmtu_probe, self.handshake_confirmed, self.handshake_done_sent,
4018                    active_path.recovery.cwnd_available(), out_len, left);
4019
4020            if pmtu_probe {
4021                trace!(
4022                    "{} sending pmtud probe pmtu_probe={} next_size={} pmtu={}",
4023                    self.trace_id,
4024                    active_path.pmtud.get_probe_size(),
4025                    active_path.pmtud.get_probe_status(),
4026                    active_path.pmtud.get_current(),
4027                );
4028
4029                left = active_path.pmtud.get_probe_size();
4030
4031                match left.checked_sub(overhead) {
4032                    Some(v) => left = v,
4033
4034                    None => {
4035                        // We can't send more because there isn't enough space
4036                        // available in the output buffer.
4037                        //
4038                        // This usually happens when we try to send a new packet
4039                        // but failed because cwnd is almost full.
4040                        //
4041                        // In such case app_limited is set to false here to make
4042                        // cwnd grow when ACK is received.
4043                        active_path.recovery.update_app_limited(false);
4044                        return Err(Error::Done);
4045                    },
4046                }
4047
4048                let frame = frame::Frame::Padding {
4049                    len: active_path.pmtud.get_probe_size() - overhead - 1,
4050                };
4051
4052                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4053                    let frame = frame::Frame::Ping {
4054                        mtu_probe: Some(active_path.pmtud.get_probe_size()),
4055                    };
4056
4057                    if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4058                        ack_eliciting = true;
4059                        in_flight = true;
4060                    }
4061                }
4062
4063                pmtud_probe = true;
4064            }
4065
4066            let path = self.paths.get_mut(send_pid)?;
4067            // Create PATH_RESPONSE frame if needed.
4068            // We do not try to ensure that these are really sent.
4069            while let Some(challenge) = path.pop_received_challenge() {
4070                let frame = frame::Frame::PathResponse { data: challenge };
4071
4072                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4073                    ack_eliciting = true;
4074                    in_flight = true;
4075                } else {
4076                    // If there are other pending PATH_RESPONSE, don't lose them
4077                    // now.
4078                    break;
4079                }
4080            }
4081
4082            // Create PATH_CHALLENGE frame if needed.
4083            if path.validation_requested() {
4084                // TODO: ensure that data is unique over paths.
4085                let data = rand::rand_u64().to_be_bytes();
4086
4087                let frame = frame::Frame::PathChallenge { data };
4088
4089                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4090                    // Let's notify the path once we know the packet size.
4091                    challenge_data = Some(data);
4092
4093                    ack_eliciting = true;
4094                    in_flight = true;
4095                }
4096            }
4097
4098            if let Some(key_update) = pkt_space.key_update.as_mut() {
4099                key_update.update_acked = true;
4100            }
4101        }
4102
4103        let path = self.paths.get_mut(send_pid)?;
4104
4105        if pkt_type == packet::Type::Short && !is_closing {
4106            // Create NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames as needed.
4107            while let Some(seq_num) = self.ids.next_advertise_new_scid_seq() {
4108                let frame = self.ids.get_new_connection_id_frame_for(seq_num)?;
4109
4110                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4111                    self.ids.mark_advertise_new_scid_seq(seq_num, false);
4112
4113                    ack_eliciting = true;
4114                    in_flight = true;
4115                } else {
4116                    break;
4117                }
4118            }
4119        }
4120
4121        if pkt_type == packet::Type::Short && !is_closing && path.active() {
4122            // Create HANDSHAKE_DONE frame.
4123            // self.should_send_handshake_done() but without the need to borrow
4124            if self.handshake_completed &&
4125                !self.handshake_done_sent &&
4126                self.is_server
4127            {
4128                let frame = frame::Frame::HandshakeDone;
4129
4130                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4131                    self.handshake_done_sent = true;
4132
4133                    ack_eliciting = true;
4134                    in_flight = true;
4135                }
4136            }
4137
4138            // Create MAX_STREAMS_BIDI frame.
4139            if self.streams.should_update_max_streams_bidi() {
4140                let frame = frame::Frame::MaxStreamsBidi {
4141                    max: self.streams.max_streams_bidi_next(),
4142                };
4143
4144                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4145                    self.streams.update_max_streams_bidi();
4146
4147                    ack_eliciting = true;
4148                    in_flight = true;
4149                }
4150            }
4151
