4152            // Create MAX_STREAMS_UNI frame.
4153            if self.streams.should_update_max_streams_uni() {
4154                let frame = frame::Frame::MaxStreamsUni {
4155                    max: self.streams.max_streams_uni_next(),
4156                };
4157
4158                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4159                    self.streams.update_max_streams_uni();
4160
4161                    ack_eliciting = true;
4162                    in_flight = true;
4163                }
4164            }
4165
4166            // Create DATA_BLOCKED frame.
4167            if let Some(limit) = self.blocked_limit {
4168                let frame = frame::Frame::DataBlocked { limit };
4169
4170                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4171                    self.blocked_limit = None;
4172
4173                    ack_eliciting = true;
4174                    in_flight = true;
4175                }
4176            }
4177
4178            // Create MAX_STREAM_DATA frames as needed.
4179            for stream_id in self.streams.almost_full() {
4180                let stream = match self.streams.get_mut(stream_id) {
4181                    Some(v) => v,
4182
4183                    None => {
4184                        // The stream doesn't exist anymore, so remove it from
4185                        // the almost full set.
4186                        self.streams.remove_almost_full(stream_id);
4187                        continue;
4188                    },
4189                };
4190
4191                // Autotune the stream window size.
4192                stream.recv.autotune_window(now, path.recovery.rtt());
4193
4194                let frame = frame::Frame::MaxStreamData {
4195                    stream_id,
4196                    max: stream.recv.max_data_next(),
4197                };
4198
4199                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4200                    let recv_win = stream.recv.window();
4201
4202                    stream.recv.update_max_data(now);
4203
4204                    self.streams.remove_almost_full(stream_id);
4205
4206                    ack_eliciting = true;
4207                    in_flight = true;
4208
4209                    // Make sure the connection window always has some
4210                    // room compared to the stream window.
4211                    flow_control.ensure_window_lower_bound(
4212                        (recv_win as f64 * CONNECTION_WINDOW_FACTOR) as u64,
4213                    );
4214
4215                    // Also send MAX_DATA when MAX_STREAM_DATA is sent, to avoid a
4216                    // potential race condition.
4217                    self.almost_full = true;
4218                }
4219            }
4220
4221            // Create MAX_DATA frame as needed.
4222            if self.almost_full &&
4223                flow_control.max_data() < flow_control.max_data_next()
4224            {
4225                // Autotune the connection window size.
4226                flow_control.autotune_window(now, path.recovery.rtt());
4227
4228                let frame = frame::Frame::MaxData {
4229                    max: flow_control.max_data_next(),
4230                };
4231
4232                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4233                    self.almost_full = false;
4234
4235                    // Commits the new max_rx_data limit.
4236                    flow_control.update_max_data(now);
4237
4238                    ack_eliciting = true;
4239                    in_flight = true;
4240                }
4241            }
4242
4243            // Create STOP_SENDING frames as needed.
4244            for (stream_id, error_code) in self
4245                .streams
4246                .stopped()
4247                .map(|(&k, &v)| (k, v))
4248                .collect::<Vec<(u64, u64)>>()
4249            {
4250                let frame = frame::Frame::StopSending {
4251                    stream_id,
4252                    error_code,
4253                };
4254
4255                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4256                    self.streams.remove_stopped(stream_id);
4257
4258                    ack_eliciting = true;
4259                    in_flight = true;
4260                }
4261            }
4262
4263            // Create RESET_STREAM frames as needed.
4264            for (stream_id, (error_code, final_size)) in self
4265                .streams
4266                .reset()
4267                .map(|(&k, &v)| (k, v))
4268                .collect::<Vec<(u64, (u64, u64))>>()
4269            {
4270                let frame = frame::Frame::ResetStream {
4271                    stream_id,
4272                    error_code,
4273                    final_size,
4274                };
4275
4276                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4277                    self.streams.remove_reset(stream_id);
4278
4279                    ack_eliciting = true;
4280                    in_flight = true;
4281                }
4282            }
4283
4284            // Create STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frames as needed.
4285            for (stream_id, limit) in self
4286                .streams
4287                .blocked()
4288                .map(|(&k, &v)| (k, v))
4289                .collect::<Vec<(u64, u64)>>()
4290            {
4291                let frame = frame::Frame::StreamDataBlocked { stream_id, limit };
4292
4293                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4294                    self.streams.remove_blocked(stream_id);
4295
4296                    ack_eliciting = true;
4297                    in_flight = true;
4298                }
4299            }
4300
4301            // Create RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames as needed.
4302            while let Some(seq_num) = self.ids.next_retire_dcid_seq() {
4303                // The sequence number specified in a RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frame
4304                // MUST NOT refer to the Destination Connection ID field of the
4305                // packet in which the frame is contained.
4306                let dcid_seq = path.active_dcid_seq.ok_or(Error::InvalidState)?;
4307
4308                if seq_num == dcid_seq {
4309                    continue;
4310                }
4311
4312                let frame = frame::Frame::RetireConnectionId { seq_num };
4313
4314                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4315                    self.ids.mark_retire_dcid_seq(seq_num, false)?;
4316
4317                    ack_eliciting = true;
4318                    in_flight = true;
4319                } else {
4320                    break;
4321                }
4322            }
4323        }
4324
4325        // Create CONNECTION_CLOSE frame. Try to send this only on the active
4326        // path, unless it is the last one available.
4327        if path.active() || n_paths == 1 {
4328            if let Some(conn_err) = self.local_error.as_ref() {
4329                if conn_err.is_app {
4330                    // Create ApplicationClose frame.
4331                    if pkt_type == packet::Type::Short {
4332                        let frame = frame::Frame::ApplicationClose {
4333                            error_code: conn_err.error_code,
4334                            reason: conn_err.reason.clone(),
4335                        };
4336
4337                        if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4338                            let pto = path.recovery.pto();
4339                            self.draining_timer = Some(now + (pto * 3));
4340
4341                            ack_eliciting = true;
4342                            in_flight = true;
4343                        }
4344                    }
4345                } else {
4346                    // Create ConnectionClose frame.
4347                    let frame = frame::Frame::ConnectionClose {
4348                        error_code: conn_err.error_code,
4349                        frame_type: 0,
4350                        reason: conn_err.reason.clone(),
4351                    };
4352
4353                    if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4354                        let pto = path.recovery.pto();
4355                        self.draining_timer = Some(now + (pto * 3));
4356
4357                        ack_eliciting = true;
4358                        in_flight = true;
4359                    }
4360                }
4361            }
4362        }
4363
4364        // Create CRYPTO frame.
4365        if pkt_space.crypto_stream.is_flushable() &&
4366            left > frame::MAX_CRYPTO_OVERHEAD &&
4367            !is_closing &&
4368            path.active()
4369        {
4370            let crypto_off = pkt_space.crypto_stream.send.off_front();
4371
4372            // Encode the frame.
4373            //
4374            // Instead of creating a `frame::Frame` object, encode the frame
4375            // directly into the packet buffer.
4376            //
4377            // First we reserve some space in the output buffer for writing the
4378            // frame header (we assume the length field is always a 2-byte
4379            // varint as we don't know the value yet).
4380            //
4381            // Then we emit the data from the crypto stream's send buffer.
4382            //
4383            // Finally we go back and encode the frame header with the now
4384            // available information.
4385            let hdr_off = b.off();
4386            let hdr_len = 1 + // frame type
4387                octets::varint_len(crypto_off) + // offset
4388                2; // length, always encode as 2-byte varint
4389
4390            if let Some(max_len) = left.checked_sub(hdr_len) {
4391                let (mut crypto_hdr, mut crypto_payload) =
4392                    b.split_at(hdr_off + hdr_len)?;
4393
4394                // Write stream data into the packet buffer.
4395                let (len, _) = pkt_space
4396                    .crypto_stream
4397                    .send
4398                    .emit(&mut crypto_payload.as_mut()[..max_len])?;
4399
4400                // Encode the frame's header.
4401                //
4402                // Due to how `OctetsMut::split_at()` works, `crypto_hdr` starts
4403                // from the initial offset of `b` (rather than the current
4404                // offset), so it needs to be advanced to the
4405                // initial frame offset.
4406                crypto_hdr.skip(hdr_off)?;
4407
4408                frame::encode_crypto_header(
4409                    crypto_off,
4410                    len as u64,
4411                    &mut crypto_hdr,
4412                )?;
4413
4414                // Advance the packet buffer's offset.
4415                b.skip(hdr_len + len)?;
4416
4417                let frame = frame::Frame::CryptoHeader {
4418                    offset: crypto_off,
4419                    length: len,
4420                };
4421
4422                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4423                    ack_eliciting = true;
4424                    in_flight = true;
4425                    has_data = true;
4426                }
4427            }
4428        }
4429
4430        // The preference of data-bearing frame to include in a packet
4431        // is managed by `self.emit_dgram`. However, whether any frames
4432        // can be sent depends on the state of their buffers. In the case
4433        // where one type is preferred but its buffer is empty, fall back
4434        // to the other type in order not to waste this function call.
4435        let mut dgram_emitted = false;
4436        let dgrams_to_emit = max_dgram_len.is_some();
4437        let stream_to_emit = self.streams.has_flushable();
4438
4439        let mut do_dgram = self.emit_dgram && dgrams_to_emit;
4440        let do_stream = !self.emit_dgram && stream_to_emit;
4441
4442        if !do_stream && dgrams_to_emit {
4443            do_dgram = true;
4444        }
4445
4446        // Create DATAGRAM frame.
4447        if (pkt_type == packet::Type::Short || pkt_type == packet::Type::ZeroRTT) &&
4448            left > frame::MAX_DGRAM_OVERHEAD &&
4449            !is_closing &&
4450            path.active() &&
4451            do_dgram
4452        {
4453            if let Some(max_dgram_payload) = max_dgram_len {
4454                while let Some(len) = self.dgram_send_queue.peek_front_len() {
4455                    let hdr_off = b.off();
4456                    let hdr_len = 1 + // frame type
4457                        2; // length, always encode as 2-byte varint
4458
4459                    if (hdr_len + len) <= left {
4460                        // Front of the queue fits this packet, send it.
4461                        match self.dgram_send_queue.pop() {
4462                            Some(data) => {
4463                                // Encode the frame.
4464                                //
4465                                // Instead of creating a `frame::Frame` object,
4466                                // encode the frame directly into the packet
4467                                // buffer.
4468                                //
4469                                // First we reserve some space in the output
4470                                // buffer for writing the frame header (we
4471                                // assume the length field is always a 2-byte
4472                                // varint as we don't know the value yet).
4473                                //
4474                                // Then we emit the data from the DATAGRAM's
4475                                // buffer.
4476                                //
4477                                // Finally we go back and encode the frame
4478                                // header with the now available information.
4479                                let (mut dgram_hdr, mut dgram_payload) =
4480                                    b.split_at(hdr_off + hdr_len)?;
4481
4482                                dgram_payload.as_mut()[..len]
4483                                    .copy_from_slice(&data);
4484
4485                                // Encode the frame's header.
4486                                //
4487                                // Due to how `OctetsMut::split_at()` works,
4488                                // `dgram_hdr` starts from the initial offset
4489                                // of `b` (rather than the current offset), so
4490                                // it needs to be advanced to the initial frame
4491                                // offset.
4492                                dgram_hdr.skip(hdr_off)?;
4493
4494                                frame::encode_dgram_header(
4495                                    len as u64,
4496                                    &mut dgram_hdr,
4497                                )?;
4498
4499                                // Advance the packet buffer's offset.
4500                                b.skip(hdr_len + len)?;
4501
4502                                let frame =
4503                                    frame::Frame::DatagramHeader { length: len };
4504
4505                                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4506                                    ack_eliciting = true;
4507                                    in_flight = true;
4508                                    dgram_emitted = true;
4509                                    let _ =
4510                                        self.dgram_sent_count.saturating_add(1);
4511                                    let _ =
4512                                        path.dgram_sent_count.saturating_add(1);
4513                                }
4514                            },
4515
4516                            None => continue,
4517                        };
4518                    } else if len > max_dgram_payload {
4519                        // This dgram frame will never fit. Let's purge it.
4520                        self.dgram_send_queue.pop();
4521                    } else {
4522                        break;
4523                    }
4524                }
4525            }
4526        }
4527
4528        // Create a single STREAM frame for the first stream that is flushable.
4529        if (pkt_type == packet::Type::Short || pkt_type == packet::Type::ZeroRTT) &&
4530            left > frame::MAX_STREAM_OVERHEAD &&
4531            !is_closing &&
4532            path.active() &&
4533            !dgram_emitted
4534        {
4535            while let Some(priority_key) = self.streams.peek_flushable() {
4536                let stream_id = priority_key.id;
4537                let stream = match self.streams.get_mut(stream_id) {
4538                    // Avoid sending frames for streams that were already stopped.
4539                    //
4540                    // This might happen if stream data was buffered but not yet
4541                    // flushed on the wire when a STOP_SENDING frame is received.
4542                    Some(v) if !v.send.is_stopped() => v,
4543                    _ => {
4544                        self.streams.remove_flushable(&priority_key);
4545                        continue;
4546                    },
4547                };
4548
4549                let stream_off = stream.send.off_front();
4550
4551                // Encode the frame.
4552                //
4553                // Instead of creating a `frame::Frame` object, encode the frame
4554                // directly into the packet buffer.
4555                //
4556                // First we reserve some space in the output buffer for writing
4557                // the frame header (we assume the length field is always a
4558                // 2-byte varint as we don't know the value yet).
4559                //
4560                // Then we emit the data from the stream's send buffer.
4561                //
4562                // Finally we go back and encode the frame header with the now
4563                // available information.
4564                let hdr_off = b.off();
4565                let hdr_len = 1 + // frame type
4566                    octets::varint_len(stream_id) + // stream_id
4567                    octets::varint_len(stream_off) + // offset
4568                    2; // length, always encode as 2-byte varint
4569
4570                let max_len = match left.checked_sub(hdr_len) {
4571                    Some(v) => v,
4572                    None => {
4573                        let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
4574                        self.streams.remove_flushable(&priority_key);
4575
4576                        continue;
4577                    },
4578                };
4579
4580                let (mut stream_hdr, mut stream_payload) =
4581                    b.split_at(hdr_off + hdr_len)?;
4582
4583                // Write stream data into the packet buffer.
4584                let (len, fin) =
4585                    stream.send.emit(&mut stream_payload.as_mut()[..max_len])?;
4586
4587                // Encode the frame's header.
4588                //
4589                // Due to how `OctetsMut::split_at()` works, `stream_hdr` starts
4590                // from the initial offset of `b` (rather than the current
4591                // offset), so it needs to be advanced to the initial frame
4592                // offset.
4593                stream_hdr.skip(hdr_off)?;
4594
4595                frame::encode_stream_header(
4596                    stream_id,
4597                    stream_off,
4598                    len as u64,
4599                    fin,
4600                    &mut stream_hdr,
4601                )?;
4602
4603                // Advance the packet buffer's offset.
4604                b.skip(hdr_len + len)?;
4605
4606                let frame = frame::Frame::StreamHeader {
4607                    stream_id,
4608                    offset: stream_off,
4609                    length: len,
4610                    fin,
4611                };
4612
4613                if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4614                    ack_eliciting = true;
4615                    in_flight = true;
4616                    has_data = true;
4617                }
4618
4619                let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
4620                // If the stream is no longer flushable, remove it from the queue
4621                if !stream.is_flushable() {
4622                    self.streams.remove_flushable(&priority_key);
4623                } else if stream.incremental {
4624                    // Shuffle the incremental stream to the back of the
4625                    // queue.
4626                    self.streams.remove_flushable(&priority_key);
4627                    self.streams.insert_flushable(&priority_key);
4628                }
4629
4630                #[cfg(feature = "fuzzing")]
4631                // Coalesce STREAM frames when fuzzing.
4632                if left > frame::MAX_STREAM_OVERHEAD {
4633                    continue;
4634                }
4635
4636                break;
4637            }
4638        }
4639
4640        // Alternate trying to send DATAGRAMs next time.
4641        self.emit_dgram = !dgram_emitted;
4642
4643        // If no other ack-eliciting frame is sent, include a PING frame
4644        // - if PTO probe needed; OR
4645        // - if we've sent too many non ack-eliciting packets without having
4646        // sent an ACK eliciting one; OR
4647        // - the application requested an ack-eliciting frame be sent.
4648        if (ack_elicit_required || path.needs_ack_eliciting) &&
4649            !ack_eliciting &&
4650            left >= 1 &&
4651            !is_closing
4652        {
4653            let frame = frame::Frame::Ping { mtu_probe: None };
4654
4655            if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4656                ack_eliciting = true;
4657                in_flight = true;
4658            }
4659        }
4660
4661        if ack_eliciting && !pmtud_probe {
4662            path.needs_ack_eliciting = false;
4663            path.recovery.ping_sent(epoch);
4664        }
4665
4666        if !has_data &&
4667            !dgram_emitted &&
4668            cwnd_available > frame::MAX_STREAM_OVERHEAD
4669        {
4670            path.recovery.on_app_limited();
4671        }
4672
4673        if frames.is_empty() {
4674            // When we reach this point we are not able to write more, so set
4675            // app_limited to false.
4676            path.recovery.update_app_limited(false);
4677            return Err(Error::Done);
4678        }
4679
4680        // When coalescing a 1-RTT packet, we can't add padding in the UDP
4681        // datagram, so use PADDING frames instead.
4682        //
4683        // This is only needed if
4684        // 1) an Initial packet has already been written to the UDP datagram,
4685        // as Initial always requires padding.
4686        //
4687        // 2) this is a probing packet towards an unvalidated peer address.
4688        if (has_initial || !path.validated()) &&
4689            pkt_type == packet::Type::Short &&
4690            left >= 1
4691        {
4692            let frame = frame::Frame::Padding { len: left };
4693
4694            if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4695                in_flight = true;
4696            }
4697        }
4698
4699        // Pad payload so that it's always at least 4 bytes.
4700        if b.off() - payload_offset < PAYLOAD_MIN_LEN {
4701            let payload_len = b.off() - payload_offset;
4702
4703            let frame = frame::Frame::Padding {
4704                len: PAYLOAD_MIN_LEN - payload_len,
4705            };
4706
4707            #[allow(unused_assignments)]
4708            if push_frame_to_pkt!(b, frames, frame, left) {
4709                in_flight = true;
4710            }
4711        }
4712
4713        let payload_len = b.off() - payload_offset;
4714
4715        // Fill in payload length.
4716        if pkt_type != packet::Type::Short {
4717            let len = pn_len + payload_len + crypto_overhead;
4718
4719            let (_, mut payload_with_len) = b.split_at(header_offset)?;
4720            payload_with_len
4721                .put_varint_with_len(len as u64, PAYLOAD_LENGTH_LEN)?;
4722        }
4723
4724        trace!(
4725            "{} tx pkt {} len={} pn={} {}",
4726            self.trace_id,
4727            hdr_trace.unwrap_or_default(),
4728            payload_len,
4729            pn,
4730            AddrTupleFmt(path.local_addr(), path.peer_addr())
4731        );
4732
4733        #[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
4734        let mut qlog_frames: SmallVec<
4735            [qlog::events::quic::QuicFrame; 1],
4736        > = SmallVec::with_capacity(frames.len());
4737
4738        for frame in &mut frames {
4739            trace!("{} tx frm {:?}", self.trace_id, frame);
4740
4741            qlog_with_type!(QLOG_PACKET_TX, self.qlog, _q, {
4742                qlog_frames.push(frame.to_qlog());
4743            });
4744        }
4745
4746        qlog_with_type!(QLOG_PACKET_TX, self.qlog, q, {
4747            if let Some(header) = qlog_pkt_hdr {
4748                // Qlog packet raw info described at
4749                // https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-ietf-quic-qlog-main-schema-00#section-5.1
4750                //
4751                // `length` includes packet headers and trailers (AEAD tag).
4752                let length = payload_len + payload_offset + crypto_overhead;
4753                let qlog_raw_info = RawInfo {
4754                    length: Some(length as u64),
4755                    payload_length: Some(payload_len as u64),
4756                    data: None,
4757                };
4758
4759                let send_at_time =
4760                    now.duration_since(q.start_time()).as_secs_f32() * 1000.0;
4761
4762                let ev_data =
4763                    EventData::PacketSent(qlog::events::quic::PacketSent {
4764                        header,
4765                        frames: Some(qlog_frames),
4766                        raw: Some(qlog_raw_info),
4767                        send_at_time: Some(send_at_time),
4768                        ..Default::default()
4769                    });
4770
4771                q.add_event_data_with_instant(ev_data, now).ok();
4772            }
4773        });
4774
4775        let aead = match pkt_space.crypto_seal {
4776            Some(ref v) => v,
4777            None => return Err(Error::InvalidState),
4778        };
4779
4780        let written = packet::encrypt_pkt(
4781            &mut b,
4782            pn,
4783            pn_len,
4784            payload_len,
4785            payload_offset,
4786            None,
4787            aead,
4788        )?;
4789
4790        let sent_pkt_has_data = if path.recovery.gcongestion_enabled() {
4791            has_data || dgram_emitted
4792        } else {
4793            has_data
4794        };
4795
4796        let sent_pkt = recovery::Sent {
4797            pkt_num: pn,
4798            frames,
4799            time_sent: now,
4800            time_acked: None,
4801            time_lost: None,
4802            size: if ack_eliciting { written } else { 0 },
4803            ack_eliciting,
4804            in_flight,
4805            delivered: 0,
4806            delivered_time: now,
4807            first_sent_time: now,
4808            is_app_limited: false,
4809            tx_in_flight: 0,
4810            lost: 0,
4811            has_data: sent_pkt_has_data,
4812            pmtud: pmtud_probe,
4813        };
4814
4815        if in_flight && is_app_limited {
4816            path.recovery.delivery_rate_update_app_limited(true);
4817        }
4818
4819        self.next_pkt_num += 1;
4820
4821        let handshake_status = recovery::HandshakeStatus {
4822            has_handshake_keys: self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Handshake]
4823                .has_keys(),
4824            peer_verified_address: self.peer_verified_initial_address,
4825            completed: self.handshake_completed,
4826        };
4827
4828        path.recovery.on_packet_sent(
4829            sent_pkt,
4830            epoch,
4831            handshake_status,
4832            now,
4833            &self.trace_id,
4834        );
4835
4836        qlog_with_type!(QLOG_METRICS, self.qlog, q, {
4837            if let Some(ev_data) = path.recovery.maybe_qlog() {
4838                q.add_event_data_with_instant(ev_data, now).ok();
4839            }
4840        });
4841
4842        // Record sent packet size if we probe the path.
4843        if let Some(data) = challenge_data {
4844            path.add_challenge_sent(data, written, now);
4845        }
4846
4847        self.sent_count += 1;
4848        self.sent_bytes += written as u64;
4849        path.sent_count += 1;
4850        path.sent_bytes += written as u64;
4851
4852        if self.dgram_send_queue.byte_size() > path.recovery.cwnd_available() {
4853            path.recovery.update_app_limited(false);
4854        }
4855
4856        path.max_send_bytes = path.max_send_bytes.saturating_sub(written);
4857
4858        // On the client, drop initial state after sending an Handshake packet.
4859        if !self.is_server && hdr_ty == packet::Type::Handshake {
4860            self.drop_epoch_state(packet::Epoch::Initial, now);
4861        }
4862
4863        // (Re)start the idle timer if we are sending the first ack-eliciting
4864        // packet since last receiving a packet.
4865        if ack_eliciting && !self.ack_eliciting_sent {
4866            if let Some(idle_timeout) = self.idle_timeout() {
4867                self.idle_timer = Some(now + idle_timeout);
4868            }
4869        }
4870
4871        if ack_eliciting {
4872            self.ack_eliciting_sent = true;
4873        }
4874
4875        let active_path = self.paths.get_active_mut()?;
4876        if active_path.pmtud.is_enabled() {
4877            active_path
4878                .recovery
4879                .pmtud_update_max_datagram_size(active_path.pmtud.get_current());
4880        }
4881
4882        Ok((pkt_type, written))
4883    }
4884
4885    /// Returns the desired send time for the next packet.
4886    #[inline]
4887    pub fn get_next_release_time(&self) -> Option<ReleaseDecision> {
4888        Some(
4889            self.paths
4890                .get_active()
4891                .ok()?
4892                .recovery
4893                .get_next_release_time(),
4894        )
4895    }
4896
4897    /// Returns whether gcongestion is enabled.
4898    #[inline]
4899    pub fn gcongestion_enabled(&self) -> Option<bool> {
4900        Some(self.paths.get_active().ok()?.recovery.gcongestion_enabled())
4901    }
4902
4903    /// Returns the maximum pacing into the future.
4904    ///
4905    /// Equals 1/8 of the smoothed RTT, but not greater than 5ms.
4906    pub fn max_release_into_future(&self) -> time::Duration {
4907        self.paths
4908            .get_active()
4909            .map(|p| p.recovery.rtt().mul_f64(0.125))
4910            .unwrap_or(time::Duration::from_millis(1))
4911            .min(Duration::from_millis(5))
4912    }
4913
4914    /// Returns whether pacing is enabled.
4915    #[inline]
4916    pub fn pacing_enabled(&self) -> bool {
4917        self.recovery_config.pacing
4918    }
4919
4920    /// Returns the size of the send quantum, in bytes.
4921    ///
4922    /// This represents the maximum size of a packet burst as determined by the
4923    /// congestion control algorithm in use.
4924    ///
4925    /// Applications can, for example, use it in conjunction with segmentation
4926    /// offloading mechanisms as the maximum limit for outgoing aggregates of
4927    /// multiple packets.
4928    #[inline]
4929    pub fn send_quantum(&self) -> usize {
4930        match self.paths.get_active() {
4931            Ok(p) => p.recovery.send_quantum(),
4932            _ => 0,
4933        }
4934    }
4935
4936    /// Returns the size of the send quantum over the given 4-tuple, in bytes.
4937    ///
4938    /// This represents the maximum size of a packet burst as determined by the
4939    /// congestion control algorithm in use.
4940    ///
4941    /// Applications can, for example, use it in conjunction with segmentation
4942    /// offloading mechanisms as the maximum limit for outgoing aggregates of
4943    /// multiple packets.
4944    ///
4945    /// If the (`local_addr`, peer_addr`) 4-tuple relates to a non-existing
4946    /// path, this method returns 0.
4947    pub fn send_quantum_on_path(
4948        &self, local_addr: SocketAddr, peer_addr: SocketAddr,
4949    ) -> usize {
4950        self.paths
4951            .path_id_from_addrs(&(local_addr, peer_addr))
4952            .and_then(|pid| self.paths.get(pid).ok())
4953            .map(|path| path.recovery.send_quantum())
4954            .unwrap_or(0)
4955    }
4956
4957    /// Reads contiguous data from a stream into the provided slice.
4958    ///
4959    /// The slice must be sized by the caller and will be populated up to its
4960    /// capacity.
4961    ///
4962    /// On success the amount of bytes read and a flag indicating the fin state
4963    /// is returned as a tuple, or [`Done`] if there is no data to read.
4964    ///
4965    /// Reading data from a stream may trigger queueing of control messages
4966    /// (e.g. MAX_STREAM_DATA). [`send()`] should be called after reading.
4967    ///
4968    /// [`Done`]: enum.Error.html#variant.Done
4969    /// [`send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.send
4970    ///
4971    /// ## Examples:
4972    ///
4973    /// ```no_run
4974    /// # let mut buf = [0; 512];
4975    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
4976    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
4977    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
4978    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
4979    /// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
4980    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
4981    /// # let stream_id = 0;
4982    /// while let Ok((read, fin)) = conn.stream_recv(stream_id, &mut buf) {
4983    ///     println!("Got {} bytes on stream {}", read, stream_id);
4984    /// }
4985    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
4986    /// ```
4987    pub fn stream_recv(
4988        &mut self, stream_id: u64, out: &mut [u8],
4989    ) -> Result<(usize, bool)> {
4990        // We can't read on our own unidirectional streams.
4991        if !stream::is_bidi(stream_id) &&
4992            stream::is_local(stream_id, self.is_server)
4993        {
4994            return Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(stream_id));
4995        }
4996
4997        let stream = self
4998            .streams
4999            .get_mut(stream_id)
5000            .ok_or(Error::InvalidStreamState(stream_id))?;
5001
5002        if !stream.is_readable() {
5003            return Err(Error::Done);
5004        }
5005
5006        let local = stream.local;
5007        let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
5008
5009        #[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
5010        let offset = stream.recv.off_front();
5011
5012        let (read, fin) = match stream.recv.emit(out) {
5013            Ok(v) => v,
5014
5015            Err(e) => {
5016                // Collect the stream if it is now complete. This can happen if
5017                // we got a `StreamReset` error which will now be propagated to
5018                // the application, so we don't need to keep the stream's state
5019                // anymore.
5020                if stream.is_complete() {
5021                    self.streams.collect(stream_id, local);
5022                }
5023
5024                self.streams.remove_readable(&priority_key);
5025                return Err(e);
5026            },
5027        };
5028
5029        self.flow_control.add_consumed(read as u64);
5030
5031        let readable = stream.is_readable();
5032
5033        let complete = stream.is_complete();
5034
5035        if stream.recv.almost_full() {
5036            self.streams.insert_almost_full(stream_id);
5037        }
5038
5039        if !readable {
5040            self.streams.remove_readable(&priority_key);
5041        }
5042
5043        if complete {
5044            self.streams.collect(stream_id, local);
5045        }
5046
5047        qlog_with_type!(QLOG_DATA_MV, self.qlog, q, {
5048            let ev_data = EventData::DataMoved(qlog::events::quic::DataMoved {
5049                stream_id: Some(stream_id),
5050                offset: Some(offset),
5051                length: Some(read as u64),
5052                from: Some(DataRecipient::Transport),
5053                to: Some(DataRecipient::Application),
5054                ..Default::default()
5055            });
5056
5057            let now = time::Instant::now();
5058            q.add_event_data_with_instant(ev_data, now).ok();
5059        });
5060
5061        if self.should_update_max_data() {
5062            self.almost_full = true;
5063        }
5064
5065        if priority_key.incremental && readable {
5066            // Shuffle the incremental stream to the back of the queue.
5067            self.streams.remove_readable(&priority_key);
5068            self.streams.insert_readable(&priority_key);
5069        }
5070
5071        Ok((read, fin))
5072    }
5073
5074    /// Writes data to a stream.
5075    ///
5076    /// On success the number of bytes written is returned, or [`Done`] if no
5077    /// data was written (e.g. because the stream has no capacity).
5078    ///
5079    /// Applications can provide a 0-length buffer with the fin flag set to
5080    /// true. This will lead to a 0-length FIN STREAM frame being sent at the
5081    /// latest offset. The `Ok(0)` value is only returned when the application
5082    /// provided a 0-length buffer.
5083    ///
5084    /// In addition, if the peer has signalled that it doesn't want to receive
5085    /// any more data from this stream by sending the `STOP_SENDING` frame, the
5086    /// [`StreamStopped`] error will be returned instead of any data.
5087    ///
5088    /// Note that in order to avoid buffering an infinite amount of data in the
5089    /// stream's send buffer, streams are only allowed to buffer outgoing data
5090    /// up to the amount that the peer allows it to send (that is, up to the
5091    /// stream's outgoing flow control capacity).
5092    ///
5093    /// This means that the number of written bytes returned can be lower than
5094    /// the length of the input buffer when the stream doesn't have enough
5095    /// capacity for the operation to complete. The application should retry the
5096    /// operation once the stream is reported as writable again.
5097    ///
5098    /// Applications should call this method only after the handshake is
5099    /// completed (whenever [`is_established()`] returns `true`) or during
5100    /// early data if enabled (whenever [`is_in_early_data()`] returns `true`).
5101    ///
5102    /// [`Done`]: enum.Error.html#variant.Done
5103    /// [`StreamStopped`]: enum.Error.html#variant.StreamStopped
5104    /// [`is_established()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.is_established
5105    /// [`is_in_early_data()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.is_in_early_data
5106    ///
5107    /// ## Examples:
5108    ///
5109    /// ```no_run
5110    /// # let mut buf = [0; 512];
5111    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
5112    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
5113    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
5114    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
5115    /// # let local = "127.0.0.1:4321".parse().unwrap();
5116    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
5117    /// # let stream_id = 0;
5118    /// conn.stream_send(stream_id, b"hello", true)?;
5119    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
5120    /// ```
5121    pub fn stream_send(
5122        &mut self, stream_id: u64, buf: &[u8], fin: bool,
5123    ) -> Result<usize> {
5124        // We can't write on the peer's unidirectional streams.
5125        if !stream::is_bidi(stream_id) &&
5126            !stream::is_local(stream_id, self.is_server)
5127        {
5128            return Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(stream_id));
5129        }
5130
5131        // Mark the connection as blocked if the connection-level flow control
5132        // limit doesn't let us buffer all the data.
5133        //
5134        // Note that this is separate from "send capacity" as that also takes
5135        // congestion control into consideration.
5136        if self.max_tx_data - self.tx_data < buf.len() as u64 {
5137            self.blocked_limit = Some(self.max_tx_data);
5138        }
5139
5140        let cap = self.tx_cap;
5141
5142        // Get existing stream or create a new one.
5143        let stream = self.get_or_create_stream(stream_id, true)?;
5144
5145        #[cfg(feature = "qlog")]
5146        let offset = stream.send.off_back();
5147
5148        let was_writable = stream.is_writable();
5149
5150        let was_flushable = stream.is_flushable();
5151
5152        let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
5153
5154        // Truncate the input buffer based on the connection's send capacity if
5155        // necessary.
5156        //
5157        // When the cap is zero, the method returns Ok(0) *only* when the passed
5158        // buffer is empty. We return Error::Done otherwise.
5159        if cap == 0 && !buf.is_empty() {
5160            if was_writable {
5161                // When `stream_writable_next()` returns a stream, the writable
5162                // mark is removed, but because the stream is blocked by the
5163                // connection-level send capacity it won't be marked as writable
5164                // again once the capacity increases.
5165                //
5166                // Since the stream is writable already, mark it here instead.
5167                self.streams.insert_writable(&priority_key);
5168            }
5169
5170            return Err(Error::Done);
5171        }
5172
5173        let (buf, fin, blocked_by_cap) = if cap < buf.len() {
5174            (&buf[..cap], false, true)
5175        } else {
5176            (buf, fin, false)
5177        };
5178
5179        let sent = match stream.send.write(buf, fin) {
5180            Ok(v) => v,
5181
5182            Err(e) => {
5183                self.streams.remove_writable(&priority_key);
5184                return Err(e);
5185            },
5186        };
5187
5188        let incremental = stream.incremental;
5189        let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
5190
5191        let flushable = stream.is_flushable();
5192
5193        let writable = stream.is_writable();
5194
5195        let empty_fin = buf.is_empty() && fin;
5196
5197        if sent < buf.len() {
5198            let max_off = stream.send.max_off();
5199
5200            if stream.send.blocked_at() != Some(max_off) {
5201                stream.send.update_blocked_at(Some(max_off));
5202                self.streams.insert_blocked(stream_id, max_off);
5203            }
5204        } else {
5205            stream.send.update_blocked_at(None);
5206            self.streams.remove_blocked(stream_id);
5207        }
5208
5209        // If the stream is now flushable push it to the flushable queue, but
5210        // only if it wasn't already queued.
5211        //
5212        // Consider the stream flushable also when we are sending a zero-length
5213        // frame that has the fin flag set.
5214        if (flushable || empty_fin) && !was_flushable {
5215            self.streams.insert_flushable(&priority_key);
5216        }
5217
5218        if !writable {
5219            self.streams.remove_writable(&priority_key);
5220        } else if was_writable && blocked_by_cap {
5221            // When `stream_writable_next()` returns a stream, the writable
5222            // mark is removed, but because the stream is blocked by the
5223            // connection-level send capacity it won't be marked as writable
5224            // again once the capacity increases.
5225            //
5226            // Since the stream is writable already, mark it here instead.
5227            self.streams.insert_writable(&priority_key);
5228        }
5229
5230        self.tx_cap -= sent;
5231
5232        self.tx_data += sent as u64;
5233
5234        self.tx_buffered += sent;
5235
5236        qlog_with_type!(QLOG_DATA_MV, self.qlog, q, {
5237            let ev_data = EventData::DataMoved(qlog::events::quic::DataMoved {
5238                stream_id: Some(stream_id),
5239                offset: Some(offset),
5240                length: Some(sent as u64),
5241                from: Some(DataRecipient::Application),
5242                to: Some(DataRecipient::Transport),
5243                ..Default::default()
5244            });
5245
5246            let now = time::Instant::now();
5247            q.add_event_data_with_instant(ev_data, now).ok();
5248        });
5249
5250        if sent == 0 && !buf.is_empty() {
5251            return Err(Error::Done);
5252        }
5253
5254        if incremental && writable {
5255            // Shuffle the incremental stream to the back of the queue.
5256            self.streams.remove_writable(&priority_key);
5257            self.streams.insert_writable(&priority_key);
5258        }
5259
5260        Ok(sent)
5261    }
5262
5263    /// Sets the priority for a stream.
5264    ///
5265    /// A stream's priority determines the order in which stream data is sent
5266    /// on the wire (streams with lower priority are sent first). Streams are
5267    /// created with a default priority of `127`.
5268    ///
5269    /// The target stream is created if it did not exist before calling this
5270    /// method.
5271    pub fn stream_priority(
5272        &mut self, stream_id: u64, urgency: u8, incremental: bool,
5273    ) -> Result<()> {
5274        // Get existing stream or create a new one, but if the stream
5275        // has already been closed and collected, ignore the prioritization.
5276        let stream = match self.get_or_create_stream(stream_id, true) {
5277            Ok(v) => v,
5278
5279            Err(Error::Done) => return Ok(()),
5280
5281            Err(e) => return Err(e),
5282        };
5283
5284        if stream.urgency == urgency && stream.incremental == incremental {
5285            return Ok(());
5286        }
5287
5288        stream.urgency = urgency;
5289        stream.incremental = incremental;
5290
5291        let new_priority_key = Arc::new(StreamPriorityKey {
5292            urgency: stream.urgency,
5293            incremental: stream.incremental,
5294            id: stream_id,
5295            ..Default::default()
5296        });
5297
5298        let old_priority_key =
5299            std::mem::replace(&mut stream.priority_key, new_priority_key.clone());
5300
5301        self.streams
5302            .update_priority(&old_priority_key, &new_priority_key);
5303
5304        Ok(())
5305    }
5306
5307    /// Shuts down reading or writing from/to the specified stream.
5308    ///
5309    /// When the `direction` argument is set to [`Shutdown::Read`], outstanding
5310    /// data in the stream's receive buffer is dropped, and no additional data
5311    /// is added to it. Data received after calling this method is still
5312    /// validated and acked but not stored, and [`stream_recv()`] will not
5313    /// return it to the application. In addition, a `STOP_SENDING` frame will
5314    /// be sent to the peer to signal it to stop sending data.
5315    ///
5316    /// When the `direction` argument is set to [`Shutdown::Write`], outstanding
5317    /// data in the stream's send buffer is dropped, and no additional data is
5318    /// added to it. Data passed to [`stream_send()`] after calling this method
5319    /// will be ignored. In addition, a `RESET_STREAM` frame will be sent to the
5320    /// peer to signal the reset.
5321    ///
5322    /// Locally-initiated unidirectional streams can only be closed in the
5323    /// [`Shutdown::Write`] direction. Remotely-initiated unidirectional streams
5324    /// can only be closed in the [`Shutdown::Read`] direction. Using an
5325    /// incorrect direction will return [`InvalidStreamState`].
5326    ///
5327    /// [`Shutdown::Read`]: enum.Shutdown.html#variant.Read
5328    /// [`Shutdown::Write`]: enum.Shutdown.html#variant.Write
5329    /// [`stream_recv()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_recv
5330    /// [`stream_send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_send
5331    /// [`InvalidStreamState`]: enum.Error.html#variant.InvalidStreamState
5332    pub fn stream_shutdown(
5333        &mut self, stream_id: u64, direction: Shutdown, err: u64,
5334    ) -> Result<()> {
5335        // Don't try to stop a local unidirectional stream.
5336        if direction == Shutdown::Read &&
5337            stream::is_local(stream_id, self.is_server) &&
5338            !stream::is_bidi(stream_id)
5339        {
5340            return Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(stream_id));
5341        }
5342
5343        // Don't try to reset a remote unidirectional stream.
5344        if direction == Shutdown::Write &&
5345            !stream::is_local(stream_id, self.is_server) &&
5346            !stream::is_bidi(stream_id)
5347        {
5348            return Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(stream_id));
5349        }
5350
5351        // Get existing stream.
5352        let stream = self.streams.get_mut(stream_id).ok_or(Error::Done)?;
5353
5354        let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
5355
5356        match direction {
5357            Shutdown::Read => {
5358                stream.recv.shutdown()?;
5359
5360                if !stream.recv.is_fin() {
5361                    self.streams.insert_stopped(stream_id, err);
5362                }
5363
5364                // Once shutdown, the stream is guaranteed to be non-readable.
5365                self.streams.remove_readable(&priority_key);
5366
5367                self.stopped_stream_local_count =
5368                    self.stopped_stream_local_count.saturating_add(1);
5369            },
5370
5371            Shutdown::Write => {
5372                let (final_size, unsent) = stream.send.shutdown()?;
5373
5374                // Claw back some flow control allowance from data that was
5375                // buffered but not actually sent before the stream was reset.
5376                self.tx_data = self.tx_data.saturating_sub(unsent);
5377
5378                self.tx_buffered =
5379                    self.tx_buffered.saturating_sub(unsent as usize);
5380
5381                // Update send capacity.
5382                self.update_tx_cap();
5383
5384                self.streams.insert_reset(stream_id, err, final_size);
5385
5386                // Once shutdown, the stream is guaranteed to be non-writable.
5387                self.streams.remove_writable(&priority_key);
5388
5389                self.reset_stream_local_count =
5390                    self.reset_stream_local_count.saturating_add(1);
5391            },
5392        }
5393
5394        Ok(())
5395    }
5396
5397    /// Returns the stream's send capacity in bytes.
5398    ///
5399    /// If the specified stream doesn't exist (including when it has already
5400    /// been completed and closed), the [`InvalidStreamState`] error will be
5401    /// returned.
5402    ///
5403    /// In addition, if the peer has signalled that it doesn't want to receive
5404    /// any more data from this stream by sending the `STOP_SENDING` frame, the
5405    /// [`StreamStopped`] error will be returned.
5406    ///
5407    /// [`InvalidStreamState`]: enum.Error.html#variant.InvalidStreamState
5408    /// [`StreamStopped`]: enum.Error.html#variant.StreamStopped
5409    #[inline]
5410    pub fn stream_capacity(&self, stream_id: u64) -> Result<usize> {
5411        if let Some(stream) = self.streams.get(stream_id) {
5412            let cap = cmp::min(self.tx_cap, stream.send.cap()?);
5413            return Ok(cap);
5414        };
5415
5416        Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(stream_id))
5417    }
5418
5419    /// Returns the next stream that has data to read.
5420    ///
5421    /// Note that once returned by this method, a stream ID will not be returned
5422    /// again until it is "re-armed".
5423    ///
5424    /// The application will need to read all of the pending data on the stream,
5425    /// and new data has to be received before the stream is reported again.
5426    ///
5427    /// This is unlike the [`readable()`] method, that returns the same list of
5428    /// readable streams when called multiple times in succession.
5429    ///
5430    /// [`readable()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.readable
5431    pub fn stream_readable_next(&mut self) -> Option<u64> {
5432        let priority_key = self.streams.readable.front().clone_pointer()?;
5433
5434        self.streams.remove_readable(&priority_key);
5435
5436        Some(priority_key.id)
5437    }
5438
5439    /// Returns true if the stream has data that can be read.
5440    pub fn stream_readable(&self, stream_id: u64) -> bool {
5441        let stream = match self.streams.get(stream_id) {
5442            Some(v) => v,
5443
5444            None => return false,
5445        };
5446
5447        stream.is_readable()
5448    }
5449
5450    /// Returns the next stream that can be written to.
5451    ///
5452    /// Note that once returned by this method, a stream ID will not be returned
5453    /// again until it is "re-armed".
5454    ///
5455    /// This is unlike the [`writable()`] method, that returns the same list of
5456    /// writable streams when called multiple times in succession. It is not
5457    /// advised to use both `stream_writable_next()` and [`writable()`] on the
5458    /// same connection, as it may lead to unexpected results.
5459    ///
5460    /// The [`stream_writable()`] method can also be used to fine-tune when a
5461    /// stream is reported as writable again.
5462    ///
5463    /// [`stream_writable()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_writable
5464    /// [`writable()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.writable
5465    pub fn stream_writable_next(&mut self) -> Option<u64> {
5466        // If there is not enough connection-level send capacity, none of the
5467        // streams are writable.
5468        if self.tx_cap == 0 {
5469            return None;
5470        }
5471
5472        let mut cursor = self.streams.writable.front();
5473
5474        while let Some(priority_key) = cursor.clone_pointer() {
5475            if let Some(stream) = self.streams.get(priority_key.id) {
5476                let cap = match stream.send.cap() {
5477                    Ok(v) => v,
5478
5479                    // Return the stream to the application immediately if it's
5480                    // stopped.
5481                    Err(_) =>
5482                        return {
5483                            self.streams.remove_writable(&priority_key);
5484
5485                            Some(priority_key.id)
5486                        },
5487                };
5488
5489                if cmp::min(self.tx_cap, cap) >= stream.send_lowat {
5490                    self.streams.remove_writable(&priority_key);
5491                    return Some(priority_key.id);
5492                }
5493            }
5494
5495            cursor.move_next();
5496        }
5497
5498        None
5499    }
5500
5501    /// Returns true if the stream has enough send capacity.
5502    ///
5503    /// When `len` more bytes can be buffered into the given stream's send
5504    /// buffer, `true` will be returned, `false` otherwise.
5505    ///
5506    /// In the latter case, if the additional data can't be buffered due to
5507    /// flow control limits, the peer will also be notified, and a "low send
5508    /// watermark" will be set for the stream, such that it is not going to be
5509    /// reported as writable again by [`stream_writable_next()`] until its send
5510    /// capacity reaches `len`.
5511    ///
5512    /// If the specified stream doesn't exist (including when it has already
5513    /// been completed and closed), the [`InvalidStreamState`] error will be
5514    /// returned.
5515    ///
5516    /// In addition, if the peer has signalled that it doesn't want to receive
5517    /// any more data from this stream by sending the `STOP_SENDING` frame, the
5518    /// [`StreamStopped`] error will be returned.
5519    ///
5520    /// [`stream_writable_next()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_writable_next
5521    /// [`InvalidStreamState`]: enum.Error.html#variant.InvalidStreamState
5522    /// [`StreamStopped`]: enum.Error.html#variant.StreamStopped
5523    #[inline]
5524    pub fn stream_writable(
5525        &mut self, stream_id: u64, len: usize,
5526    ) -> Result<bool> {
5527        if self.stream_capacity(stream_id)? >= len {
5528            return Ok(true);
5529        }
5530
5531        let stream = match self.streams.get_mut(stream_id) {
5532            Some(v) => v,
5533
5534            None => return Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(stream_id)),
5535        };
5536
5537        stream.send_lowat = cmp::max(1, len);
5538
5539        let is_writable = stream.is_writable();
5540
5541        let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
5542
5543        if self.max_tx_data - self.tx_data < len as u64 {
5544            self.blocked_limit = Some(self.max_tx_data);
5545        }
5546
5547        if stream.send.cap()? < len {
5548            let max_off = stream.send.max_off();
5549            if stream.send.blocked_at() != Some(max_off) {
5550                stream.send.update_blocked_at(Some(max_off));
5551                self.streams.insert_blocked(stream_id, max_off);
5552            }
5553        } else if is_writable {
5554            // When `stream_writable_next()` returns a stream, the writable
5555            // mark is removed, but because the stream is blocked by the
5556            // connection-level send capacity it won't be marked as writable
5557            // again once the capacity increases.
5558            //
5559            // Since the stream is writable already, mark it here instead.
5560            self.streams.insert_writable(&priority_key);
5561        }
5562
5563        Ok(false)
5564    }
5565
5566    /// Returns true if all the data has been read from the specified stream.
5567    ///
5568    /// This instructs the application that all the data received from the
5569    /// peer on the stream has been read, and there won't be anymore in the
5570    /// future.
5571    ///
5572    /// Basically this returns true when the peer either set the `fin` flag
5573    /// for the stream, or sent `RESET_STREAM`.
5574    #[inline]
5575    pub fn stream_finished(&self, stream_id: u64) -> bool {
5576        let stream = match self.streams.get(stream_id) {
5577            Some(v) => v,
5578
5579            None => return true,
5580        };
5581
5582        stream.recv.is_fin()
5583    }
5584
5585    /// Returns the number of bidirectional streams that can be created
5586    /// before the peer's stream count limit is reached.
5587    ///
5588    /// This can be useful to know if it's possible to create a bidirectional
5589    /// stream without trying it first.
5590    #[inline]
5591    pub fn peer_streams_left_bidi(&self) -> u64 {
5592        self.streams.peer_streams_left_bidi()
5593    }
5594
5595    /// Returns the number of unidirectional streams that can be created
5596    /// before the peer's stream count limit is reached.
5597    ///
5598    /// This can be useful to know if it's possible to create a unidirectional
5599    /// stream without trying it first.
5600    #[inline]
5601    pub fn peer_streams_left_uni(&self) -> u64 {
5602        self.streams.peer_streams_left_uni()
5603    }
5604
5605    /// Returns an iterator over streams that have outstanding data to read.
5606    ///
5607    /// Note that the iterator will only include streams that were readable at
5608    /// the time the iterator itself was created (i.e. when `readable()` was
5609    /// called). To account for newly readable streams, the iterator needs to
5610    /// be created again.
5611    ///
5612    /// ## Examples:
5613    ///
5614    /// ```no_run
5615    /// # let mut buf = [0; 512];
5616    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
5617    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
5618    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
5619    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
5620    /// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
5621    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
5622    /// // Iterate over readable streams.
5623    /// for stream_id in conn.readable() {
5624    ///     // Stream is readable, read until there's no more data.
5625    ///     while let Ok((read, fin)) = conn.stream_recv(stream_id, &mut buf) {
5626    ///         println!("Got {} bytes on stream {}", read, stream_id);
5627    ///     }
5628    /// }
5629    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
5630    /// ```
5631    #[inline]
5632    pub fn readable(&self) -> StreamIter {
5633        self.streams.readable()
5634    }
5635
5636    /// Returns an iterator over streams that can be written in priority order.
5637    ///
5638    /// The priority order is based on RFC 9218 scheduling recommendations.
5639    /// Stream priority can be controlled using [`stream_priority()`]. In order
5640    /// to support fairness requirements, each time this method is called,
5641    /// internal state is updated. Therefore the iterator ordering can change
5642    /// between calls, even if no streams were added or removed.
5643    ///
5644    /// A "writable" stream is a stream that has enough flow control capacity to
5645    /// send data to the peer. To avoid buffering an infinite amount of data,
5646    /// streams are only allowed to buffer outgoing data up to the amount that
5647    /// the peer allows to send.
5648    ///
5649    /// Note that the iterator will only include streams that were writable at
5650    /// the time the iterator itself was created (i.e. when `writable()` was
5651    /// called). To account for newly writable streams, the iterator needs to be
5652    /// created again.
5653    ///
5654    /// ## Examples:
5655    ///
5656    /// ```no_run
5657    /// # let mut buf = [0; 512];
5658    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
5659    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
5660    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
5661    /// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
5662    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
5663    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
5664    /// // Iterate over writable streams.
5665    /// for stream_id in conn.writable() {
5666    ///     // Stream is writable, write some data.
5667    ///     if let Ok(written) = conn.stream_send(stream_id, &buf, false) {
5668    ///         println!("Written {} bytes on stream {}", written, stream_id);
5669    ///     }
5670    /// }
5671    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
5672    /// ```
5673    /// [`stream_priority()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.stream_priority
5674    #[inline]
5675    pub fn writable(&self) -> StreamIter {
5676        // If there is not enough connection-level send capacity, none of the
5677        // streams are writable, so return an empty iterator.
5678        if self.tx_cap == 0 {
5679            return StreamIter::default();
5680        }
5681
5682        self.streams.writable()
5683    }
5684
5685    /// Returns the maximum possible size of egress UDP payloads.
5686    ///
5687    /// This is the maximum size of UDP payloads that can be sent, and depends
5688    /// on both the configured maximum send payload size of the local endpoint
5689    /// (as configured with [`set_max_send_udp_payload_size()`]), as well as
5690    /// the transport parameter advertised by the remote peer.
5691    ///
5692    /// Note that this value can change during the lifetime of the connection,
5693    /// but should remain stable across consecutive calls to [`send()`].
5694    ///
5695    /// [`set_max_send_udp_payload_size()`]:
5696    ///     struct.Config.html#method.set_max_send_udp_payload_size
5697    /// [`send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.send
5698    pub fn max_send_udp_payload_size(&self) -> usize {
5699        let max_datagram_size = self
5700            .paths
5701            .get_active()
5702            .ok()
5703            .map(|p| p.recovery.max_datagram_size());
5704
5705        if let Some(max_datagram_size) = max_datagram_size {
5706            if self.is_established() {
5707                // We cap the maximum packet size to 16KB or so, so that it can be
5708                // always encoded with a 2-byte varint.
5709                return cmp::min(16383, max_datagram_size);
5710            }
5711        }
5712
5713        // Allow for 1200 bytes (minimum QUIC packet size) during the
5714        // handshake.
5715        MIN_CLIENT_INITIAL_LEN
5716    }
5717
5718    /// Schedule an ack-eliciting packet on the active path.
5719    ///
5720    /// QUIC packets might not contain ack-eliciting frames during normal
5721    /// operating conditions. If the packet would already contain
5722    /// ack-eliciting frames, this method does not change any behavior.
5723    /// However, if the packet would not ordinarily contain ack-eliciting
5724    /// frames, this method ensures that a PING frame sent.
5725    ///
5726    /// Calling this method multiple times before [`send()`] has no effect.
5727    ///
5728    /// [`send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.send
5729    pub fn send_ack_eliciting(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
5730        if self.is_closed() || self.is_draining() {
5731            return Ok(());
5732        }
5733        self.paths.get_active_mut()?.needs_ack_eliciting = true;
5734        Ok(())
5735    }
5736
5737    /// Schedule an ack-eliciting packet on the specified path.
5738    ///
5739    /// See [`send_ack_eliciting()`] for more detail. [`InvalidState`] is
5740    /// returned if there is no record of the path.
5741    ///
5742    /// [`send_ack_eliciting()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.send_ack_eliciting
5743    /// [`InvalidState`]: enum.Error.html#variant.InvalidState
5744    pub fn send_ack_eliciting_on_path(
5745        &mut self, local: SocketAddr, peer: SocketAddr,
5746    ) -> Result<()> {
5747        if self.is_closed() || self.is_draining() {
5748            return Ok(());
5749        }
5750        let path_id = self
5751            .paths
5752            .path_id_from_addrs(&(local, peer))
5753            .ok_or(Error::InvalidState)?;
5754        self.paths.get_mut(path_id)?.needs_ack_eliciting = true;
5755        Ok(())
5756    }
5757
5758    /// Reads the first received DATAGRAM.
5759    ///
5760    /// On success the DATAGRAM's data is returned along with its size.
5761    ///
5762    /// [`Done`] is returned if there is no data to read.
5763    ///
5764    /// [`BufferTooShort`] is returned if the provided buffer is too small for
5765    /// the DATAGRAM.
5766    ///
5767    /// [`Done`]: enum.Error.html#variant.Done
5768    /// [`BufferTooShort`]: enum.Error.html#variant.BufferTooShort
5769    ///
5770    /// ## Examples:
5771    ///
5772    /// ```no_run
5773    /// # let mut buf = [0; 512];
5774    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
5775    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
5776    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
5777    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
5778    /// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
5779    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
5780    /// let mut dgram_buf = [0; 512];
5781    /// while let Ok((len)) = conn.dgram_recv(&mut dgram_buf) {
5782    ///     println!("Got {} bytes of DATAGRAM", len);
5783    /// }
5784    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
5785    /// ```
5786    #[inline]
5787    pub fn dgram_recv(&mut self, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Result<usize> {
5788        match self.dgram_recv_queue.pop() {
5789            Some(d) => {
5790                if d.len() > buf.len() {
5791                    return Err(Error::BufferTooShort);
5792                }
5793
5794                buf[..d.len()].copy_from_slice(&d);
5795                Ok(d.len())
5796            },
5797
5798            None => Err(Error::Done),
5799        }
5800    }
5801
5802    /// Reads the first received DATAGRAM.
5803    ///
5804    /// This is the same as [`dgram_recv()`] but returns the DATAGRAM as a
5805    /// `Vec<u8>` instead of copying into the provided buffer.
5806    ///
5807    /// [`dgram_recv()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.dgram_recv
5808    #[inline]
5809    pub fn dgram_recv_vec(&mut self) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
5810        match self.dgram_recv_queue.pop() {
5811            Some(d) => Ok(d),
5812
5813            None => Err(Error::Done),
5814        }
5815    }
5816
5817    /// Reads the first received DATAGRAM without removing it from the queue.
5818    ///
5819    /// On success the DATAGRAM's data is returned along with the actual number
5820    /// of bytes peeked. The requested length cannot exceed the DATAGRAM's
5821    /// actual length.
5822    ///
5823    /// [`Done`] is returned if there is no data to read.
5824    ///
5825    /// [`BufferTooShort`] is returned if the provided buffer is smaller the
5826    /// number of bytes to peek.
5827    ///
5828    /// [`Done`]: enum.Error.html#variant.Done
5829    /// [`BufferTooShort`]: enum.Error.html#variant.BufferTooShort
5830    #[inline]
5831    pub fn dgram_recv_peek(&self, buf: &mut [u8], len: usize) -> Result<usize> {
5832        self.dgram_recv_queue.peek_front_bytes(buf, len)
5833    }
5834
5835    /// Returns the length of the first stored DATAGRAM.
5836    #[inline]
5837    pub fn dgram_recv_front_len(&self) -> Option<usize> {
5838        self.dgram_recv_queue.peek_front_len()
5839    }
5840
5841    /// Returns the number of items in the DATAGRAM receive queue.
5842    #[inline]
5843    pub fn dgram_recv_queue_len(&self) -> usize {
5844        self.dgram_recv_queue.len()
5845    }
5846
5847    /// Returns the total size of all items in the DATAGRAM receive queue.
5848    #[inline]
5849    pub fn dgram_recv_queue_byte_size(&self) -> usize {
5850        self.dgram_recv_queue.byte_size()
5851    }
5852
5853    /// Returns the number of items in the DATAGRAM send queue.
5854    #[inline]
5855    pub fn dgram_send_queue_len(&self) -> usize {
5856        self.dgram_send_queue.len()
5857    }
5858
5859    /// Returns the total size of all items in the DATAGRAM send queue.
5860    #[inline]
5861    pub fn dgram_send_queue_byte_size(&self) -> usize {
5862        self.dgram_send_queue.byte_size()
5863    }
5864
5865    /// Returns whether or not the DATAGRAM send queue is full.
5866    #[inline]
5867    pub fn is_dgram_send_queue_full(&self) -> bool {
5868        self.dgram_send_queue.is_full()
5869    }
5870
5871    /// Returns whether or not the DATAGRAM recv queue is full.
5872    #[inline]
5873    pub fn is_dgram_recv_queue_full(&self) -> bool {
5874        self.dgram_recv_queue.is_full()
5875    }
5876
5877    /// Sends data in a DATAGRAM frame.
5878    ///
5879    /// [`Done`] is returned if no data was written.
5880    /// [`InvalidState`] is returned if the peer does not support DATAGRAM.
5881    /// [`BufferTooShort`] is returned if the DATAGRAM frame length is larger
5882    /// than peer's supported DATAGRAM frame length. Use
5883    /// [`dgram_max_writable_len()`] to get the largest supported DATAGRAM
5884    /// frame length.
5885    ///
5886    /// Note that there is no flow control of DATAGRAM frames, so in order to
5887    /// avoid buffering an infinite amount of frames we apply an internal
5888    /// limit.
5889    ///
5890    /// [`Done`]: enum.Error.html#variant.Done
5891    /// [`InvalidState`]: enum.Error.html#variant.InvalidState
5892    /// [`BufferTooShort`]: enum.Error.html#variant.BufferTooShort
5893    /// [`dgram_max_writable_len()`]:
5894    /// struct.Connection.html#method.dgram_max_writable_len
5895    ///
5896    /// ## Examples:
5897    ///
5898    /// ```no_run
5899    /// # let mut buf = [0; 512];
5900    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
5901    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
5902    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
5903    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
5904    /// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
5905    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
5906    /// conn.dgram_send(b"hello")?;
5907    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
5908    /// ```
5909    pub fn dgram_send(&mut self, buf: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
5910        let max_payload_len = match self.dgram_max_writable_len() {
5911            Some(v) => v,
5912
5913            None => return Err(Error::InvalidState),
5914        };
5915
5916        if buf.len() > max_payload_len {
5917            return Err(Error::BufferTooShort);
5918        }
5919
5920        self.dgram_send_queue.push(buf.to_vec())?;
5921
5922        let active_path = self.paths.get_active_mut()?;
5923
5924        if self.dgram_send_queue.byte_size() >
5925            active_path.recovery.cwnd_available()
5926        {
5927            active_path.recovery.update_app_limited(false);
5928        }
5929
5930        Ok(())
5931    }
5932
5933    /// Sends data in a DATAGRAM frame.
5934    ///
5935    /// This is the same as [`dgram_send()`] but takes a `Vec<u8>` instead of
5936    /// a slice.
5937    ///
5938    /// [`dgram_send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.dgram_send
5939    pub fn dgram_send_vec(&mut self, buf: Vec<u8>) -> Result<()> {
5940        let max_payload_len = match self.dgram_max_writable_len() {
5941            Some(v) => v,
5942
5943            None => return Err(Error::InvalidState),
5944        };
5945
5946        if buf.len() > max_payload_len {
5947            return Err(Error::BufferTooShort);
5948        }
5949
5950        self.dgram_send_queue.push(buf)?;
5951
5952        let active_path = self.paths.get_active_mut()?;
5953
5954        if self.dgram_send_queue.byte_size() >
5955            active_path.recovery.cwnd_available()
5956        {
5957            active_path.recovery.update_app_limited(false);
5958        }
5959
5960        Ok(())
5961    }
5962
5963    /// Purges queued outgoing DATAGRAMs matching the predicate.
5964    ///
5965    /// In other words, remove all elements `e` such that `f(&e)` returns true.
5966    ///
5967    /// ## Examples:
5968    /// ```no_run
5969    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
5970    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
5971    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
5972    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
5973    /// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
5974    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
5975    /// conn.dgram_send(b"hello")?;
5976    /// conn.dgram_purge_outgoing(&|d: &[u8]| -> bool { d[0] == 0 });
5977    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
5978    /// ```
5979    #[inline]
5980    pub fn dgram_purge_outgoing<F: Fn(&[u8]) -> bool>(&mut self, f: F) {
5981        self.dgram_send_queue.purge(f);
5982    }
5983
5984    /// Returns the maximum DATAGRAM payload that can be sent.
5985    ///
5986    /// [`None`] is returned if the peer hasn't advertised a maximum DATAGRAM
5987    /// frame size.
5988    ///
5989    /// ## Examples:
5990    ///
5991    /// ```no_run
5992    /// # let mut buf = [0; 512];
5993    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
5994    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
5995    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
5996    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
5997    /// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
5998    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
5999    /// if let Some(payload_size) = conn.dgram_max_writable_len() {
6000    ///     if payload_size > 5 {
6001    ///         conn.dgram_send(b"hello")?;
6002    ///     }
6003    /// }
6004    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
6005    /// ```
6006    #[inline]
6007    pub fn dgram_max_writable_len(&self) -> Option<usize> {
6008        match self.peer_transport_params.max_datagram_frame_size {
6009            None => None,
6010            Some(peer_frame_len) => {
6011                let dcid = self.destination_id();
6012                // Start from the maximum packet size...
6013                let mut max_len = self.max_send_udp_payload_size();
6014                // ...subtract the Short packet header overhead...
6015                // (1 byte of pkt_len + len of dcid)
6016                max_len = max_len.saturating_sub(1 + dcid.len());
6017                // ...subtract the packet number (max len)...
6018                max_len = max_len.saturating_sub(packet::MAX_PKT_NUM_LEN);
6019                // ...subtract the crypto overhead...
6020                max_len = max_len.saturating_sub(
6021                    self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Application]
6022                        .crypto_overhead()?,
6023                );
6024                // ...clamp to what peer can support...
6025                max_len = cmp::min(peer_frame_len as usize, max_len);
6026                // ...subtract frame overhead, checked for underflow.
6027                // (1 byte of frame type + len of length )
6028                max_len.checked_sub(1 + frame::MAX_DGRAM_OVERHEAD)
6029            },
6030        }
6031    }
6032
6033    fn dgram_enabled(&self) -> bool {
6034        self.local_transport_params
6035            .max_datagram_frame_size
6036            .is_some()
6037    }
6038
6039    /// Returns when the next timeout event will occur.
6040    ///
6041    /// Once the timeout Instant has been reached, the [`on_timeout()`] method
6042    /// should be called. A timeout of `None` means that the timer should be
6043    /// disarmed.
6044    ///
6045    /// [`on_timeout()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.on_timeout
6046    pub fn timeout_instant(&self) -> Option<time::Instant> {
6047        if self.is_closed() {
6048            return None;
6049        }
6050
6051        if self.is_draining() {
6052            // Draining timer takes precedence over all other timers. If it is
6053            // set it means the connection is closing so there's no point in
6054            // processing the other timers.
6055            self.draining_timer
6056        } else {
6057            // Use the lowest timer value (i.e. "sooner") among idle and loss
6058            // detection timers. If they are both unset (i.e. `None`) then the
6059            // result is `None`, but if at least one of them is set then a
6060            // `Some(...)` value is returned.
6061            let path_timer = self
6062                .paths
6063                .iter()
6064                .filter_map(|(_, p)| p.recovery.loss_detection_timer())
6065                .min();
6066
