6067            let key_update_timer = self.pkt_num_spaces
6068                [packet::Epoch::Application]
6069                .key_update
6070                .as_ref()
6071                .map(|key_update| key_update.timer);
6072
6073            let timers = [self.idle_timer, path_timer, key_update_timer];
6074
6075            timers.iter().filter_map(|&x| x).min()
6076        }
6077    }
6078
6079    /// Returns the amount of time until the next timeout event.
6080    ///
6081    /// Once the given duration has elapsed, the [`on_timeout()`] method should
6082    /// be called. A timeout of `None` means that the timer should be disarmed.
6083    ///
6084    /// [`on_timeout()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.on_timeout
6085    pub fn timeout(&self) -> Option<time::Duration> {
6086        self.timeout_instant().map(|timeout| {
6087            let now = time::Instant::now();
6088
6089            if timeout <= now {
6090                time::Duration::ZERO
6091            } else {
6092                timeout.duration_since(now)
6093            }
6094        })
6095    }
6096
6097    /// Processes a timeout event.
6098    ///
6099    /// If no timeout has occurred it does nothing.
6100    pub fn on_timeout(&mut self) {
6101        let now = time::Instant::now();
6102
6103        if let Some(draining_timer) = self.draining_timer {
6104            if draining_timer <= now {
6105                trace!("{} draining timeout expired", self.trace_id);
6106
6107                self.mark_closed();
6108            }
6109
6110            // Draining timer takes precedence over all other timers. If it is
6111            // set it means the connection is closing so there's no point in
6112            // processing the other timers.
6113            return;
6114        }
6115
6116        if let Some(timer) = self.idle_timer {
6117            if timer <= now {
6118                trace!("{} idle timeout expired", self.trace_id);
6119
6120                self.mark_closed();
6121                self.timed_out = true;
6122                return;
6123            }
6124        }
6125
6126        if let Some(timer) = self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Application]
6127            .key_update
6128            .as_ref()
6129            .map(|key_update| key_update.timer)
6130        {
6131            if timer <= now {
6132                // Discard previous key once key update timer expired.
6133                let _ = self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Application]
6134                    .key_update
6135                    .take();
6136            }
6137        }
6138
6139        let handshake_status = self.handshake_status();
6140
6141        for (_, p) in self.paths.iter_mut() {
6142            if let Some(timer) = p.recovery.loss_detection_timer() {
6143                if timer <= now {
6144                    trace!("{} loss detection timeout expired", self.trace_id);
6145
6146                    let (lost_packets, lost_bytes) = p.on_loss_detection_timeout(
6147                        handshake_status,
6148                        now,
6149                        self.is_server,
6150                        &self.trace_id,
6151                    );
6152
6153                    self.lost_count += lost_packets;
6154                    self.lost_bytes += lost_bytes as u64;
6155
6156                    qlog_with_type!(QLOG_METRICS, self.qlog, q, {
6157                        if let Some(ev_data) = p.recovery.maybe_qlog() {
6158                            q.add_event_data_with_instant(ev_data, now).ok();
6159                        }
6160                    });
6161                }
6162            }
6163        }
6164
6165        // Notify timeout events to the application.
6166        self.paths.notify_failed_validations();
6167
6168        // If the active path failed, try to find a new candidate.
6169        if self.paths.get_active_path_id().is_err() {
6170            match self.paths.find_candidate_path() {
6171                Some(pid) => {
6172                    if self.set_active_path(pid, now).is_err() {
6173                        // The connection cannot continue.
6174                        self.mark_closed();
6175                    }
6176                },
6177
6178                // The connection cannot continue.
6179                None => {
6180                    self.mark_closed();
6181                },
6182            }
6183        }
6184    }
6185
6186    /// Requests the stack to perform path validation of the proposed 4-tuple.
6187    ///
6188    /// Probing new paths requires spare Connection IDs at both the host and the
6189    /// peer sides. If it is not the case, it raises an [`OutOfIdentifiers`].
6190    ///
6191    /// The probing of new addresses can only be done by the client. The server
6192    /// can only probe network paths that were previously advertised by
6193    /// [`PathEvent::New`]. If the server tries to probe such an unseen network
6194    /// path, this call raises an [`InvalidState`].
6195    ///
6196    /// The caller might also want to probe an existing path. In such case, it
6197    /// triggers a PATH_CHALLENGE frame, but it does not require spare CIDs.
6198    ///
6199    /// A server always probes a new path it observes. Calling this method is
6200    /// hence not required to validate a new path. However, a server can still
6201    /// request an additional path validation of the proposed 4-tuple.
6202    ///
6203    /// Calling this method several times before calling [`send()`] or
6204    /// [`send_on_path()`] results in a single probe being generated. An
6205    /// application wanting to send multiple in-flight probes must call this
6206    /// method again after having sent packets.
6207    ///
6208    /// Returns the Destination Connection ID sequence number associated to that
6209    /// path.
6210    ///
6211    /// [`PathEvent::New`]: enum.PathEvent.html#variant.New
6212    /// [`OutOfIdentifiers`]: enum.Error.html#OutOfIdentifiers
6213    /// [`InvalidState`]: enum.Error.html#InvalidState
6214    /// [`send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.send
6215    /// [`send_on_path()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.send_on_path
6216    pub fn probe_path(
6217        &mut self, local_addr: SocketAddr, peer_addr: SocketAddr,
6218    ) -> Result<u64> {
6219        // We may want to probe an existing path.
6220        let pid = match self.paths.path_id_from_addrs(&(local_addr, peer_addr)) {
6221            Some(pid) => pid,
6222            None => self.create_path_on_client(local_addr, peer_addr)?,
6223        };
6224
6225        let path = self.paths.get_mut(pid)?;
6226        path.request_validation();
6227
6228        path.active_dcid_seq.ok_or(Error::InvalidState)
6229    }
6230
6231    /// Migrates the connection to a new local address `local_addr`.
6232    ///
6233    /// The behavior is similar to [`migrate()`], with the nuance that the
6234    /// connection only changes the local address, but not the peer one.
6235    ///
6236    /// See [`migrate()`] for the full specification of this method.
6237    ///
6238    /// [`migrate()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.migrate
6239    pub fn migrate_source(&mut self, local_addr: SocketAddr) -> Result<u64> {
6240        let peer_addr = self.paths.get_active()?.peer_addr();
6241        self.migrate(local_addr, peer_addr)
6242    }
6243
6244    /// Migrates the connection over the given network path between `local_addr`
6245    /// and `peer_addr`.
6246    ///
6247    /// Connection migration can only be initiated by the client. Calling this
6248    /// method as a server returns [`InvalidState`].
6249    ///
6250    /// To initiate voluntary migration, there should be enough Connection IDs
6251    /// at both sides. If this requirement is not satisfied, this call returns
6252    /// [`OutOfIdentifiers`].
6253    ///
6254    /// Returns the Destination Connection ID associated to that migrated path.
6255    ///
6256    /// [`OutOfIdentifiers`]: enum.Error.html#OutOfIdentifiers
6257    /// [`InvalidState`]: enum.Error.html#InvalidState
6258    pub fn migrate(
6259        &mut self, local_addr: SocketAddr, peer_addr: SocketAddr,
6260    ) -> Result<u64> {
6261        if self.is_server {
6262            return Err(Error::InvalidState);
6263        }
6264
6265        // If the path already exists, mark it as the active one.
6266        let (pid, dcid_seq) = if let Some(pid) =
6267            self.paths.path_id_from_addrs(&(local_addr, peer_addr))
6268        {
6269            let path = self.paths.get_mut(pid)?;
6270
6271            // If it is already active, do nothing.
6272            if path.active() {
6273                return path.active_dcid_seq.ok_or(Error::OutOfIdentifiers);
6274            }
6275
6276            // Ensures that a Source Connection ID has been dedicated to this
6277            // path, or a free one is available. This is only required if the
6278            // host uses non-zero length Source Connection IDs.
6279            if !self.ids.zero_length_scid() &&
6280                path.active_scid_seq.is_none() &&
6281                self.ids.available_scids() == 0
6282            {
6283                return Err(Error::OutOfIdentifiers);
6284            }
6285
6286            // Ensures that the migrated path has a Destination Connection ID.
6287            let dcid_seq = if let Some(dcid_seq) = path.active_dcid_seq {
6288                dcid_seq
6289            } else {
6290                let dcid_seq = self
6291                    .ids
6292                    .lowest_available_dcid_seq()
6293                    .ok_or(Error::OutOfIdentifiers)?;
6294
6295                self.ids.link_dcid_to_path_id(dcid_seq, pid)?;
6296                path.active_dcid_seq = Some(dcid_seq);
6297
6298                dcid_seq
6299            };
6300
6301            (pid, dcid_seq)
6302        } else {
6303            let pid = self.create_path_on_client(local_addr, peer_addr)?;
6304
6305            let dcid_seq = self
6306                .paths
6307                .get(pid)?
6308                .active_dcid_seq
6309                .ok_or(Error::InvalidState)?;
6310
6311            (pid, dcid_seq)
6312        };
6313
6314        // Change the active path.
6315        self.set_active_path(pid, time::Instant::now())?;
6316
6317        Ok(dcid_seq)
6318    }
6319
6320    /// Provides additional source Connection IDs that the peer can use to reach
6321    /// this host.
6322    ///
6323    /// This triggers sending NEW_CONNECTION_ID frames if the provided Source
6324    /// Connection ID is not already present. In the case the caller tries to
6325    /// reuse a Connection ID with a different reset token, this raises an
6326    /// `InvalidState`.
6327    ///
6328    /// At any time, the peer cannot have more Destination Connection IDs than
6329    /// the maximum number of active Connection IDs it negotiated. In such case
6330    /// (i.e., when [`scids_left()`] returns 0), if the host agrees to
6331    /// request the removal of previous connection IDs, it sets the
6332    /// `retire_if_needed` parameter. Otherwise, an [`IdLimit`] is returned.
6333    ///
6334    /// Note that setting `retire_if_needed` does not prevent this function from
6335    /// returning an [`IdLimit`] in the case the caller wants to retire still
6336    /// unannounced Connection IDs.
6337    ///
6338    /// The caller is responsible for ensuring that the provided `scid` is not
6339    /// repeated several times over the connection. quiche ensures that as long
6340    /// as the provided Connection ID is still in use (i.e., not retired), it
6341    /// does not assign a different sequence number.
6342    ///
6343    /// Note that if the host uses zero-length Source Connection IDs, it cannot
6344    /// advertise Source Connection IDs and calling this method returns an
6345    /// [`InvalidState`].
6346    ///
6347    /// Returns the sequence number associated to the provided Connection ID.
6348    ///
6349    /// [`scids_left()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.scids_left
6350    /// [`IdLimit`]: enum.Error.html#IdLimit
6351    /// [`InvalidState`]: enum.Error.html#InvalidState
6352    pub fn new_scid(
6353        &mut self, scid: &ConnectionId, reset_token: u128, retire_if_needed: bool,
6354    ) -> Result<u64> {
6355        self.ids.new_scid(
6356            scid.to_vec().into(),
6357            Some(reset_token),
6358            true,
6359            None,
6360            retire_if_needed,
6361        )
6362    }
6363
6364    /// Returns the number of source Connection IDs that are active. This is
6365    /// only meaningful if the host uses non-zero length Source Connection IDs.
6366    pub fn active_scids(&self) -> usize {
6367        self.ids.active_source_cids()
6368    }
6369
6370    /// Returns the number of source Connection IDs that should be provided
6371    /// to the peer without exceeding the limit it advertised.
6372    ///
6373    /// This will automatically limit the number of Connection IDs to the
6374    /// minimum between the locally configured active connection ID limit,
6375    /// and the one sent by the peer.
6376    ///
6377    /// To obtain the maximum possible value allowed by the peer an application
6378    /// can instead inspect the [`peer_active_conn_id_limit`] value.
6379    ///
6380    /// [`peer_active_conn_id_limit`]: struct.Stats.html#structfield.peer_active_conn_id_limit
6381    #[inline]
6382    pub fn scids_left(&self) -> usize {
6383        let max_active_source_cids = cmp::min(
6384            self.peer_transport_params.active_conn_id_limit,
6385            self.local_transport_params.active_conn_id_limit,
6386        ) as usize;
6387
6388        max_active_source_cids - self.active_scids()
6389    }
6390
6391    /// Requests the retirement of the destination Connection ID used by the
6392    /// host to reach its peer.
6393    ///
6394    /// This triggers sending RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID frames.
6395    ///
6396    /// If the application tries to retire a non-existing Destination Connection
6397    /// ID sequence number, or if it uses zero-length Destination Connection ID,
6398    /// this method returns an [`InvalidState`].
6399    ///
6400    /// At any time, the host must have at least one Destination ID. If the
6401    /// application tries to retire the last one, or if the caller tries to
6402    /// retire the destination Connection ID used by the current active path
6403    /// while having neither spare Destination Connection IDs nor validated
6404    /// network paths, this method returns an [`OutOfIdentifiers`]. This
6405    /// behavior prevents the caller from stalling the connection due to the
6406    /// lack of validated path to send non-probing packets.
6407    ///
6408    /// [`InvalidState`]: enum.Error.html#InvalidState
6409    /// [`OutOfIdentifiers`]: enum.Error.html#OutOfIdentifiers
6410    pub fn retire_dcid(&mut self, dcid_seq: u64) -> Result<()> {
6411        if self.ids.zero_length_dcid() {
6412            return Err(Error::InvalidState);
6413        }
6414
6415        let active_path_dcid_seq = self
6416            .paths
6417            .get_active()?
6418            .active_dcid_seq
6419            .ok_or(Error::InvalidState)?;
6420
6421        let active_path_id = self.paths.get_active_path_id()?;
6422
6423        if active_path_dcid_seq == dcid_seq &&
6424            self.ids.lowest_available_dcid_seq().is_none() &&
6425            !self
6426                .paths
6427                .iter()
6428                .any(|(pid, p)| pid != active_path_id && p.usable())
6429        {
6430            return Err(Error::OutOfIdentifiers);
6431        }
6432
6433        if let Some(pid) = self.ids.retire_dcid(dcid_seq)? {
6434            // The retired Destination CID was associated to a given path. Let's
6435            // find an available DCID to associate to that path.
6436            let path = self.paths.get_mut(pid)?;
6437            let dcid_seq = self.ids.lowest_available_dcid_seq();
6438
6439            if let Some(dcid_seq) = dcid_seq {
6440                self.ids.link_dcid_to_path_id(dcid_seq, pid)?;
6441            }
6442
6443            path.active_dcid_seq = dcid_seq;
6444        }
6445
6446        Ok(())
6447    }
6448
6449    /// Processes path-specific events.
6450    ///
6451    /// On success it returns a [`PathEvent`], or `None` when there are no
6452    /// events to report. Please refer to [`PathEvent`] for the exhaustive event
6453    /// list.
6454    ///
6455    /// Note that all events are edge-triggered, meaning that once reported they
6456    /// will not be reported again by calling this method again, until the event
6457    /// is re-armed.
6458    ///
6459    /// [`PathEvent`]: enum.PathEvent.html
6460    pub fn path_event_next(&mut self) -> Option<PathEvent> {
6461        self.paths.pop_event()
6462    }
6463
6464    /// Returns the number of source Connection IDs that are retired.
6465    pub fn retired_scids(&self) -> usize {
6466        self.ids.retired_source_cids()
6467    }
6468
6469    /// Returns a source `ConnectionId` that has been retired.
6470    ///
6471    /// On success it returns a [`ConnectionId`], or `None` when there are no
6472    /// more retired connection IDs.
6473    ///
6474    /// [`ConnectionId`]: struct.ConnectionId.html
6475    pub fn retired_scid_next(&mut self) -> Option<ConnectionId<'static>> {
6476        self.ids.pop_retired_scid()
6477    }
6478
6479    /// Returns the number of spare Destination Connection IDs, i.e.,
6480    /// Destination Connection IDs that are still unused.
6481    ///
6482    /// Note that this function returns 0 if the host uses zero length
6483    /// Destination Connection IDs.
6484    pub fn available_dcids(&self) -> usize {
6485        self.ids.available_dcids()
6486    }
6487
6488    /// Returns an iterator over destination `SockAddr`s whose association
6489    /// with `from` forms a known QUIC path on which packets can be sent to.
6490    ///
6491    /// This function is typically used in combination with [`send_on_path()`].
6492    ///
6493    /// Note that the iterator includes all the possible combination of
6494    /// destination `SockAddr`s, even those whose sending is not required now.
6495    /// In other words, this is another way for the application to recall from
6496    /// past [`PathEvent::New`] events.
6497    ///
6498    /// [`PathEvent::New`]: enum.PathEvent.html#variant.New
6499    /// [`send_on_path()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.send_on_path
6500    ///
6501    /// ## Examples:
6502    ///
6503    /// ```no_run
6504    /// # let mut out = [0; 512];
6505    /// # let socket = std::net::UdpSocket::bind("127.0.0.1:0").unwrap();
6506    /// # let mut config = quiche::Config::new(quiche::PROTOCOL_VERSION)?;
6507    /// # let scid = quiche::ConnectionId::from_ref(&[0xba; 16]);
6508    /// # let local = socket.local_addr().unwrap();
6509    /// # let peer = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
6510    /// # let mut conn = quiche::accept(&scid, None, local, peer, &mut config)?;
6511    /// // Iterate over possible destinations for the given local `SockAddr`.
6512    /// for dest in conn.paths_iter(local) {
6513    ///     loop {
6514    ///         let (write, send_info) =
6515    ///             match conn.send_on_path(&mut out, Some(local), Some(dest)) {
6516    ///                 Ok(v) => v,
6517    ///
6518    ///                 Err(quiche::Error::Done) => {
6519    ///                     // Done writing for this destination.
6520    ///                     break;
6521    ///                 },
6522    ///
6523    ///                 Err(e) => {
6524    ///                     // An error occurred, handle it.
6525    ///                     break;
6526    ///                 },
6527    ///             };
6528    ///
6529    ///         socket.send_to(&out[..write], &send_info.to).unwrap();
6530    ///     }
6531    /// }
6532    /// # Ok::<(), quiche::Error>(())
6533    /// ```
6534    #[inline]
6535    pub fn paths_iter(&self, from: SocketAddr) -> SocketAddrIter {
6536        // Instead of trying to identify whether packets will be sent on the
6537        // given 4-tuple, simply filter paths that cannot be used.
6538        SocketAddrIter {
6539            sockaddrs: self
6540                .paths
6541                .iter()
6542                .filter(|(_, p)| p.active_dcid_seq.is_some())
6543                .filter(|(_, p)| p.usable() || p.probing_required())
6544                .filter(|(_, p)| p.local_addr() == from)
6545                .map(|(_, p)| p.peer_addr())
6546                .collect(),
6547
6548            index: 0,
6549        }
6550    }
6551
6552    /// Closes the connection with the given error and reason.
6553    ///
6554    /// The `app` parameter specifies whether an application close should be
6555    /// sent to the peer. Otherwise a normal connection close is sent.
6556    ///
6557    /// If `app` is true but the connection is not in a state that is safe to
6558    /// send an application error (not established nor in early data), in
6559    /// accordance with [RFC
6560    /// 9000](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9000.html#section-10.2.3-3), the
6561    /// error code is changed to APPLICATION_ERROR and the reason phrase is
6562    /// cleared.
6563    ///
6564    /// Returns [`Done`] if the connection had already been closed.
6565    ///
6566    /// Note that the connection will not be closed immediately. An application
6567    /// should continue calling the [`recv()`], [`send()`], [`timeout()`] and
6568    /// [`on_timeout()`] methods as normal, until the [`is_closed()`] method
6569    /// returns `true`.
6570    ///
6571    /// [`Done`]: enum.Error.html#variant.Done
6572    /// [`recv()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.recv
6573    /// [`send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.send
6574    /// [`timeout()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.timeout
6575    /// [`on_timeout()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.on_timeout
6576    /// [`is_closed()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.is_closed
6577    pub fn close(&mut self, app: bool, err: u64, reason: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
6578        if self.is_closed() || self.is_draining() {
6579            return Err(Error::Done);
6580        }
6581
6582        if self.local_error.is_some() {
6583            return Err(Error::Done);
6584        }
6585
6586        let is_safe_to_send_app_data =
6587            self.is_established() || self.is_in_early_data();
6588
6589        if app && !is_safe_to_send_app_data {
6590            // Clear error information.
6591            self.local_error = Some(ConnectionError {
6592                is_app: false,
6593                error_code: 0x0c,
6594                reason: vec![],
6595            });
6596        } else {
6597            self.local_error = Some(ConnectionError {
6598                is_app: app,
6599                error_code: err,
6600                reason: reason.to_vec(),
6601            });
6602        }
6603
6604        // When no packet was successfully processed close connection immediately.
6605        if self.recv_count == 0 {
6606            self.mark_closed();
6607        }
6608
6609        Ok(())
6610    }
6611
6612    /// Returns a string uniquely representing the connection.
6613    ///
6614    /// This can be used for logging purposes to differentiate between multiple
6615    /// connections.
6616    #[inline]
6617    pub fn trace_id(&self) -> &str {
6618        &self.trace_id
6619    }
6620
6621    /// Returns the negotiated ALPN protocol.
6622    ///
6623    /// If no protocol has been negotiated, the returned value is empty.
6624    #[inline]
6625    pub fn application_proto(&self) -> &[u8] {
6626        self.alpn.as_ref()
6627    }
6628
6629    /// Returns the server name requested by the client.
6630    #[inline]
6631    pub fn server_name(&self) -> Option<&str> {
6632        self.handshake.server_name()
6633    }
6634
6635    /// Returns the peer's leaf certificate (if any) as a DER-encoded buffer.
6636    #[inline]
6637    pub fn peer_cert(&self) -> Option<&[u8]> {
6638        self.handshake.peer_cert()
6639    }
6640
6641    /// Returns the peer's certificate chain (if any) as a vector of DER-encoded
6642    /// buffers.
6643    ///
6644    /// The certificate at index 0 is the peer's leaf certificate, the other
6645    /// certificates (if any) are the chain certificate authorities used to
6646    /// sign the leaf certificate.
6647    #[inline]
6648    pub fn peer_cert_chain(&self) -> Option<Vec<&[u8]>> {
6649        self.handshake.peer_cert_chain()
6650    }
6651
6652    /// Returns the serialized cryptographic session for the connection.
6653    ///
6654    /// This can be used by a client to cache a connection's session, and resume
6655    /// it later using the [`set_session()`] method.
6656    ///
6657    /// [`set_session()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.set_session
6658    #[inline]
6659    pub fn session(&self) -> Option<&[u8]> {
6660        self.session.as_deref()
6661    }
6662
6663    /// Returns the source connection ID.
6664    ///
6665    /// When there are multiple IDs, and if there is an active path, the ID used
6666    /// on that path is returned. Otherwise the oldest ID is returned.
6667    ///
6668    /// Note that the value returned can change throughout the connection's
6669    /// lifetime.
6670    #[inline]
6671    pub fn source_id(&self) -> ConnectionId {
6672        if let Ok(path) = self.paths.get_active() {
6673            if let Some(active_scid_seq) = path.active_scid_seq {
6674                if let Ok(e) = self.ids.get_scid(active_scid_seq) {
6675                    return ConnectionId::from_ref(e.cid.as_ref());
6676                }
6677            }
6678        }
6679
6680        let e = self.ids.oldest_scid();
6681        ConnectionId::from_ref(e.cid.as_ref())
6682    }
6683
6684    /// Returns all active source connection IDs.
6685    ///
6686    /// An iterator is returned for all active IDs (i.e. ones that have not
6687    /// been explicitly retired yet).
6688    #[inline]
6689    pub fn source_ids(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &ConnectionId> {
6690        self.ids.scids_iter()
6691    }
6692
6693    /// Returns the destination connection ID.
6694    ///
6695    /// Note that the value returned can change throughout the connection's
6696    /// lifetime.
6697    #[inline]
6698    pub fn destination_id(&self) -> ConnectionId {
6699        if let Ok(path) = self.paths.get_active() {
6700            if let Some(active_dcid_seq) = path.active_dcid_seq {
6701                if let Ok(e) = self.ids.get_dcid(active_dcid_seq) {
6702                    return ConnectionId::from_ref(e.cid.as_ref());
6703                }
6704            }
6705        }
6706
6707        let e = self.ids.oldest_dcid();
6708        ConnectionId::from_ref(e.cid.as_ref())
6709    }
6710
6711    /// Returns true if the connection handshake is complete.
6712    #[inline]
6713    pub fn is_established(&self) -> bool {
6714        self.handshake_completed
6715    }
6716
6717    /// Returns true if the connection is resumed.
6718    #[inline]
6719    pub fn is_resumed(&self) -> bool {
6720        self.handshake.is_resumed()
6721    }
6722
6723    /// Returns true if the connection has a pending handshake that has
6724    /// progressed enough to send or receive early data.
6725    #[inline]
6726    pub fn is_in_early_data(&self) -> bool {
6727        self.handshake.is_in_early_data()
6728    }
6729
6730    /// Returns whether there is stream or DATAGRAM data available to read.
6731    #[inline]
6732    pub fn is_readable(&self) -> bool {
6733        self.streams.has_readable() || self.dgram_recv_front_len().is_some()
6734    }
6735
6736    /// Returns whether the network path with local address `from` and remote
6737    /// address `peer` has been validated.
6738    ///
6739    /// If the 4-tuple does not exist over the connection, returns an
6740    /// [`InvalidState`].
6741    ///
6742    /// [`InvalidState`]: enum.Error.html#variant.InvalidState
6743    pub fn is_path_validated(
6744        &self, from: SocketAddr, to: SocketAddr,
6745    ) -> Result<bool> {
6746        let pid = self
6747            .paths
6748            .path_id_from_addrs(&(from, to))
6749            .ok_or(Error::InvalidState)?;
6750
6751        Ok(self.paths.get(pid)?.validated())
6752    }
6753
6754    /// Returns true if the connection is draining.
6755    ///
6756    /// If this returns `true`, the connection object cannot yet be dropped, but
6757    /// no new application data can be sent or received. An application should
6758    /// continue calling the [`recv()`], [`timeout()`], and [`on_timeout()`]
6759    /// methods as normal, until the [`is_closed()`] method returns `true`.
6760    ///
6761    /// In contrast, once `is_draining()` returns `true`, calling [`send()`]
6762    /// is not required because no new outgoing packets will be generated.
6763    ///
6764    /// [`recv()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.recv
6765    /// [`send()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.send
6766    /// [`timeout()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.timeout
6767    /// [`on_timeout()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.on_timeout
6768    /// [`is_closed()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.is_closed
6769    #[inline]
6770    pub fn is_draining(&self) -> bool {
6771        self.draining_timer.is_some()
6772    }
6773
6774    /// Returns true if the connection is closed.
6775    ///
6776    /// If this returns true, the connection object can be dropped.
6777    #[inline]
6778    pub fn is_closed(&self) -> bool {
6779        self.closed
6780    }
6781
6782    /// Returns true if the connection was closed due to the idle timeout.
6783    #[inline]
6784    pub fn is_timed_out(&self) -> bool {
6785        self.timed_out
6786    }
6787
6788    /// Returns the error received from the peer, if any.
6789    ///
6790    /// Note that a `Some` return value does not necessarily imply
6791    /// [`is_closed()`] or any other connection state.
6792    ///
6793    /// [`is_closed()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.is_closed
6794    #[inline]
6795    pub fn peer_error(&self) -> Option<&ConnectionError> {
6796        self.peer_error.as_ref()
6797    }
6798
6799    /// Returns the error [`close()`] was called with, or internally
6800    /// created quiche errors, if any.
6801    ///
6802    /// Note that a `Some` return value does not necessarily imply
6803    /// [`is_closed()`] or any other connection state.
6804    /// `Some` also does not guarantee that the error has been sent to
6805    /// or received by the peer.
6806    ///
6807    /// [`close()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.close
6808    /// [`is_closed()`]: struct.Connection.html#method.is_closed
6809    #[inline]
6810    pub fn local_error(&self) -> Option<&ConnectionError> {
6811        self.local_error.as_ref()
6812    }
6813
6814    /// Collects and returns statistics about the connection.
6815    #[inline]
6816    pub fn stats(&self) -> Stats {
6817        Stats {
6818            recv: self.recv_count,
6819            sent: self.sent_count,
6820            lost: self.lost_count,
6821            retrans: self.retrans_count,
6822            sent_bytes: self.sent_bytes,
6823            recv_bytes: self.recv_bytes,
6824            acked_bytes: self.acked_bytes,
6825            lost_bytes: self.lost_bytes,
6826            stream_retrans_bytes: self.stream_retrans_bytes,
6827            dgram_recv: self.dgram_recv_count,
6828            dgram_sent: self.dgram_sent_count,
6829            paths_count: self.paths.len(),
6830            reset_stream_count_local: self.reset_stream_local_count,
6831            stopped_stream_count_local: self.stopped_stream_local_count,
6832            reset_stream_count_remote: self.reset_stream_remote_count,
6833            stopped_stream_count_remote: self.stopped_stream_remote_count,
6834            path_challenge_rx_count: self.path_challenge_rx_count,
6835        }
6836    }
6837
6838    /// Returns reference to peer's transport parameters. Returns `None` if we
6839    /// have not yet processed the peer's transport parameters.
6840    pub fn peer_transport_params(&self) -> Option<&TransportParams> {
6841        if !self.parsed_peer_transport_params {
6842            return None;
6843        }
6844
6845        Some(&self.peer_transport_params)
6846    }
6847
6848    /// Collects and returns statistics about each known path for the
6849    /// connection.
6850    pub fn path_stats(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = PathStats> + '_ {
6851        self.paths.iter().map(|(_, p)| p.stats())
6852    }
6853
6854    /// Returns whether or not this is a server-side connection.
6855    pub fn is_server(&self) -> bool {
6856        self.is_server
6857    }
6858
6859    fn encode_transport_params(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
6860        let mut raw_params = [0; 128];
6861
6862        let raw_params = TransportParams::encode(
6863            &self.local_transport_params,
6864            self.is_server,
6865            &mut raw_params,
6866        )?;
6867
6868        self.handshake.set_quic_transport_params(raw_params)?;
6869
6870        Ok(())
6871    }
6872
6873    fn parse_peer_transport_params(
6874        &mut self, peer_params: TransportParams,
6875    ) -> Result<()> {
6876        // Validate initial_source_connection_id.
6877        match &peer_params.initial_source_connection_id {
6878            Some(v) if v != &self.destination_id() =>
6879                return Err(Error::InvalidTransportParam),
6880
6881            Some(_) => (),
6882
6883            // initial_source_connection_id must be sent by
6884            // both endpoints.
6885            None => return Err(Error::InvalidTransportParam),
6886        }
6887
6888        // Validate original_destination_connection_id.
6889        if let Some(odcid) = &self.odcid {
6890            match &peer_params.original_destination_connection_id {
6891                Some(v) if v != odcid =>
6892                    return Err(Error::InvalidTransportParam),
6893
6894                Some(_) => (),
6895
6896                // original_destination_connection_id must be
6897                // sent by the server.
6898                None if !self.is_server =>
6899                    return Err(Error::InvalidTransportParam),
6900
6901                None => (),
6902            }
6903        }
6904
6905        // Validate retry_source_connection_id.
6906        if let Some(rscid) = &self.rscid {
6907            match &peer_params.retry_source_connection_id {
6908                Some(v) if v != rscid =>
6909                    return Err(Error::InvalidTransportParam),
6910
6911                Some(_) => (),
6912
6913                // retry_source_connection_id must be sent by
6914                // the server.
6915                None => return Err(Error::InvalidTransportParam),
6916            }
6917        }
6918
6919        self.process_peer_transport_params(peer_params)?;
6920
6921        self.parsed_peer_transport_params = true;
6922
6923        Ok(())
6924    }
6925
6926    fn process_peer_transport_params(
6927        &mut self, peer_params: TransportParams,
6928    ) -> Result<()> {
6929        self.max_tx_data = peer_params.initial_max_data;
6930
6931        // Update send capacity.
6932        self.update_tx_cap();
6933
6934        self.streams
6935            .update_peer_max_streams_bidi(peer_params.initial_max_streams_bidi);
6936        self.streams
6937            .update_peer_max_streams_uni(peer_params.initial_max_streams_uni);
6938
6939        let max_ack_delay =
6940            time::Duration::from_millis(peer_params.max_ack_delay);
6941
6942        self.recovery_config.max_ack_delay = max_ack_delay;
6943
6944        let active_path = self.paths.get_active_mut()?;
6945
6946        active_path.recovery.update_max_ack_delay(max_ack_delay);
6947
6948        if active_path.pmtud.get_probe_status() {
6949            active_path.recovery.pmtud_update_max_datagram_size(
6950                active_path
6951                    .pmtud
6952                    .get_probe_size()
6953                    .min(peer_params.max_udp_payload_size as usize),
6954            );
6955        } else {
6956            active_path.recovery.update_max_datagram_size(
6957                peer_params.max_udp_payload_size as usize,
6958            );
6959        }
6960
6961        // Record the max_active_conn_id parameter advertised by the peer.
6962        self.ids
6963            .set_source_conn_id_limit(peer_params.active_conn_id_limit);
6964
6965        self.peer_transport_params = peer_params;
6966
6967        Ok(())
6968    }
6969
6970    /// Continues the handshake.
6971    ///
6972    /// If the connection is already established, it does nothing.
6973    fn do_handshake(&mut self, now: time::Instant) -> Result<()> {
6974        let mut ex_data = tls::ExData {
6975            application_protos: &self.application_protos,
6976
6977            pkt_num_spaces: &mut self.pkt_num_spaces,
6978
6979            session: &mut self.session,
6980
6981            local_error: &mut self.local_error,
6982
6983            keylog: self.keylog.as_mut(),
6984
6985            trace_id: &self.trace_id,
6986
6987            recovery_config: self.recovery_config,
6988
6989            is_server: self.is_server,
6990        };
6991
6992        if self.handshake_completed {
6993            return self.handshake.process_post_handshake(&mut ex_data);
6994        }
6995
6996        match self.handshake.do_handshake(&mut ex_data) {
6997            Ok(_) => (),
6998
6999            Err(Error::Done) => {
7000                // Apply in-handshake configuration from callbacks before any
7001                // packet has been sent.
7002                if self.sent_count == 0 &&
7003                    ex_data.recovery_config != self.recovery_config
7004                {
7005                    if let Ok(path) = self.paths.get_active_mut() {
7006                        self.recovery_config = ex_data.recovery_config;
7007                        path.reinit_recovery(&self.recovery_config);
7008                    }
7009                }
7010
7011                // Try to parse transport parameters as soon as the first flight
7012                // of handshake data is processed.
7013                //
7014                // This is potentially dangerous as the handshake hasn't been
7015                // completed yet, though it's required to be able to send data
7016                // in 0.5 RTT.
7017                let raw_params = self.handshake.quic_transport_params();
7018
7019                if !self.parsed_peer_transport_params && !raw_params.is_empty() {
7020                    let peer_params = TransportParams::decode(
7021                        raw_params,
7022                        self.is_server,
7023                        self.peer_transport_params_track_unknown,
7024                    )?;
7025
7026                    self.parse_peer_transport_params(peer_params)?;
7027                }
7028
7029                return Ok(());
7030            },
7031
7032            Err(e) => return Err(e),
7033        };
7034
7035        self.handshake_completed = self.handshake.is_completed();
7036
7037        self.alpn = self.handshake.alpn_protocol().to_vec();
7038
7039        let raw_params = self.handshake.quic_transport_params();
7040
7041        if !self.parsed_peer_transport_params && !raw_params.is_empty() {
7042            let peer_params = TransportParams::decode(
7043                raw_params,
7044                self.is_server,
7045                self.peer_transport_params_track_unknown,
7046            )?;
7047
7048            self.parse_peer_transport_params(peer_params)?;
7049        }
7050
7051        if self.handshake_completed {
7052            // The handshake is considered confirmed at the server when the
7053            // handshake completes, at which point we can also drop the
7054            // handshake epoch.
7055            if self.is_server {
7056                self.handshake_confirmed = true;
7057
7058                self.drop_epoch_state(packet::Epoch::Handshake, now);
7059            }
7060
7061            // Once the handshake is completed there's no point in processing
7062            // 0-RTT packets anymore, so clear the buffer now.
7063            self.undecryptable_pkts.clear();
7064
7065            trace!("{} connection established: proto={:?} cipher={:?} curve={:?} sigalg={:?} resumed={} {:?}",
7066                   &self.trace_id,
7067                   std::str::from_utf8(self.application_proto()),
7068                   self.handshake.cipher(),
7069                   self.handshake.curve(),
7070                   self.handshake.sigalg(),
7071                   self.handshake.is_resumed(),
7072                   self.peer_transport_params);
7073        }
7074
7075        Ok(())
7076    }
7077
7078    /// Selects the packet type for the next outgoing packet.
7079    fn write_pkt_type(&self, send_pid: usize) -> Result<packet::Type> {
7080        // On error send packet in the latest epoch available, but only send
7081        // 1-RTT ones when the handshake is completed.
7082        if self
7083            .local_error
7084            .as_ref()
7085            .is_some_and(|conn_err| !conn_err.is_app)
7086        {
7087            let epoch = match self.handshake.write_level() {
7088                crypto::Level::Initial => packet::Epoch::Initial,
7089                crypto::Level::ZeroRTT => unreachable!(),
7090                crypto::Level::Handshake => packet::Epoch::Handshake,
7091                crypto::Level::OneRTT => packet::Epoch::Application,
7092            };
7093
7094            if !self.handshake_confirmed {
7095                match epoch {
7096                    // Downgrade the epoch to Handshake as the handshake is not
7097                    // completed yet.
7098                    packet::Epoch::Application =>
7099                        return Ok(packet::Type::Handshake),
7100
7101                    // Downgrade the epoch to Initial as the remote peer might
7102                    // not be able to decrypt handshake packets yet.
7103                    packet::Epoch::Handshake
7104                        if self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Initial]
7105                            .has_keys() =>
7106                        return Ok(packet::Type::Initial),
7107
7108                    _ => (),
7109                };
7110            }
7111
7112            return Ok(packet::Type::from_epoch(epoch));
7113        }
7114
7115        for &epoch in packet::Epoch::epochs(
7116            packet::Epoch::Initial..=packet::Epoch::Application,
7117        ) {
7118            // Only send packets in a space when we have the send keys for it.
7119            if self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].crypto_seal.is_none() {
7120                continue;
7121            }
7122
7123            // We are ready to send data for this packet number space.
7124            if self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].ready() {
7125                return Ok(packet::Type::from_epoch(epoch));
7126            }
7127
7128            // There are lost frames in this packet number space.
7129            for (_, p) in self.paths.iter() {
7130                if p.recovery.has_lost_frames(epoch) {
7131                    return Ok(packet::Type::from_epoch(epoch));
7132                }
7133
7134                // We need to send PTO probe packets.
7135                if p.recovery.loss_probes(epoch) > 0 {
7136                    return Ok(packet::Type::from_epoch(epoch));
7137                }
7138            }
7139        }
7140
7141        // If there are flushable, almost full or blocked streams, use the
7142        // Application epoch.
7143        let send_path = self.paths.get(send_pid)?;
7144        if (self.is_established() || self.is_in_early_data()) &&
7145            (self.should_send_handshake_done() ||
7146                self.almost_full ||
7147                self.blocked_limit.is_some() ||
7148                self.dgram_send_queue.has_pending() ||
7149                self.local_error
7150                    .as_ref()
7151                    .is_some_and(|conn_err| conn_err.is_app) ||
7152                self.streams.should_update_max_streams_bidi() ||
7153                self.streams.should_update_max_streams_uni() ||
7154                self.streams.has_flushable() ||
7155                self.streams.has_almost_full() ||
7156                self.streams.has_blocked() ||
7157                self.streams.has_reset() ||
7158                self.streams.has_stopped() ||
7159                self.ids.has_new_scids() ||
7160                self.ids.has_retire_dcids() ||
7161                send_path.pmtud.get_probe_status() ||
7162                send_path.needs_ack_eliciting ||
7163                send_path.probing_required())
7164        {
7165            // Only clients can send 0-RTT packets.
7166            if !self.is_server && self.is_in_early_data() {
7167                return Ok(packet::Type::ZeroRTT);
7168            }
7169
7170            return Ok(packet::Type::Short);
7171        }
7172
7173        Err(Error::Done)
7174    }
7175
7176    /// Returns the mutable stream with the given ID if it exists, or creates
7177    /// a new one otherwise.
7178    fn get_or_create_stream(
7179        &mut self, id: u64, local: bool,
7180    ) -> Result<&mut stream::Stream> {
7181        self.streams.get_or_create(
7182            id,
7183            &self.local_transport_params,
7184            &self.peer_transport_params,
7185            local,
7186            self.is_server,
7187        )
7188    }
7189
7190    /// Processes an incoming frame.
7191    fn process_frame(
7192        &mut self, frame: frame::Frame, hdr: &packet::Header,
7193        recv_path_id: usize, epoch: packet::Epoch, now: time::Instant,
7194    ) -> Result<()> {
7195        trace!("{} rx frm {:?}", self.trace_id, frame);
7196
7197        match frame {
7198            frame::Frame::Padding { .. } => (),
7199
7200            frame::Frame::Ping { .. } => (),
7201
7202            frame::Frame::ACK {
7203                ranges, ack_delay, ..
7204            } => {
7205                let ack_delay = ack_delay
7206                    .checked_mul(2_u64.pow(
7207                        self.peer_transport_params.ack_delay_exponent as u32,
7208                    ))
7209                    .ok_or(Error::InvalidFrame)?;
7210
7211                if epoch == packet::Epoch::Handshake ||
7212                    (epoch == packet::Epoch::Application &&
7213                        self.is_established())
7214                {
7215                    self.peer_verified_initial_address = true;
7216                }
7217
7218                let handshake_status = self.handshake_status();
7219
7220                let is_app_limited = self.delivery_rate_check_if_app_limited();
7221
7222                for (_, p) in self.paths.iter_mut() {
7223                    if is_app_limited {
7224                        p.recovery.delivery_rate_update_app_limited(true);
7225                    }
7226
7227                    let (lost_packets, lost_bytes, acked_bytes) =
7228                        p.recovery.on_ack_received(
7229                            &ranges,
7230                            ack_delay,
7231                            epoch,
7232                            handshake_status,
7233                            now,
7234                            &self.trace_id,
7235                        );
7236
7237                    self.lost_count += lost_packets;
7238                    self.lost_bytes += lost_bytes as u64;
7239                    self.acked_bytes += acked_bytes as u64;
7240                }
7241            },
7242
7243            frame::Frame::ResetStream {
7244                stream_id,
7245                error_code,
7246                final_size,
7247            } => {
7248                // Peer can't send on our unidirectional streams.
7249                if !stream::is_bidi(stream_id) &&
7250                    stream::is_local(stream_id, self.is_server)
7251                {
7252                    return Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(stream_id));
7253                }
7254
7255                let max_rx_data_left = self.max_rx_data() - self.rx_data;
7256
7257                // Get existing stream or create a new one, but if the stream
7258                // has already been closed and collected, ignore the frame.
7259                //
7260                // This can happen if e.g. an ACK frame is lost, and the peer
7261                // retransmits another frame before it realizes that the stream
7262                // is gone.
7263                //
7264                // Note that it makes it impossible to check if the frame is
7265                // illegal, since we have no state, but since we ignore the
7266                // frame, it should be fine.
7267                let stream = match self.get_or_create_stream(stream_id, false) {
7268                    Ok(v) => v,
7269
7270                    Err(Error::Done) => return Ok(()),
7271
7272                    Err(e) => return Err(e),
7273                };
7274
7275                let was_readable = stream.is_readable();
7276                let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
7277
7278                let max_off_delta =
7279                    stream.recv.reset(error_code, final_size)? as u64;
7280
7281                if max_off_delta > max_rx_data_left {
7282                    return Err(Error::FlowControl);
7283                }
7284
7285                if !was_readable && stream.is_readable() {
7286                    self.streams.insert_readable(&priority_key);
7287                }
7288
7289                self.rx_data += max_off_delta;
7290
7291                self.reset_stream_remote_count =
7292                    self.reset_stream_remote_count.saturating_add(1);
7293            },
7294
7295            frame::Frame::StopSending {
7296                stream_id,
7297                error_code,
7298            } => {
7299                // STOP_SENDING on a receive-only stream is a fatal error.
7300                if !stream::is_local(stream_id, self.is_server) &&
7301                    !stream::is_bidi(stream_id)
7302                {
7303                    return Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(stream_id));
7304                }
7305
7306                // Get existing stream or create a new one, but if the stream
7307                // has already been closed and collected, ignore the frame.
7308                //
7309                // This can happen if e.g. an ACK frame is lost, and the peer
7310                // retransmits another frame before it realizes that the stream
7311                // is gone.
7312                //
7313                // Note that it makes it impossible to check if the frame is
7314                // illegal, since we have no state, but since we ignore the
7315                // frame, it should be fine.
7316                let stream = match self.get_or_create_stream(stream_id, false) {
7317                    Ok(v) => v,
7318
7319                    Err(Error::Done) => return Ok(()),
7320
7321                    Err(e) => return Err(e),
7322                };
7323
7324                let was_writable = stream.is_writable();
7325
7326                let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
7327
7328                // Try stopping the stream.
7329                if let Ok((final_size, unsent)) = stream.send.stop(error_code) {
7330                    // Claw back some flow control allowance from data that was
7331                    // buffered but not actually sent before the stream was
7332                    // reset.
7333                    //
7334                    // Note that `tx_cap` will be updated later on, so no need
7335                    // to touch it here.
7336                    self.tx_data = self.tx_data.saturating_sub(unsent);
7337
7338                    self.tx_buffered =
7339                        self.tx_buffered.saturating_sub(unsent as usize);
7340
7341                    self.streams.insert_reset(stream_id, error_code, final_size);
7342
7343                    if !was_writable {
7344                        self.streams.insert_writable(&priority_key);
7345                    }
7346
7347                    self.stopped_stream_remote_count =
7348                        self.stopped_stream_remote_count.saturating_add(1);
7349                    self.reset_stream_local_count =
7350                        self.reset_stream_local_count.saturating_add(1);
7351                }
7352            },
7353
7354            frame::Frame::Crypto { data } => {
7355                if data.max_off() >= MAX_CRYPTO_STREAM_OFFSET {
7356                    return Err(Error::CryptoBufferExceeded);
7357                }
7358
7359                // Push the data to the stream so it can be re-ordered.
7360                self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].crypto_stream.recv.write(data)?;
7361
7362                // Feed crypto data to the TLS state, if there's data
7363                // available at the expected offset.
7364                let mut crypto_buf = [0; 512];
7365
7366                let level = crypto::Level::from_epoch(epoch);
7367
7368                let stream = &mut self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].crypto_stream;
7369
7370                while let Ok((read, _)) = stream.recv.emit(&mut crypto_buf) {
7371                    let recv_buf = &crypto_buf[..read];
7372                    self.handshake.provide_data(level, recv_buf)?;
7373                }
7374
7375                self.do_handshake(now)?;
7376            },
7377
7378            frame::Frame::CryptoHeader { .. } => unreachable!(),
7379
7380            // TODO: implement stateless retry
7381            frame::Frame::NewToken { .. } =>
7382                if self.is_server {
7383                    return Err(Error::InvalidPacket);
7384                },
7385
7386            frame::Frame::Stream { stream_id, data } => {
7387                // Peer can't send on our unidirectional streams.
7388                if !stream::is_bidi(stream_id) &&
7389                    stream::is_local(stream_id, self.is_server)
7390                {
7391                    return Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(stream_id));
7392                }
7393
7394                let max_rx_data_left = self.max_rx_data() - self.rx_data;
7395
7396                // Get existing stream or create a new one, but if the stream
7397                // has already been closed and collected, ignore the frame.
7398                //
7399                // This can happen if e.g. an ACK frame is lost, and the peer
7400                // retransmits another frame before it realizes that the stream
7401                // is gone.
7402                //
7403                // Note that it makes it impossible to check if the frame is
7404                // illegal, since we have no state, but since we ignore the
7405                // frame, it should be fine.
7406                let stream = match self.get_or_create_stream(stream_id, false) {
7407                    Ok(v) => v,
7408
7409                    Err(Error::Done) => return Ok(()),
7410
7411                    Err(e) => return Err(e),
7412                };
7413
7414                // Check for the connection-level flow control limit.
7415                let max_off_delta =
7416                    data.max_off().saturating_sub(stream.recv.max_off());
7417
7418                if max_off_delta > max_rx_data_left {
7419                    return Err(Error::FlowControl);
7420                }
7421
7422                let was_readable = stream.is_readable();
7423                let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
7424
7425                let was_draining = stream.recv.is_draining();
7426
7427                stream.recv.write(data)?;
7428
7429                if !was_readable && stream.is_readable() {
7430                    self.streams.insert_readable(&priority_key);
7431                }
7432
7433                self.rx_data += max_off_delta;
7434
7435                if was_draining {
7436                    // When a stream is in draining state it will not queue
7437                    // incoming data for the application to read, so consider
7438                    // the received data as consumed, which might trigger a flow
7439                    // control update.
7440                    self.flow_control.add_consumed(max_off_delta);
7441
7442                    if self.should_update_max_data() {
7443                        self.almost_full = true;
7444                    }
7445                }
7446            },
7447
7448            frame::Frame::StreamHeader { .. } => unreachable!(),
7449
7450            frame::Frame::MaxData { max } => {
7451                self.max_tx_data = cmp::max(self.max_tx_data, max);
7452            },
7453
7454            frame::Frame::MaxStreamData { stream_id, max } => {
7455                // Peer can't receive on its own unidirectional streams.
7456                if !stream::is_bidi(stream_id) &&
7457                    !stream::is_local(stream_id, self.is_server)
7458                {
7459                    return Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(stream_id));
7460                }
7461
7462                // Get existing stream or create a new one, but if the stream
7463                // has already been closed and collected, ignore the frame.
7464                //
7465                // This can happen if e.g. an ACK frame is lost, and the peer
7466                // retransmits another frame before it realizes that the stream
7467                // is gone.
7468                //
7469                // Note that it makes it impossible to check if the frame is
7470                // illegal, since we have no state, but since we ignore the
7471                // frame, it should be fine.
7472                let stream = match self.get_or_create_stream(stream_id, false) {
7473                    Ok(v) => v,
7474
7475                    Err(Error::Done) => return Ok(()),
7476
7477                    Err(e) => return Err(e),
7478                };
7479
7480                let was_flushable = stream.is_flushable();
7481
7482                stream.send.update_max_data(max);
7483
7484                let writable = stream.is_writable();
7485
7486                let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
7487
7488                // If the stream is now flushable push it to the flushable queue,
7489                // but only if it wasn't already queued.
7490                if stream.is_flushable() && !was_flushable {
7491                    let priority_key = Arc::clone(&stream.priority_key);
7492                    self.streams.insert_flushable(&priority_key);
7493                }
7494
7495                if writable {
7496                    self.streams.insert_writable(&priority_key);
7497                }
7498            },
7499
7500            frame::Frame::MaxStreamsBidi { max } => {
7501                if max > MAX_STREAM_ID {
7502                    return Err(Error::InvalidFrame);
7503                }
7504
7505                self.streams.update_peer_max_streams_bidi(max);
7506            },
7507
7508            frame::Frame::MaxStreamsUni { max } => {
7509                if max > MAX_STREAM_ID {
7510                    return Err(Error::InvalidFrame);
7511                }
7512
7513                self.streams.update_peer_max_streams_uni(max);
7514            },
7515
7516            frame::Frame::DataBlocked { .. } => (),
7517
7518            frame::Frame::StreamDataBlocked { .. } => (),
7519
7520            frame::Frame::StreamsBlockedBidi { limit } => {
7521                if limit > MAX_STREAM_ID {
7522                    return Err(Error::InvalidFrame);
7523                }
7524            },
7525
7526            frame::Frame::StreamsBlockedUni { limit } => {
7527                if limit > MAX_STREAM_ID {
7528                    return Err(Error::InvalidFrame);
7529                }
7530            },
7531
7532            frame::Frame::NewConnectionId {
7533                seq_num,
7534                retire_prior_to,
7535                conn_id,
7536                reset_token,
7537            } => {
7538                if self.ids.zero_length_dcid() {
7539                    return Err(Error::InvalidState);
7540                }
7541
7542                let mut retired_path_ids = SmallVec::new();
7543
7544                // Retire pending path IDs before propagating the error code to
7545                // make sure retired connection IDs are not in use anymore.
7546                let new_dcid_res = self.ids.new_dcid(
7547                    conn_id.into(),
7548                    seq_num,
7549                    u128::from_be_bytes(reset_token),
7550                    retire_prior_to,
7551                    &mut retired_path_ids,
7552                );
7553
7554                for (dcid_seq, pid) in retired_path_ids {
7555                    let path = self.paths.get_mut(pid)?;
7556
7557                    // Maybe the path already switched to another DCID.
7558                    if path.active_dcid_seq != Some(dcid_seq) {
7559                        continue;
7560                    }
7561
7562                    if let Some(new_dcid_seq) =
7563                        self.ids.lowest_available_dcid_seq()
7564                    {
7565                        path.active_dcid_seq = Some(new_dcid_seq);
7566
7567                        self.ids.link_dcid_to_path_id(new_dcid_seq, pid)?;
7568
7569                        trace!(
7570                            "{} path ID {} changed DCID: old seq num {} new seq num {}",
7571                            self.trace_id, pid, dcid_seq, new_dcid_seq,
7572                        );
7573                    } else {
7574                        // We cannot use this path anymore for now.
7575                        path.active_dcid_seq = None;
7576
7577                        trace!(
7578                            "{} path ID {} cannot be used; DCID seq num {} has been retired",
7579                            self.trace_id, pid, dcid_seq,
7580                        );
7581                    }
7582                }
7583
7584                // Propagate error (if any) now...
7585                new_dcid_res?;
7586            },
7587
7588            frame::Frame::RetireConnectionId { seq_num } => {
7589                if self.ids.zero_length_scid() {
7590                    return Err(Error::InvalidState);
7591                }
7592
7593                if let Some(pid) = self.ids.retire_scid(seq_num, &hdr.dcid)? {
7594                    let path = self.paths.get_mut(pid)?;
7595
7596                    // Maybe we already linked a new SCID to that path.
7597                    if path.active_scid_seq == Some(seq_num) {
7598                        // XXX: We do not remove unused paths now, we instead
7599                        // wait until we need to maintain more paths than the
7600                        // host is willing to.
7601                        path.active_scid_seq = None;
7602                    }
7603                }
7604            },
7605
7606            frame::Frame::PathChallenge { data } => {
7607                self.path_challenge_rx_count += 1;
7608
7609                self.paths
7610                    .get_mut(recv_path_id)?
7611                    .on_challenge_received(data);
7612            },
7613
7614            frame::Frame::PathResponse { data } => {
7615                self.paths.on_response_received(data)?;
7616            },
7617
7618            frame::Frame::ConnectionClose {
7619                error_code, reason, ..
7620            } => {
7621                self.peer_error = Some(ConnectionError {
7622                    is_app: false,
7623                    error_code,
7624                    reason,
7625                });
7626
7627                let path = self.paths.get_active()?;
7628                self.draining_timer = Some(now + (path.recovery.pto() * 3));
7629            },
7630
7631            frame::Frame::ApplicationClose { error_code, reason } => {
7632                self.peer_error = Some(ConnectionError {
7633                    is_app: true,
7634                    error_code,
7635                    reason,
7636                });
7637
7638                let path = self.paths.get_active()?;
7639                self.draining_timer = Some(now + (path.recovery.pto() * 3));
7640            },
7641
7642            frame::Frame::HandshakeDone => {
7643                if self.is_server {
7644                    return Err(Error::InvalidPacket);
7645                }
7646
7647                self.peer_verified_initial_address = true;
7648
7649                self.handshake_confirmed = true;
7650
7651                // Once the handshake is confirmed, we can drop Handshake keys.
7652                self.drop_epoch_state(packet::Epoch::Handshake, now);
7653            },
7654
7655            frame::Frame::Datagram { data } => {
7656                // Close the connection if DATAGRAMs are not enabled.
7657                // quiche always advertises support for 64K sized DATAGRAM
7658                // frames, as recommended by the standard, so we don't need a
7659                // size check.
7660                if !self.dgram_enabled() {
7661                    return Err(Error::InvalidState);
7662                }
7663
7664                // If recv queue is full, discard oldest
7665                if self.dgram_recv_queue.is_full() {
7666                    self.dgram_recv_queue.pop();
7667                }
7668
7669                self.dgram_recv_queue.push(data)?;
7670
7671                let _ = self.dgram_recv_count.saturating_add(1);
7672                let _ = self
7673                    .paths
7674                    .get_mut(recv_path_id)?
7675                    .dgram_recv_count
7676                    .saturating_add(1);
7677            },
7678
7679            frame::Frame::DatagramHeader { .. } => unreachable!(),
7680        }
7681
7682        Ok(())
7683    }
7684
7685    /// Drops the keys and recovery state for the given epoch.
7686    fn drop_epoch_state(&mut self, epoch: packet::Epoch, now: time::Instant) {
7687        if self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].crypto_open.is_none() {
7688            return;
7689        }
7690
7691        self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].crypto_open = None;
7692        self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].crypto_seal = None;
7693        self.pkt_num_spaces[epoch].clear();
7694
7695        let handshake_status = self.handshake_status();
7696        for (_, p) in self.paths.iter_mut() {
7697            p.recovery
7698                .on_pkt_num_space_discarded(epoch, handshake_status, now);
7699        }
7700
7701        trace!("{} dropped epoch {} state", self.trace_id, epoch);
7702    }
7703
7704    /// Returns true if the connection-level flow control needs to be updated.
7705    ///
7706    /// This happens when the new max data limit is at least double the amount
7707    /// of data that can be received before blocking.
7708    fn should_update_max_data(&self) -> bool {
7709        self.flow_control.should_update_max_data()
7710    }
7711
7712    /// Returns the connection level flow control limit.
7713    fn max_rx_data(&self) -> u64 {
7714        self.flow_control.max_data()
7715    }
7716
7717    /// Returns true if the HANDSHAKE_DONE frame needs to be sent.
7718    fn should_send_handshake_done(&self) -> bool {
7719        self.is_established() && !self.handshake_done_sent && self.is_server
7720    }
7721
7722    /// Returns the idle timeout value.
7723    ///
7724    /// `None` is returned if both end-points disabled the idle timeout.
7725    fn idle_timeout(&mut self) -> Option<time::Duration> {
7726        // If the transport parameter is set to 0, then the respective endpoint
7727        // decided to disable the idle timeout. If both are disabled we should
7728        // not set any timeout.
7729        if self.local_transport_params.max_idle_timeout == 0 &&
7730            self.peer_transport_params.max_idle_timeout == 0
7731        {
7732            return None;
7733        }
7734
7735        // If the local endpoint or the peer disabled the idle timeout, use the
7736        // other peer's value, otherwise use the minimum of the two values.
7737        let idle_timeout = if self.local_transport_params.max_idle_timeout == 0 {
7738            self.peer_transport_params.max_idle_timeout
7739        } else if self.peer_transport_params.max_idle_timeout == 0 {
7740            self.local_transport_params.max_idle_timeout
7741        } else {
7742            cmp::min(
7743                self.local_transport_params.max_idle_timeout,
7744                self.peer_transport_params.max_idle_timeout,
7745            )
7746        };
7747
7748        let path_pto = match self.paths.get_active() {
7749            Ok(p) => p.recovery.pto(),
7750            Err(_) => time::Duration::ZERO,
7751        };
7752
7753        let idle_timeout = time::Duration::from_millis(idle_timeout);
7754        let idle_timeout = cmp::max(idle_timeout, 3 * path_pto);
7755
7756        Some(idle_timeout)
7757    }
7758
7759    /// Returns the connection's handshake status for use in loss recovery.
7760    fn handshake_status(&self) -> recovery::HandshakeStatus {
7761        recovery::HandshakeStatus {
7762            has_handshake_keys: self.pkt_num_spaces[packet::Epoch::Handshake]
7763                .has_keys(),
7764
7765            peer_verified_address: self.peer_verified_initial_address,
7766
7767            completed: self.is_established(),
7768        }
7769    }
7770
7771    /// Updates send capacity.
7772    fn update_tx_cap(&mut self) {
7773        let cwin_available = match self.paths.get_active() {
7774            Ok(p) => p.recovery.cwnd_available() as u64,
7775            Err(_) => 0,
7776        };
7777
7778        self.tx_cap =
7779            cmp::min(cwin_available, self.max_tx_data - self.tx_data) as usize;
7780    }
7781
7782    fn delivery_rate_check_if_app_limited(&self) -> bool {
7783        // Enter the app-limited phase of delivery rate when these conditions
7784        // are met:
7785        //
7786        // - The remaining capacity is higher than available bytes in cwnd (there
7787        //   is more room to send).
7788        // - New data since the last send() is smaller than available bytes in
7789        //   cwnd (we queued less than what we can send).
7790        // - There is room to send more data in cwnd.
7791        //
7792        // In application-limited phases the transmission rate is limited by the
7793        // application rather than the congestion control algorithm.
7794        //
7795        // Note that this is equivalent to CheckIfApplicationLimited() from the
7796        // delivery rate draft. This is also separate from `recovery.app_limited`
7797        // and only applies to delivery rate calculation.
7798        let cwin_available = self
7799            .paths
7800            .iter()
7801            .filter(|&(_, p)| p.active())
7802            .map(|(_, p)| p.recovery.cwnd_available())
7803            .sum();
7804
7805        ((self.tx_buffered + self.dgram_send_queue_byte_size()) < cwin_available) &&
7806            (self.tx_data.saturating_sub(self.last_tx_data)) <
7807                cwin_available as u64 &&
7808            cwin_available > 0
7809    }
7810
7811    fn set_initial_dcid(
7812        &mut self, cid: ConnectionId<'static>, reset_token: Option<u128>,
7813        path_id: usize,
7814    ) -> Result<()> {
7815        self.ids.set_initial_dcid(cid, reset_token, Some(path_id));
7816        self.paths.get_mut(path_id)?.active_dcid_seq = Some(0);
7817
7818        Ok(())
7819    }
7820
7821    /// Selects the path that the incoming packet belongs to, or creates a new
7822    /// one if no existing path matches.
7823    fn get_or_create_recv_path_id(
7824        &mut self, recv_pid: Option<usize>, dcid: &ConnectionId, buf_len: usize,
7825        info: &RecvInfo,
7826    ) -> Result<usize> {
7827        let ids = &mut self.ids;
7828
7829        let (in_scid_seq, mut in_scid_pid) =
7830            ids.find_scid_seq(dcid).ok_or(Error::InvalidState)?;
7831
7832        if let Some(recv_pid) = recv_pid {
7833            // If the path observes a change of SCID used, note it.
7834            let recv_path = self.paths.get_mut(recv_pid)?;
7835
7836            let cid_entry =
7837                recv_path.active_scid_seq.and_then(|v| ids.get_scid(v).ok());
7838
7839            if cid_entry.map(|e| &e.cid) != Some(dcid) {
7840                let incoming_cid_entry = ids.get_scid(in_scid_seq)?;
7841
7842                let prev_recv_pid =
7843                    incoming_cid_entry.path_id.unwrap_or(recv_pid);
7844
7845                if prev_recv_pid != recv_pid {
7846                    trace!(
7847                        "{} peer reused CID {:?} from path {} on path {}",
7848                        self.trace_id,
7849                        dcid,
7850                        prev_recv_pid,
7851                        recv_pid
7852                    );
7853
7854                    // TODO: reset congestion control.
7855                }
7856
7857                trace!(
7858                    "{} path ID {} now see SCID with seq num {}",
7859                    self.trace_id,
7860                    recv_pid,
7861                    in_scid_seq
7862                );
7863
7864                recv_path.active_scid_seq = Some(in_scid_seq);
7865                ids.link_scid_to_path_id(in_scid_seq, recv_pid)?;
7866            }
7867
7868            return Ok(recv_pid);
7869        }
7870
7871        // This is a new 4-tuple. See if the CID has not been assigned on
7872        // another path.
7873
7874        // Ignore this step if are using zero-length SCID.
7875        if ids.zero_length_scid() {
7876            in_scid_pid = None;
7877        }
7878
7879        if let Some(in_scid_pid) = in_scid_pid {
7880            // This CID has been used by another path. If we have the
7881            // room to do so, create a new `Path` structure holding this
7882            // new 4-tuple. Otherwise, drop the packet.
7883            let old_path = self.paths.get_mut(in_scid_pid)?;
7884            let old_local_addr = old_path.local_addr();
7885            let old_peer_addr = old_path.peer_addr();
7886
7887            trace!(
7888                "{} reused CID seq {} of ({},{}) (path {}) on ({},{})",
7889                self.trace_id,
7890                in_scid_seq,
7891                old_local_addr,
7892                old_peer_addr,
7893                in_scid_pid,
7894                info.to,
7895                info.from
7896            );
7897
7898            // Notify the application.
7899            self.paths
7900                .notify_event(path::PathEvent::ReusedSourceConnectionId(
7901                    in_scid_seq,
7902                    (old_local_addr, old_peer_addr),
7903                    (info.to, info.from),
7904                ));
7905        }
7906
7907        // This is a new path using an unassigned CID; create it!
7908        let mut path = path::Path::new(
7909            info.to,
7910            info.from,
7911            &self.recovery_config,
7912            self.path_challenge_recv_max_queue_len,
7913            MIN_CLIENT_INITIAL_LEN,
7914            false,
7915        );
7916
7917        path.max_send_bytes = buf_len * self.max_amplification_factor;
7918        path.active_scid_seq = Some(in_scid_seq);
7919
7920        // Automatically probes the new path.
7921        path.request_validation();
7922
7923        let pid = self.paths.insert_path(path, self.is_server)?;
7924
7925        // Do not record path reuse.
7926        if in_scid_pid.is_none() {
7927            ids.link_scid_to_path_id(in_scid_seq, pid)?;
7928        }
7929
7930        Ok(pid)
7931    }
7932
7933    /// Selects the path on which the next packet must be sent.
7934    fn get_send_path_id(
7935        &self, from: Option<SocketAddr>, to: Option<SocketAddr>,
7936    ) -> Result<usize> {
7937        // A probing packet must be sent, but only if the connection is fully
7938        // established.
7939        if self.is_established() {
7940            let mut probing = self
7941                .paths
7942                .iter()
7943                .filter(|(_, p)| from.is_none() || Some(p.local_addr()) == from)
7944                .filter(|(_, p)| to.is_none() || Some(p.peer_addr()) == to)
7945                .filter(|(_, p)| p.active_dcid_seq.is_some())
7946                .filter(|(_, p)| p.probing_required())
7947                .map(|(pid, _)| pid);
7948
7949            if let Some(pid) = probing.next() {
7950                return Ok(pid);
7951            }
7952        }
7953
7954        if let Some((pid, p)) = self.paths.get_active_with_pid() {
7955            if from.is_some() && Some(p.local_addr()) != from {
7956                return Err(Error::Done);
7957            }
7958
7959            if to.is_some() && Some(p.peer_addr()) != to {
7960                return Err(Error::Done);
7961            }
7962
7963            return Ok(pid);
7964        };
7965
7966        Err(Error::InvalidState)
7967    }
7968
7969    /// Sets the path with identifier 'path_id' to be active.
7970    fn set_active_path(
7971        &mut self, path_id: usize, now: time::Instant,
7972    ) -> Result<()> {
7973        if let Ok(old_active_path) = self.paths.get_active_mut() {
7974            for &e in packet::Epoch::epochs(
7975                packet::Epoch::Initial..=packet::Epoch::Application,
7976            ) {
7977                let (lost_packets, lost_bytes) = old_active_path
7978                    .recovery
7979                    .on_path_change(e, now, &self.trace_id);
7980
7981                self.lost_count += lost_packets;
7982                self.lost_bytes += lost_bytes as u64;
7983            }
7984        }
7985
7986        self.paths.set_active_path(path_id)
7987    }
7988
7989    /// Handles potential connection migration.
7990    fn on_peer_migrated(
7991        &mut self, new_pid: usize, disable_dcid_reuse: bool, now: time::Instant,
7992    ) -> Result<()> {
7993        let active_path_id = self.paths.get_active_path_id()?;
7994
7995        if active_path_id == new_pid {
7996            return Ok(());
7997        }
7998
7999        self.set_active_path(new_pid, now)?;
8000
8001        let no_spare_dcid =
8002            self.paths.get_mut(new_pid)?.active_dcid_seq.is_none();
8003
8004        if no_spare_dcid && !disable_dcid_reuse {
8005            self.paths.get_mut(new_pid)?.active_dcid_seq =
8006                self.paths.get_mut(active_path_id)?.active_dcid_seq;
8007        }
8008
8009        Ok(())
8010    }
8011
8012    /// Creates a new client-side path.
8013    fn create_path_on_client(
8014        &mut self, local_addr: SocketAddr, peer_addr: SocketAddr,
8015    ) -> Result<usize> {
8016        if self.is_server {
8017            return Err(Error::InvalidState);
8018        }
8019
8020        // If we use zero-length SCID and go over our local active CID limit,
8021        // the `insert_path()` call will raise an error.
8022        if !self.ids.zero_length_scid() && self.ids.available_scids() == 0 {
8023            return Err(Error::OutOfIdentifiers);
8024        }
8025
8026        // Do we have a spare DCID? If we are using zero-length DCID, just use
8027        // the default having sequence 0 (note that if we exceed our local CID
8028        // limit, the `insert_path()` call will raise an error.
8029        let dcid_seq = if self.ids.zero_length_dcid() {
8030            0
8031        } else {
