11914        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(4));
11915        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
11916
11917        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.len(), 1);
11918        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 1);
11919
11920        // Server shuts down stream.
11921        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_shutdown(4, Shutdown::Read, 42), Ok(()));
11922
11923        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
11924        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
11925
11926        let (len, _) = pipe.server.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
11927
11928        let mut dummy = buf[..len].to_vec();
11929
11930        let frames =
11931            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut dummy[..len]).unwrap();
11932        let mut iter = frames.iter();
11933
11934        assert_eq!(
11935            iter.next(),
11936            Some(&frame::Frame::StopSending {
11937                stream_id: 4,
11938                error_code: 42,
11939            })
11940        );
11941
11942        assert_eq!(pipe.client_recv(&mut buf[..len]), Ok(len));
11943
11944        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
11945
11946        // Sending more data is forbidden.
11947        let mut r = pipe.client.writable();
11948        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(4));
11949        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
11950
11951        assert_eq!(
11952            pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"bye", false),
11953            Err(Error::StreamStopped(42))
11954        );
11955
11956        // Server sends some data, without reading the incoming data, and closes
11957        // the stream.
11958        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(4, b"hello, world", true), Ok(12));
11959        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
11960
11961        // Client reads the data.
11962        let mut r = pipe.client.readable();
11963        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(4));
11964        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
11965
11966        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_recv(4, &mut buf), Ok((12, true)));
11967
11968        // Stream is collected on both sides.
11969        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.len(), 0);
11970        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 0);
11971
11972        assert_eq!(
11973            pipe.server.stream_shutdown(4, Shutdown::Read, 0),
11974            Err(Error::Done)
11975        );
11976    }
11977
11978    #[test]
11979    fn stream_shutdown_read_after_fin() {
11980        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
11981
11982        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
11983        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
11984
11985        // Client sends some data.
11986        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"hello, world", true), Ok(12));
11987        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
11988
11989        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
11990        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(4));
11991        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
11992
11993        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.len(), 1);
11994        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 1);
11995
11996        // Server shuts down stream.
11997        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_shutdown(4, Shutdown::Read, 42), Ok(()));
11998
11999        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12000        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12001
12002        // Server has nothing to send.
12003        assert_eq!(pipe.server.send(&mut buf), Err(Error::Done));
12004
12005        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12006
12007        // Server sends some data, without reading the incoming data, and closes
12008        // the stream.
12009        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(4, b"hello, world", true), Ok(12));
12010        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12011
12012        // Client reads the data.
12013        let mut r = pipe.client.readable();
12014        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(4));
12015        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12016
12017        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_recv(4, &mut buf), Ok((12, true)));
12018
12019        // Stream is collected on both sides.
12020        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.len(), 0);
12021        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 0);
12022
12023        assert_eq!(
12024            pipe.server.stream_shutdown(4, Shutdown::Read, 0),
12025            Err(Error::Done)
12026        );
12027    }
12028
12029    #[test]
12030    fn stream_shutdown_read_update_max_data() {
12031        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
12032
12033        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
12034        config
12035            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
12036            .unwrap();
12037        config
12038            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
12039            .unwrap();
12040        config
12041            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
12042            .unwrap();
12043        config.set_initial_max_data(30);
12044        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(10000);
12045        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(10000);
12046        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(10);
12047        config.verify_peer(false);
12048
12049        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
12050        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12051
12052        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"a", false), Ok(1));
12053        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12054
12055        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut buf), Ok((1, false)));
12056        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_shutdown(0, Shutdown::Read, 123), Ok(()));
12057
12058        assert_eq!(pipe.server.rx_data, 1);
12059        assert_eq!(pipe.client.tx_data, 1);
12060        assert_eq!(pipe.client.max_tx_data, 30);
12061
12062        assert_eq!(
12063            pipe.client
12064                .stream_send(0, &buf[..pipe.client.tx_cap], false),
12065            Ok(29)
12066        );
12067        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12068
12069        assert!(!pipe.server.stream_readable(0)); // nothing can be consumed
12070
12071        // The client has increased its tx_data, and server has received it, so
12072        // it increases flow control accordingly.
12073        assert_eq!(pipe.client.tx_data, 30);
12074        assert_eq!(pipe.server.rx_data, 30);
12075        assert_eq!(pipe.client.tx_cap, 45);
12076    }
12077
12078    #[test]
12079    fn stream_shutdown_uni() {
12080        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12081        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12082
12083        // Exchange some data on uni streams.
12084        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(2, b"hello, world", false), Ok(10));
12085        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(3, b"hello, world", false), Ok(10));
12086        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12087
12088        // Test local and remote shutdown.
12089        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_shutdown(2, Shutdown::Write, 42), Ok(()));
12090        assert_eq!(
12091            pipe.client.stream_shutdown(2, Shutdown::Read, 42),
12092            Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(2))
12093        );
12094
12095        assert_eq!(
12096            pipe.client.stream_shutdown(3, Shutdown::Write, 42),
12097            Err(Error::InvalidStreamState(3))
12098        );
12099        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_shutdown(3, Shutdown::Read, 42), Ok(()));
12100    }
12101
12102    #[test]
12103    fn stream_shutdown_write() {
12104        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
12105
12106        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12107        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12108
12109        // Client sends some data.
12110        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"hello, world", false), Ok(12));
12111        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12112
12113        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12114        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(4));
12115        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12116
12117        let mut r = pipe.server.writable();
12118        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(4));
12119        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12120
12121        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.len(), 1);
12122        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 1);
12123
12124        // Server sends some data.
12125        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(4, b"goodbye, world", false), Ok(14));
12126        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12127
12128        // Server shuts down stream.
12129        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_shutdown(4, Shutdown::Write, 42), Ok(()));
12130
12131        let mut r = pipe.server.writable();
12132        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12133
12134        let (len, _) = pipe.server.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
12135
12136        let mut dummy = buf[..len].to_vec();
12137
12138        let frames =
12139            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut dummy[..len]).unwrap();
12140        let mut iter = frames.iter();
12141
12142        assert_eq!(
12143            iter.next(),
12144            Some(&frame::Frame::ResetStream {
12145                stream_id: 4,
12146                error_code: 42,
12147                final_size: 14,
12148            })
12149        );
12150
12151        assert_eq!(pipe.client_recv(&mut buf[..len]), Ok(len));
12152
12153        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12154
12155        // Sending more data is forbidden.
12156        assert_eq!(
12157            pipe.server.stream_send(4, b"bye", false),
12158            Err(Error::FinalSize)
12159        );
12160
12161        // Client sends some data and closes the stream.
12162        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"bye", true), Ok(3));
12163        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12164
12165        // Server reads the data.
12166        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12167        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(4));
12168        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12169
12170        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_recv(4, &mut buf), Ok((15, true)));
12171
12172        // Client processes readable streams.
12173        let mut r = pipe.client.readable();
12174        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(4));
12175        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12176
12177        assert_eq!(
12178            pipe.client.stream_recv(4, &mut buf),
12179            Err(Error::StreamReset(42))
12180        );
12181
12182        // Stream is collected on both sides.
12183        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.len(), 0);
12184        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 0);
12185
12186        assert_eq!(
12187            pipe.server.stream_shutdown(4, Shutdown::Write, 0),
12188            Err(Error::Done)
12189        );
12190    }
12191
12192    #[test]
12193    /// Tests that shutting down a stream restores flow control for unsent data.
12194    fn stream_shutdown_write_unsent_tx_cap() {
12195        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
12196        config
12197            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
12198            .unwrap();
12199        config
12200            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
12201            .unwrap();
12202        config
12203            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
12204            .unwrap();
12205        config.set_initial_max_data(15);
12206        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(30);
12207        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(30);
12208        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_uni(30);
12209        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(3);
12210        config.set_initial_max_streams_uni(0);
12211        config.verify_peer(false);
12212
12213        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
12214        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12215
12216        // Client sends some data.
12217        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
12218        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12219
12220        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12221        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(4));
12222        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12223
12224        let mut b = [0; 15];
12225        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_recv(4, &mut b), Ok((5, true)));
12226
12227        // Server sends some data.
12228        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(4, b"hello", false), Ok(5));
12229        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12230
12231        // Server buffers some data, until send capacity limit reached.
12232        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(4, b"hello", false), Ok(5));
12233        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(4, b"hello", false), Ok(5));
12234        assert_eq!(
12235            pipe.server.stream_send(4, b"hello", false),
12236            Err(Error::Done)
12237        );
12238
12239        // Client shouldn't update flow control.
12240        assert!(!pipe.client.should_update_max_data());
12241
12242        // Server shuts down stream.
12243        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_shutdown(4, Shutdown::Write, 42), Ok(()));
12244        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12245
12246        // Server can now send more data (on a different stream).
12247        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(8, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
12248        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12249
12250        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(8, b"hello", false), Ok(5));
12251        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(8, b"hello", false), Ok(5));
12252        assert_eq!(
12253            pipe.server.stream_send(8, b"hello", false),
12254            Err(Error::Done)
12255        );
12256        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12257    }
12258
12259    #[test]
12260    /// Tests that the order of flushable streams scheduled on the wire is the
12261    /// same as the order of `stream_send()` calls done by the application.
12262    fn stream_round_robin() {
12263        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
12264
12265        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12266        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12267
12268        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(8, b"aaaaa", false), Ok(5));
12269        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaa", false), Ok(5));
12270        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"aaaaa", false), Ok(5));
12271
12272        let (len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
12273
12274        let frames =
12275            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.server, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
12276
12277        let mut iter = frames.iter();
12278
12279        // Skip ACK frame.
12280        iter.next();
12281
12282        assert_eq!(
12283            iter.next(),
12284            Some(&frame::Frame::Stream {
12285                stream_id: 8,
12286                data: stream::RangeBuf::from(b"aaaaa", 0, false),
12287            })
12288        );
12289
12290        let (len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
12291
12292        let frames =
12293            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.server, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
12294
12295        assert_eq!(
12296            frames.first(),
12297            Some(&frame::Frame::Stream {
12298                stream_id: 0,
12299                data: stream::RangeBuf::from(b"aaaaa", 0, false),
12300            })
12301        );
12302
12303        let (len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
12304
12305        let frames =
12306            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.server, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
12307
12308        assert_eq!(
12309            frames.first(),
12310            Some(&frame::Frame::Stream {
12311                stream_id: 4,
12312                data: stream::RangeBuf::from(b"aaaaa", 0, false),
12313            })
12314        );
12315    }
12316
12317    #[test]
12318    /// Tests the readable iterator.
12319    fn stream_readable() {
12320        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12321        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12322
12323        // No readable streams.
12324        let mut r = pipe.client.readable();
12325        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12326
12327        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaa", false), Ok(5));
12328
12329        let mut r = pipe.client.readable();
12330        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12331
12332        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12333        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12334
12335        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12336
12337        // Server received stream.
12338        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12339        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
12340        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12341
12342        assert_eq!(
12343            pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa", false),
12344            Ok(15)
12345        );
12346        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12347
12348        let mut r = pipe.client.readable();
12349        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
12350        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12351
12352        // Client drains stream.
12353        let mut b = [0; 15];
12354        pipe.client.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
12355        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12356
12357        let mut r = pipe.client.readable();
12358        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12359
12360        // Server shuts down stream.
12361        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12362        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
12363        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12364
12365        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_shutdown(0, Shutdown::Read, 0), Ok(()));
12366
12367        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12368        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12369
12370        // Client creates multiple streams.
12371        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"aaaaa", false), Ok(5));
12372        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12373
12374        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(8, b"aaaaa", false), Ok(5));
12375        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12376
12377        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12378        assert_eq!(r.len(), 2);
12379
12380        assert!(r.next().is_some());
12381        assert!(r.next().is_some());
12382        assert!(r.next().is_none());
12383
12384        assert_eq!(r.len(), 0);
12385    }
12386
12387    #[test]
12388    /// Tests the writable iterator.
12389    fn stream_writable() {
12390        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12391        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12392
12393        // No writable streams.
12394        let mut w = pipe.client.writable();
12395        assert_eq!(w.next(), None);
12396
12397        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaa", false), Ok(5));
12398
12399        // Client created stream.
12400        let mut w = pipe.client.writable();
12401        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(0));
12402        assert_eq!(w.next(), None);
12403
12404        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12405
12406        // Server created stream.
12407        let mut w = pipe.server.writable();
12408        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(0));
12409        assert_eq!(w.next(), None);
12410
12411        assert_eq!(
12412            pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa", false),
12413            Ok(15)
12414        );
12415
12416        // Server stream is full.
12417        let mut w = pipe.server.writable();
12418        assert_eq!(w.next(), None);
12419
12420        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12421
12422        // Client drains stream.
12423        let mut b = [0; 15];
12424        pipe.client.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
12425        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12426
12427        // Server stream is writable again.
12428        let mut w = pipe.server.writable();
12429        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(0));
12430        assert_eq!(w.next(), None);
12431
12432        // Server shuts down stream.
12433        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_shutdown(0, Shutdown::Write, 0), Ok(()));
12434
12435        let mut w = pipe.server.writable();
12436        assert_eq!(w.next(), None);
12437
12438        // Client creates multiple streams.
12439        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"aaaaa", false), Ok(5));
12440        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12441
12442        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(8, b"aaaaa", false), Ok(5));
12443        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12444
12445        let mut w = pipe.server.writable();
12446        assert_eq!(w.len(), 2);
12447
12448        assert!(w.next().is_some());
12449        assert!(w.next().is_some());
12450        assert!(w.next().is_none());
12451
12452        assert_eq!(w.len(), 0);
12453
12454        // Server finishes stream.
12455        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(8, b"aaaaa", true), Ok(5));
12456
12457        let mut w = pipe.server.writable();
12458        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(4));
12459        assert_eq!(w.next(), None);
12460    }
12461
12462    #[test]
12463    fn stream_writable_blocked() {
12464        let mut config = crate::Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
12465        config
12466            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
12467            .unwrap();
12468        config
12469            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
12470            .unwrap();
12471        config.set_application_protos(&[b"h3"]).unwrap();
12472        config.set_initial_max_data(70);
12473        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(150000);
12474        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(150000);
12475        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_uni(150000);
12476        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(100);
12477        config.set_initial_max_streams_uni(5);
12478        config.verify_peer(false);
12479
12480        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
12481        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12482
12483        // Client creates stream and sends some data.
12484        let send_buf = [0; 35];
12485        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, &send_buf, false), Ok(35));
12486
12487        // Stream is still writable as it still has capacity.
12488        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_writable_next(), Some(0));
12489        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_writable_next(), None);
12490
12491        // Client fills stream, which becomes unwritable due to connection
12492        // capacity.
12493        let send_buf = [0; 36];
12494        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, &send_buf, false), Ok(35));
12495
12496        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_writable_next(), None);
12497
12498        assert_eq!(pipe.client.tx_cap, 0);
12499
12500        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12501
12502        let mut b = [0; 70];
12503        pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
12504
12505        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12506
12507        // The connection capacity has increased and the stream is now writable
12508        // again.
12509        assert_ne!(pipe.client.tx_cap, 0);
12510
12511        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_writable_next(), Some(0));
12512        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_writable_next(), None);
12513    }
12514
12515    #[test]
12516    /// Tests that we don't exceed the per-connection flow control limit set by
12517    /// the peer.
12518    fn flow_control_limit_send() {
12519        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12520        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12521
12522        assert_eq!(
12523            pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa", false),
12524            Ok(15)
12525        );
12526        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12527        assert_eq!(
12528            pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa", false),
12529            Ok(15)
12530        );
12531        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12532        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(8, b"a", false), Err(Error::Done));
12533        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12534
12535        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12536        assert!(r.next().is_some());
12537        assert!(r.next().is_some());
12538        assert!(r.next().is_none());
12539    }
12540
12541    #[test]
12542    /// Tests that invalid packets received before any other valid ones cause
12543    /// the server to close the connection immediately.
12544    fn invalid_initial_server() {
12545        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
12546        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12547
12548        let frames = [frame::Frame::Padding { len: 10 }];
12549
12550        let written = testing::encode_pkt(
12551            &mut pipe.client,
12552            packet::Type::Initial,
12553            &frames,
12554            &mut buf,
12555        )
12556        .unwrap();
12557
12558        // Corrupt the packets's last byte to make decryption fail (the last
12559        // byte is part of the AEAD tag, so changing it means that the packet
12560        // cannot be authenticated during decryption).
12561        buf[written - 1] = !buf[written - 1];
12562
12563        assert_eq!(pipe.server.timeout(), None);
12564
12565        assert_eq!(
12566            pipe.server_recv(&mut buf[..written]),
12567            Err(Error::CryptoFail)
12568        );
12569
12570        assert!(pipe.server.is_closed());
12571    }
12572
12573    #[test]
12574    /// Tests that invalid Initial packets received to cause
12575    /// the client to close the connection immediately.
12576    fn invalid_initial_client() {
12577        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
12578        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12579
12580        // Client sends initial flight.
12581        let (len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
12582
12583        // Server sends initial flight.
12584        assert_eq!(pipe.server_recv(&mut buf[..len]), Ok(1200));
12585
12586        let frames = [frame::Frame::Padding { len: 10 }];
12587
12588        let written = testing::encode_pkt(
12589            &mut pipe.server,
12590            packet::Type::Initial,
12591            &frames,
12592            &mut buf,
12593        )
12594        .unwrap();
12595
12596        // Corrupt the packets's last byte to make decryption fail (the last
12597        // byte is part of the AEAD tag, so changing it means that the packet
12598        // cannot be authenticated during decryption).
12599        buf[written - 1] = !buf[written - 1];
12600
12601        // Client will ignore invalid packet.
12602        assert_eq!(pipe.client_recv(&mut buf[..written]), Ok(71));
12603
12604        // The connection should be alive...
12605        assert!(!pipe.client.is_closed());
12606
12607        // ...and the idle timeout should be armed.
12608        assert!(pipe.client.idle_timer.is_some());
12609    }
12610
12611    #[test]
12612    /// Tests that packets with invalid payload length received before any other
12613    /// valid packet cause the server to close the connection immediately.
12614    fn invalid_initial_payload() {
12615        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
12616        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12617
12618        let mut b = octets::OctetsMut::with_slice(&mut buf);
12619
12620        let epoch = packet::Type::Initial.to_epoch().unwrap();
12621
12622        let pn = 0;
12623        let pn_len = packet::pkt_num_len(pn, 0);
12624
12625        let dcid = pipe.client.destination_id();
12626        let scid = pipe.client.source_id();
12627
12628        let hdr = Header {
12629            ty: packet::Type::Initial,
12630            version: pipe.client.version,
12631            dcid: ConnectionId::from_ref(&dcid),
12632            scid: ConnectionId::from_ref(&scid),
12633            pkt_num: 0,
12634            pkt_num_len: pn_len,
12635            token: pipe.client.token.clone(),
12636            versions: None,
12637            key_phase: false,
12638        };
12639
12640        hdr.to_bytes(&mut b).unwrap();
12641
12642        // Payload length is invalid!!!
12643        let payload_len = 4096;
12644
12645        let len = pn_len + payload_len;
12646        b.put_varint(len as u64).unwrap();
12647
12648        packet::encode_pkt_num(pn, pn_len, &mut b).unwrap();
12649
12650        let payload_offset = b.off();
12651
12652        let frames = [frame::Frame::Padding { len: 10 }];
12653
12654        for frame in &frames {
12655            frame.to_bytes(&mut b).unwrap();
12656        }
12657
12658        let space = &mut pipe.client.pkt_num_spaces[epoch];
12659
12660        // Use correct payload length when encrypting the packet.
12661        let payload_len = frames.iter().fold(0, |acc, x| acc + x.wire_len());
12662
12663        let aead = space.crypto_seal.as_ref().unwrap();
12664
12665        let written = packet::encrypt_pkt(
12666            &mut b,
12667            pn,
12668            pn_len,
12669            payload_len,
12670            payload_offset,
12671            None,
12672            aead,
12673        )
12674        .unwrap();
12675
12676        assert_eq!(pipe.server.timeout(), None);
12677
12678        assert_eq!(
12679            pipe.server_recv(&mut buf[..written]),
12680            Err(Error::InvalidPacket)
12681        );
12682
12683        assert!(pipe.server.is_closed());
12684    }
12685
12686    #[test]
12687    /// Tests that invalid packets don't cause the connection to be closed.
12688    fn invalid_packet() {
12689        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
12690
12691        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12692        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12693
12694        let frames = [frame::Frame::Padding { len: 10 }];
12695
12696        let written = testing::encode_pkt(
12697            &mut pipe.client,
12698            packet::Type::Short,
12699            &frames,
12700            &mut buf,
12701        )
12702        .unwrap();
12703
12704        // Corrupt the packets's last byte to make decryption fail (the last
12705        // byte is part of the AEAD tag, so changing it means that the packet
12706        // cannot be authenticated during decryption).
12707        buf[written - 1] = !buf[written - 1];
12708
12709        assert_eq!(pipe.server_recv(&mut buf[..written]), Ok(written));
12710
12711        // Corrupt the packets's first byte to make the header fail decoding.
12712        buf[0] = 255;
12713
12714        assert_eq!(pipe.server_recv(&mut buf[..written]), Ok(written));
12715    }
12716
12717    #[test]
12718    fn recv_empty_buffer() {
12719        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
12720
12721        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12722        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12723
12724        assert_eq!(pipe.server_recv(&mut buf[..0]), Err(Error::BufferTooShort));
12725    }
12726
12727    #[test]
12728    fn stop_sending_before_flushed_packets() {
12729        let mut b = [0; 15];
12730
12731        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
12732
12733        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
12734        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12735
12736        // Client sends some data, and closes stream.
12737        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
12738        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12739
12740        // Server gets data.
12741        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12742        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
12743        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12744
12745        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b), Ok((5, true)));
12746        assert!(pipe.server.stream_finished(0));
12747
12748        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12749        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12750
12751        // Server sends data, until blocked.
12752        let mut r = pipe.server.writable();
12753        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
12754        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12755
12756        while pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"world", false) != Err(Error::Done) {}
12757
12758        let mut r = pipe.server.writable();
12759        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12760
12761        // Client sends STOP_SENDING.
12762        let frames = [frame::Frame::StopSending {
12763            stream_id: 0,
12764            error_code: 42,
12765        }];
12766
12767        let pkt_type = packet::Type::Short;
12768        let len = pipe
12769            .send_pkt_to_server(pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf)
12770            .unwrap();
12771
12772        // Server sent a RESET_STREAM frame in response.
12773        let frames =
12774            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
12775
12776        let mut iter = frames.iter();
12777
12778        // Skip ACK frame.
12779        iter.next();
12780
12781        assert_eq!(
12782            iter.next(),
12783            Some(&frame::Frame::ResetStream {
12784                stream_id: 0,
12785                error_code: 42,
12786                final_size: 0,
12787            })
12788        );
12789
12790        // Stream is writable, but writing returns an error.
12791        let mut r = pipe.server.writable();
12792        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
12793        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12794
12795        assert_eq!(
12796            pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"world", true),
12797            Err(Error::StreamStopped(42)),
12798        );
12799
12800        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 1);
12801
12802        // Client acks RESET_STREAM frame.
12803        let mut ranges = ranges::RangeSet::default();
12804        ranges.insert(0..6);
12805
12806        let frames = [frame::Frame::ACK {
12807            ack_delay: 15,
12808            ranges,
12809            ecn_counts: None,
12810        }];
12811
12812        assert_eq!(pipe.send_pkt_to_server(pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf), Ok(0));
12813
12814        // Client has ACK'd the RESET_STREAM so the stream is collected.
12815        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 0);
12816    }
12817
12818    #[test]
12819    fn reset_before_flushed_packets() {
12820        let mut b = [0; 15];
12821
12822        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
12823        config
12824            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
12825            .unwrap();
12826        config
12827            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
12828            .unwrap();
12829        config
12830            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
12831            .unwrap();
12832        config.set_initial_max_data(30);
12833        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(5);
12834        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(15);
12835        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(3);
12836        config.verify_peer(false);
12837
12838        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
12839        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12840
12841        // Client sends some data, and closes stream.
12842        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
12843        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12844
12845        // Server gets data.
12846        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12847        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
12848        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12849
12850        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b), Ok((5, true)));
12851        assert!(pipe.server.stream_finished(0));
12852
12853        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
12854        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12855
12856        // Server sends data and is blocked by small stream flow control.
12857        let mut r = pipe.server.writable();
12858        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
12859        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
12860
12861        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"helloworld", false), Ok(5));
12862        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12863
12864        // Client reads to give flow control back.
12865        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_recv(0, &mut b), Ok((5, false)));
12866        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12867
12868        // Server writes stream data and resets the stream before sending a
12869        // packet.
12870        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"world", false), Ok(5));
12871        pipe.server.stream_shutdown(0, Shutdown::Write, 42).unwrap();
12872        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12873
12874        // Client has ACK'd the RESET_STREAM so the stream is collected.
12875        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 0);
12876    }
12877
12878    #[test]
12879    /// Tests that the MAX_STREAMS frame is sent for bidirectional streams.
12880    fn stream_limit_update_bidi() {
12881        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
12882        config
12883            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
12884            .unwrap();
12885        config
12886            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
12887            .unwrap();
12888        config
12889            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
12890            .unwrap();
12891        config.set_initial_max_data(30);
12892        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(15);
12893        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(15);
12894        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_uni(10);
12895        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(3);
12896        config.set_initial_max_streams_uni(0);
12897        config.verify_peer(false);
12898
12899        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
12900        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12901
12902        // Client sends stream data.
12903        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"a", false), Ok(1));
12904        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12905
12906        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"a", false), Ok(1));
12907        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12908
12909        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"b", true), Ok(1));
12910        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12911
12912        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"b", true), Ok(1));
12913        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12914
12915        // Server reads stream data.
12916        let mut b = [0; 15];
12917        pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
12918        pipe.server.stream_recv(4, &mut b).unwrap();
12919        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12920
12921        // Server sends stream data, with fin.
12922        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"a", false), Ok(1));
12923        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12924
12925        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(4, b"a", false), Ok(1));
12926        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12927
12928        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(4, b"b", true), Ok(1));
12929        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12930
12931        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"b", true), Ok(1));
12932
12933        // Server sends MAX_STREAMS.
12934        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12935
12936        // Client tries to create new streams.
12937        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(8, b"a", false), Ok(1));
12938        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12939
12940        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(12, b"a", false), Ok(1));
12941        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12942
12943        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(16, b"a", false), Ok(1));
12944        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12945
12946        assert_eq!(
12947            pipe.client.stream_send(20, b"a", false),
12948            Err(Error::StreamLimit)
12949        );
12950
12951        assert_eq!(pipe.server.readable().len(), 3);
12952    }
12953
12954    #[test]
12955    /// Tests that the MAX_STREAMS frame is sent for unidirectional streams.
12956    fn stream_limit_update_uni() {
12957        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
12958        config
12959            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
12960            .unwrap();
12961        config
12962            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
12963            .unwrap();
12964        config
12965            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
12966            .unwrap();
12967        config.set_initial_max_data(30);
12968        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(15);
12969        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(15);
12970        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_uni(10);
12971        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(0);
12972        config.set_initial_max_streams_uni(3);
12973        config.verify_peer(false);
12974
12975        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
12976        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
12977
12978        // Client sends stream data.
12979        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(2, b"a", false), Ok(1));
12980        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12981
12982        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(6, b"a", false), Ok(1));
12983        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12984
12985        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(6, b"b", true), Ok(1));
12986        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12987
12988        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(2, b"b", true), Ok(1));
12989        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12990
12991        // Server reads stream data.
12992        let mut b = [0; 15];
12993        pipe.server.stream_recv(2, &mut b).unwrap();
12994        pipe.server.stream_recv(6, &mut b).unwrap();
12995
12996        // Server sends MAX_STREAMS.
12997        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
12998
12999        // Client tries to create new streams.
13000        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(10, b"a", false), Ok(1));
13001        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13002
13003        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(14, b"a", false), Ok(1));
13004        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13005
13006        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(18, b"a", false), Ok(1));
13007        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13008
13009        assert_eq!(
13010            pipe.client.stream_send(22, b"a", false),
13011            Err(Error::StreamLimit)
13012        );
13013
13014        assert_eq!(pipe.server.readable().len(), 3);
13015    }
13016
13017    #[test]
13018    /// Tests that the stream's fin flag is properly flushed even if there's no
13019    /// data in the buffer, and that the buffer becomes readable on the other
13020    /// side.
13021    fn stream_zero_length_fin() {
13022        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13023        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13024
13025        assert_eq!(
13026            pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa", false),
13027            Ok(15)
13028        );
13029        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13030
13031        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
13032        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
13033        assert!(r.next().is_none());
13034
13035        let mut b = [0; 15];
13036        pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
13037        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13038
13039        // Client sends zero-length frame.
13040        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"", true), Ok(0));
13041        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13042
13043        // Stream should be readable on the server after receiving empty fin.
13044        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
13045        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
13046        assert!(r.next().is_none());
13047
13048        let mut b = [0; 15];
13049        pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
13050        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13051
13052        // Client sends zero-length frame (again).
13053        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"", true), Ok(0));
13054        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13055
13056        // Stream should _not_ be readable on the server after receiving empty
13057        // fin, because it was already finished.
13058        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
13059        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
13060    }
13061
13062    #[test]
13063    /// Tests that the stream's fin flag is properly flushed even if there's no
13064    /// data in the buffer, that the buffer becomes readable on the other
13065    /// side and stays readable even if the stream is fin'd locally.
13066    fn stream_zero_length_fin_deferred_collection() {
13067        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13068        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13069
13070        assert_eq!(
13071            pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa", false),
13072            Ok(15)
13073        );
13074        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13075
13076        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
13077        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
13078        assert!(r.next().is_none());
13079
13080        let mut b = [0; 15];
13081        pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
13082        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13083
13084        // Client sends zero-length frame.
13085        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"", true), Ok(0));
13086        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13087
13088        // Server sends zero-length frame.
13089        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"", true), Ok(0));
13090        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13091
13092        // Stream should be readable on the server after receiving empty fin.
13093        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
13094        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
13095        assert!(r.next().is_none());
13096
13097        let mut b = [0; 15];
13098        pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
13099        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13100
13101        // Client sends zero-length frame (again).
13102        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"", true), Ok(0));
13103        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13104
13105        // Stream should _not_ be readable on the server after receiving empty
13106        // fin, because it was already finished.
13107        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
13108        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
13109
13110        // Stream _is_readable on the client side.
13111        let mut r = pipe.client.readable();
13112        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
13113
13114        pipe.client.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
13115        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13116
13117        // Stream is completed and _is not_ readable.
13118        let mut r = pipe.client.readable();
13119        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
13120    }
13121
13122    #[test]
13123    /// Tests that the stream gets created with stream_send() even if there's
13124    /// no data in the buffer and the fin flag is not set.
13125    fn stream_zero_length_non_fin() {
13126        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13127        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13128
13129        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"", false), Ok(0));
13130
13131        // The stream now should have been created.
13132        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.len(), 1);
13133        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13134
13135        // Sending an empty non-fin should not change any stream state on the
13136        // other side.
13137        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
13138        assert!(r.next().is_none());
13139    }
13140
13141    #[test]
13142    /// Tests that completed streams are garbage collected.
13143    fn collect_streams() {
13144        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
13145
13146        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13147        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13148
13149        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.len(), 0);
13150        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 0);
13151
13152        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaa", true), Ok(5));
13153        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13154
13155        assert!(!pipe.client.stream_finished(0));
13156        assert!(!pipe.server.stream_finished(0));
13157
13158        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.len(), 1);
13159        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 1);
13160
13161        let mut b = [0; 5];
13162        pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
13163        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13164
13165        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"aaaaa", true), Ok(5));
13166        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13167
13168        assert!(!pipe.client.stream_finished(0));
13169        assert!(pipe.server.stream_finished(0));
13170
13171        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.len(), 1);
13172        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 0);
13173
13174        let mut b = [0; 5];
13175        pipe.client.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
13176        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13177
13178        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.len(), 0);
13179        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 0);
13180
13181        assert!(pipe.client.stream_finished(0));
13182        assert!(pipe.server.stream_finished(0));
13183
13184        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"", true), Err(Error::Done));
13185
13186        let frames = [frame::Frame::Stream {
13187            stream_id: 0,
13188            data: stream::RangeBuf::from(b"aa", 0, false),
13189        }];
13190
13191        let pkt_type = packet::Type::Short;
13192        assert_eq!(pipe.send_pkt_to_server(pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf), Ok(39));
13193    }
13194
13195    #[test]
13196    fn config_set_cc_algorithm_name() {
13197        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13198
13199        assert_eq!(config.set_cc_algorithm_name("reno"), Ok(()));
13200
13201        // Unknown name.
13202        assert_eq!(
13203            config.set_cc_algorithm_name("???"),
13204            Err(Error::CongestionControl)
13205        );
13206    }
13207
13208    #[test]
13209    fn peer_cert() {
13210        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13211        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13212
13213        match pipe.client.peer_cert() {
13214            Some(c) => assert_eq!(c.len(), 753),
13215
13216            None => panic!("missing server certificate"),
13217        }
13218    }
13219
13220    #[test]
13221    fn peer_cert_chain() {
13222        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13223        config
13224            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert-big.crt")
13225            .unwrap();
13226        config
13227            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
13228            .unwrap();
13229        config
13230            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
13231            .unwrap();
13232
13233        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_server_config(&mut config).unwrap();
13234        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13235
13236        match pipe.client.peer_cert_chain() {
13237            Some(c) => assert_eq!(c.len(), 5),
13238
13239            None => panic!("missing server certificate chain"),
13240        }
13241    }
13242
13243    #[test]
13244    fn retry() {
13245        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
13246
13247        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13248        config
13249            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
13250            .unwrap();
13251        config
13252            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
13253            .unwrap();
13254        config
13255            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
13256            .unwrap();
13257
13258        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_server_config(&mut config).unwrap();
13259
13260        // Client sends initial flight.
13261        let (mut len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13262
13263        // Server sends Retry packet.
13264        let hdr = Header::from_slice(&mut buf[..len], MAX_CONN_ID_LEN).unwrap();
13265
13266        let odcid = hdr.dcid.clone();
13267
13268        let mut scid = [0; MAX_CONN_ID_LEN];
13269        rand::rand_bytes(&mut scid[..]);
13270        let scid = ConnectionId::from_ref(&scid);
13271
13272        let token = b"quiche test retry token";
13273
13274        len = packet::retry(
13275            &hdr.scid,
13276            &hdr.dcid,
13277            &scid,
13278            token,
13279            hdr.version,
13280            &mut buf,
13281        )
13282        .unwrap();
13283
13284        // Client receives Retry and sends new Initial.
13285        assert_eq!(pipe.client_recv(&mut buf[..len]), Ok(len));
13286
13287        let (len, send_info) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13288
13289        let hdr = Header::from_slice(&mut buf[..len], MAX_CONN_ID_LEN).unwrap();
13290        assert_eq!(&hdr.token.unwrap(), token);
13291
13292        // Server accepts connection.
13293        pipe.server = accept(
13294            &scid,
13295            Some(&odcid),
13296            testing::Pipe::server_addr(),
13297            send_info.from,
13298            &mut config,
13299        )
13300        .unwrap();
13301        assert_eq!(pipe.server_recv(&mut buf[..len]), Ok(len));
13302
13303        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13304
13305        assert!(pipe.client.is_established());
13306        assert!(pipe.server.is_established());
13307    }
13308
13309    #[test]
13310    fn retry_with_pto() {
13311        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
13312
13313        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13314        config
13315            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
13316            .unwrap();
13317        config
13318            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
13319            .unwrap();
13320        config
13321            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
13322            .unwrap();
13323
13324        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_server_config(&mut config).unwrap();
13325
13326        // Client sends initial flight.
13327        let (mut len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13328
13329        // Server sends Retry packet.
13330        let hdr = Header::from_slice(&mut buf[..len], MAX_CONN_ID_LEN).unwrap();
13331
13332        let odcid = hdr.dcid.clone();
13333
13334        let mut scid = [0; MAX_CONN_ID_LEN];
13335        rand::rand_bytes(&mut scid[..]);
13336        let scid = ConnectionId::from_ref(&scid);
13337
13338        let token = b"quiche test retry token";
13339
13340        len = packet::retry(
13341            &hdr.scid,
13342            &hdr.dcid,
13343            &scid,
13344            token,
13345            hdr.version,
13346            &mut buf,
13347        )
13348        .unwrap();
13349
13350        // Client receives Retry and sends new Initial.
13351        assert_eq!(pipe.client_recv(&mut buf[..len]), Ok(len));
13352
13353        let (len, send_info) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13354
13355        let hdr = Header::from_slice(&mut buf[..len], MAX_CONN_ID_LEN).unwrap();
13356        assert_eq!(&hdr.token.unwrap(), token);
13357
13358        // Server accepts connection.
13359        pipe.server = accept(
13360            &scid,
13361            Some(&odcid),
13362            testing::Pipe::server_addr(),
13363            send_info.from,
13364            &mut config,
13365        )
13366        .unwrap();
13367        assert_eq!(pipe.server_recv(&mut buf[..len]), Ok(len));
13368
13369        // Wait for the client's PTO so it will try to send an Initial again.
13370        let timer = pipe.client.timeout().unwrap();
13371        std::thread::sleep(timer + time::Duration::from_millis(1));
13372        pipe.client.on_timeout();
13373
13374        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13375
13376        assert!(pipe.client.is_established());
13377        assert!(pipe.server.is_established());
13378    }
13379
13380    #[test]
13381    fn missing_retry_source_connection_id() {
13382        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
13383
13384        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13385        config
13386            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
13387            .unwrap();
13388        config
13389            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
13390            .unwrap();
13391        config
13392            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
13393            .unwrap();
13394
13395        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_server_config(&mut config).unwrap();
13396
13397        // Client sends initial flight.
13398        let (mut len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13399
13400        // Server sends Retry packet.
13401        let hdr = Header::from_slice(&mut buf[..len], MAX_CONN_ID_LEN).unwrap();
13402
13403        let mut scid = [0; MAX_CONN_ID_LEN];
13404        rand::rand_bytes(&mut scid[..]);
13405        let scid = ConnectionId::from_ref(&scid);
13406
13407        let token = b"quiche test retry token";
13408
13409        len = packet::retry(
13410            &hdr.scid,
13411            &hdr.dcid,
13412            &scid,
13413            token,
13414            hdr.version,
13415            &mut buf,
13416        )
13417        .unwrap();
13418
13419        // Client receives Retry and sends new Initial.
13420        assert_eq!(pipe.client_recv(&mut buf[..len]), Ok(len));
13421
13422        let (len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13423
13424        // Server accepts connection and send first flight. But original
13425        // destination connection ID is ignored.
13426        let from = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
13427        pipe.server =
13428            accept(&scid, None, testing::Pipe::server_addr(), from, &mut config)
13429                .unwrap();
13430        assert_eq!(pipe.server_recv(&mut buf[..len]), Ok(len));
13431
13432        let flight = testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.server).unwrap();
13433
13434        assert_eq!(
13435            testing::process_flight(&mut pipe.client, flight),
13436            Err(Error::InvalidTransportParam)
13437        );
13438    }
13439
13440    #[test]
13441    fn invalid_retry_source_connection_id() {
13442        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
13443
13444        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13445        config
13446            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
13447            .unwrap();
13448        config
13449            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
13450            .unwrap();
13451        config
13452            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
13453            .unwrap();
13454
13455        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_server_config(&mut config).unwrap();
13456
13457        // Client sends initial flight.
13458        let (mut len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13459
13460        // Server sends Retry packet.
13461        let hdr = Header::from_slice(&mut buf[..len], MAX_CONN_ID_LEN).unwrap();
13462
13463        let mut scid = [0; MAX_CONN_ID_LEN];
13464        rand::rand_bytes(&mut scid[..]);
13465        let scid = ConnectionId::from_ref(&scid);
13466
13467        let token = b"quiche test retry token";
13468
13469        len = packet::retry(
13470            &hdr.scid,
13471            &hdr.dcid,
13472            &scid,
13473            token,
13474            hdr.version,
13475            &mut buf,
13476        )
13477        .unwrap();
13478
13479        // Client receives Retry and sends new Initial.
13480        assert_eq!(pipe.client_recv(&mut buf[..len]), Ok(len));
13481
13482        let (len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13483
13484        // Server accepts connection and send first flight. But original
13485        // destination connection ID is invalid.
13486        let from = "127.0.0.1:1234".parse().unwrap();
13487        let odcid = ConnectionId::from_ref(b"bogus value");
13488        pipe.server = accept(
13489            &scid,
13490            Some(&odcid),
13491            testing::Pipe::server_addr(),
13492            from,
13493            &mut config,
13494        )
13495        .unwrap();
13496        assert_eq!(pipe.server_recv(&mut buf[..len]), Ok(len));
13497
13498        let flight = testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.server).unwrap();
13499
13500        assert_eq!(
13501            testing::process_flight(&mut pipe.client, flight),
13502            Err(Error::InvalidTransportParam)
13503        );
13504    }
13505
13506    #[test]
13507    /// Tests that a zero-length NEW_TOKEN frame is detected as an error.
13508    fn zero_length_new_token() {
13509        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
13510
13511        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13512        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13513
13514        let frames = vec![frame::Frame::NewToken { token: vec![] }];
13515
13516        let pkt_type = packet::Type::Short;
13517
13518        let written =
13519            testing::encode_pkt(&mut pipe.server, pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf)
13520                .unwrap();
13521
13522        assert_eq!(
13523            pipe.client_recv(&mut buf[..written]),
13524            Err(Error::InvalidFrame)
13525        );
13526    }
13527
13528    #[test]
13529    /// Tests that a NEW_TOKEN frame sent by client is detected as an error.
13530    fn client_sent_new_token() {
13531        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
13532
13533        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13534        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13535
13536        let frames = vec![frame::Frame::NewToken {
13537            token: vec![1, 2, 3],
13538        }];
13539
13540        let pkt_type = packet::Type::Short;
13541
13542        let written =
13543            testing::encode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf)
13544                .unwrap();
13545
13546        assert_eq!(
13547            pipe.server_recv(&mut buf[..written]),
13548            Err(Error::InvalidPacket)
13549        );
13550    }
13551
13552    fn check_send(_: &mut impl Send) {}
13553
13554    #[test]
13555    fn config_must_be_send() {
13556        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13557        check_send(&mut config);
13558    }
13559
13560    #[test]
13561    fn connection_must_be_send() {
13562        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13563        check_send(&mut pipe.client);
13564    }
13565
13566    fn check_sync(_: &mut impl Sync) {}
13567
13568    #[test]
13569    fn config_must_be_sync() {
13570        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13571        check_sync(&mut config);

13574    #[test]
13575    fn connection_must_be_sync() {
13576        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13577        check_sync(&mut pipe.client);
13578    }
13579
13580    #[test]
13581    fn data_blocked() {
13582        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
13583
13584        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13585        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13586
13587        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaaaaaaa", false), Ok(10));
13588        assert_eq!(pipe.client.blocked_limit, None);
13589        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13590
13591        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"aaaaaaaaaa", false), Ok(10));
13592        assert_eq!(pipe.client.blocked_limit, None);
13593        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13594
13595        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(8, b"aaaaaaaaaaa", false), Ok(10));
13596        assert_eq!(pipe.client.blocked_limit, Some(30));
13597
13598        let (len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13599        assert_eq!(pipe.client.blocked_limit, None);
13600
13601        let frames =
13602            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.server, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
13603
13604        let mut iter = frames.iter();
13605
13606        assert_eq!(iter.next(), Some(&frame::Frame::DataBlocked { limit: 30 }));
13607
13608        assert_eq!(
13609            iter.next(),
13610            Some(&frame::Frame::Stream {
13611                stream_id: 8,
13612                data: stream::RangeBuf::from(b"aaaaaaaaaa", 0, false),
13613            })
13614        );
13615
13616        assert_eq!(iter.next(), None);
13617    }
13618
13619    #[test]
13620    fn stream_data_blocked() {
13621        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
13622
13623        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13624        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13625
13626        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaa", false), Ok(5));
13627        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 0);
13628
13629        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaa", false), Ok(5));
13630        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 0);
13631
13632        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaaa", false), Ok(5));
13633        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 1);
13634
13635        let (len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13636        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 0);
13637
13638        let frames =
13639            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.server, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
13640
13641        let mut iter = frames.iter();
13642
13643        // Skip ACK frame.
13644        iter.next();
13645
13646        assert_eq!(
13647            iter.next(),
13648            Some(&frame::Frame::StreamDataBlocked {
13649                stream_id: 0,
13650                limit: 15,
13651            })
13652        );
13653
13654        assert_eq!(
13655            iter.next(),
13656            Some(&frame::Frame::Stream {
13657                stream_id: 0,
13658                data: stream::RangeBuf::from(b"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaa", 0, false),
13659            })
13660        );
13661
13662        assert_eq!(iter.next(), None);
13663
13664        // Send from another stream, make sure we don't send STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED
13665        // again.
13666        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"a", false), Ok(1));
13667
13668        let (len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13669        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 0);
13670
13671        let frames =
13672            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.server, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
13673
13674        let mut iter = frames.iter();
13675
13676        assert_eq!(
13677            iter.next(),
13678            Some(&frame::Frame::Stream {
13679                stream_id: 4,
13680                data: stream::RangeBuf::from(b"a", 0, false),
13681            })
13682        );
13683
13684        assert_eq!(iter.next(), None);
13685
13686        // Send again from blocked stream and make sure it is not marked as
13687        // blocked again.
13688        assert_eq!(
13689            pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaaa", false),
13690            Err(Error::Done)
13691        );
13692        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 0);
13693        assert_eq!(pipe.client.send(&mut buf), Err(Error::Done));
13694    }
13695
13696    #[test]
13697    fn stream_data_blocked_unblocked_flow_control() {
13698        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
13699        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
13700        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13701
13702        assert_eq!(
13703            pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"aaaaaaaaaaaaaaah", false),
13704            Ok(15)
13705        );
13706        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 1);
13707        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13708        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 0);
13709
13710        // Send again on blocked stream. It's blocked at the same offset as
13711        // previously, so it should not be marked as blocked again.
13712        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"h", false), Err(Error::Done));
13713        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 0);
13714
13715        // No matter how many times we try to write stream data tried, no
13716        // packets containing STREAM_BLOCKED should be emitted.
13717        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"h", false), Err(Error::Done));
13718        assert_eq!(pipe.client.send(&mut buf), Err(Error::Done));
13719
13720        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"h", false), Err(Error::Done));
13721        assert_eq!(pipe.client.send(&mut buf), Err(Error::Done));
13722
13723        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"h", false), Err(Error::Done));
13724        assert_eq!(pipe.client.send(&mut buf), Err(Error::Done));
13725
13726        // Now read some data at the server to release flow control.
13727        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
13728        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
13729        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
13730
13731        let mut b = [0; 10];
13732        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b), Ok((10, false)));
13733        assert_eq!(&b[..10], b"aaaaaaaaaa");
13734        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13735
13736        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"hhhhhhhhhh!", false), Ok(10));
13737        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 1);
13738
13739        let (len, _) = pipe.client.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
13740        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 0);
13741
13742        let frames =
13743            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.server, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
13744
13745        let mut iter = frames.iter();
13746
13747        assert_eq!(
13748            iter.next(),
13749            Some(&frame::Frame::StreamDataBlocked {
13750                stream_id: 0,
13751                limit: 25,
13752            })
13753        );
13754
13755        // don't care about remaining received frames
13756
13757        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"!", false), Err(Error::Done));
13758        assert_eq!(pipe.client.streams.blocked().len(), 0);
13759        assert_eq!(pipe.client.send(&mut buf), Err(Error::Done));
13760    }
13761
13762    #[test]
13763    fn app_limited_true() {
13764        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13765        config
13766            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
13767            .unwrap();
13768        config.set_initial_max_data(50000);
13769        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(50000);
13770        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(50000);
13771        config.set_max_recv_udp_payload_size(1200);
13772        config.verify_peer(false);
13773
13774        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_client_config(&mut config).unwrap();
13775        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13776
13777        // Client sends stream data.
13778        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"a", true), Ok(1));
13779        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13780
13781        // Server reads stream data.
13782        let mut b = [0; 15];
13783        pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
13784        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13785
13786        // Server sends stream data smaller than cwnd.
13787        let send_buf = [0; 10000];
13788        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(0, &send_buf, false), Ok(10000));
13789        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13790
13791        // app_limited should be true because we send less than cwnd.
13792        assert!(pipe
13793            .server
13794            .paths
13795            .get_active()
13796            .expect("no active")
13797            .recovery
13798            .app_limited());
13799    }
13800
13801    #[test]
13802    fn app_limited_false() {
13803        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13804        config
13805            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
13806            .unwrap();
13807        config.set_initial_max_data(50000);
13808        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(50000);
13809        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(50000);
13810        config.set_max_recv_udp_payload_size(1200);
13811        config.verify_peer(false);
13812
13813        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_client_config(&mut config).unwrap();
13814        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13815
13816        // Client sends stream data.
13817        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"a", true), Ok(1));
13818        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13819
13820        // Server reads stream data.
13821        let mut b = [0; 15];
13822        pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
13823        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
13824
13825        // Server sends stream data bigger than cwnd.
13826        let send_buf1 = [0; 20000];
13827        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(0, &send_buf1, false), Ok(12000));
13828
13829        testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.server).ok();
13830
13831        // We can't create a new packet header because there is no room by cwnd.
13832        // app_limited should be false because we can't send more by cwnd.
13833        assert!(!pipe
13834            .server
13835            .paths
13836            .get_active()
13837            .expect("no active")
13838            .recovery
13839            .app_limited());
13840    }
13841
13842    #[test]
13843    fn sends_ack_only_pkt_when_full_cwnd_and_ack_elicited() {
13844        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13845        config
13846            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
13847            .unwrap();
13848        config
13849            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
13850            .unwrap();
13851        config
13852            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
13853            .unwrap();
13854        config.set_initial_max_data(50000);
13855        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(50000);
13856        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(50000);
13857        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(3);
13858        config.set_initial_max_streams_uni(3);
13859        config.set_max_recv_udp_payload_size(1200);
13860        config.verify_peer(false);
13861
13862        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
13863        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13864
13865        // Client sends stream data bigger than cwnd (it will never arrive to the
13866        // server).
13867        let send_buf1 = [0; 20000];
13868        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, &send_buf1, false), Ok(12000));
13869
13870        testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.client).ok();
13871
13872        // Server sends some stream data that will need ACKs.
13873        assert_eq!(
13874            pipe.server.stream_send(1, &send_buf1[..500], false),
13875            Ok(500)
13876        );
13877
13878        testing::process_flight(
13879            &mut pipe.client,
13880            testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.server).unwrap(),
13881        )
13882        .unwrap();
13883
13884        let mut buf = [0; 2000];
13885
13886        let ret = pipe.client.send(&mut buf);
13887
13888        assert_eq!(pipe.client.tx_cap, 0);
13889
13890        assert!(matches!(ret, Ok((_, _))), "the client should at least send one packet to acknowledge the newly received data");
13891
13892        let (sent, _) = ret.unwrap();
13893
13894        assert_ne!(sent, 0, "the client should at least send a pure ACK packet");
13895
13896        let frames =
13897            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.server, &mut buf[..sent]).unwrap();
13898        assert_eq!(1, frames.len());
13899        assert!(
13900            matches!(frames[0], frame::Frame::ACK { .. }),
13901            "the packet sent by the client must be an ACK only packet"
13902        );
13903    }
13904
13905    /// Like sends_ack_only_pkt_when_full_cwnd_and_ack_elicited, but when
13906    /// ack_eliciting is explicitly requested.
13907    #[test]
13908    fn sends_ack_only_pkt_when_full_cwnd_and_ack_elicited_despite_max_unacknowledging(
13909    ) {
13910        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13911        config
13912            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
13913            .unwrap();
13914        config
13915            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
13916            .unwrap();
13917        config
13918            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
13919            .unwrap();
13920        config.set_initial_max_data(50000);
13921        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(50000);
13922        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(50000);
13923        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(3);
13924        config.set_initial_max_streams_uni(3);
13925        config.set_max_recv_udp_payload_size(1200);
13926        config.verify_peer(false);
13927
13928        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
13929        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13930
13931        // Client sends stream data bigger than cwnd (it will never arrive to the
13932        // server). This exhausts the congestion window.
13933        let send_buf1 = [0; 20000];
13934        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, &send_buf1, false), Ok(12000));
13935
13936        testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.client).ok();
13937
13938        // Client gets PING frames from server, which elicit ACK
13939        let mut buf = [0; 2000];
13940        for _ in 0..recovery::MAX_OUTSTANDING_NON_ACK_ELICITING {
13941            let written = testing::encode_pkt(
13942                &mut pipe.server,
13943                packet::Type::Short,
13944                &[frame::Frame::Ping { mtu_probe: None }],
13945                &mut buf,
13946            )
13947            .unwrap();
13948
13949            pipe.client_recv(&mut buf[..written])
13950                .expect("client recv ping");
13951
13952            // Client acknowledges despite a full congestion window
13953            let ret = pipe.client.send(&mut buf);
13954
13955            assert!(matches!(ret, Ok((_, _))), "the client should at least send one packet to acknowledge the newly received data");
13956
13957            let (sent, _) = ret.unwrap();
13958
13959            assert_ne!(
13960                sent, 0,
13961                "the client should at least send a pure ACK packet"
13962            );
13963
13964            let frames =
13965                testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.server, &mut buf[..sent]).unwrap();
13966
13967            assert_eq!(1, frames.len());
13968
13969            assert!(
13970                matches!(frames[0], frame::Frame::ACK { .. }),
13971                "the packet sent by the client must be an ACK only packet"
13972            );
13973        }
13974
13975        // The client shouldn't need to send any more packets after the ACK only
13976        // packet it just sent.
13977        assert_eq!(
13978            pipe.client.send(&mut buf),
13979            Err(Error::Done),
13980            "nothing for client to send after ACK-only packet"
13981        );
13982    }
13983
13984    #[test]
13985    fn app_limited_false_no_frame() {
13986        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
13987        config
13988            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
13989            .unwrap();
13990        config.set_initial_max_data(50000);
13991        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(50000);
13992        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(50000);
13993        config.set_max_recv_udp_payload_size(1405);
13994        config.verify_peer(false);
13995
13996        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_client_config(&mut config).unwrap();
13997        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
13998
13999        // Client sends stream data.
14000        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"a", true), Ok(1));
14001        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
14002
14003        // Server reads stream data.
14004        let mut b = [0; 15];
14005        pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b).unwrap();
14006        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
14007
14008        // Server sends stream data bigger than cwnd.
14009        let send_buf1 = [0; 20000];
14010        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(0, &send_buf1, false), Ok(12000));
14011
14012        testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.server).ok();
14013
14014        // We can't create a new packet header because there is no room by cwnd.
14015        // app_limited should be false because we can't send more by cwnd.
14016        assert!(!pipe
14017            .server
14018            .paths
14019            .get_active()
14020            .expect("no active")
14021            .recovery
14022            .app_limited());
14023    }
14024
14025    #[test]
14026    fn app_limited_false_no_header() {
14027        let mut config = Config::new(PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
14028        config
14029            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
