16064            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16065            .unwrap();
16066        config
16067            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16068            .unwrap();
16069        config
16070            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16071            .unwrap();
16072        config.verify_peer(false);
16073        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
16074
16075        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
16076        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
16077
16078        let mut frames = Vec::new();
16079
16080        // Client adds a CID that is too short.
16081        let (scid, reset_token) = testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(0);
16082
16083        frames.push(frame::Frame::NewConnectionId {
16084            seq_num: 1,
16085            retire_prior_to: 0,
16086            conn_id: scid.to_vec(),
16087            reset_token: reset_token.to_be_bytes(),
16088        });
16089
16090        let pkt_type = packet::Type::Short;
16091
16092        let written =
16093            testing::encode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf)
16094                .unwrap();
16095
16096        let active_path = pipe.server.paths.get_active().unwrap();
16097        let info = RecvInfo {
16098            to: active_path.local_addr(),
16099            from: active_path.peer_addr(),
16100        };
16101
16102        assert_eq!(
16103            pipe.server.recv(&mut buf[..written], info),
16104            Err(Error::InvalidFrame)
16105        );
16106
16107        let written = match pipe.server.send(&mut buf) {
16108            Ok((write, _)) => write,
16109
16110            Err(_) => unreachable!(),
16111        };
16112
16113        let frames =
16114            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut buf[..written]).unwrap();
16115        let mut iter = frames.iter();
16116
16117        assert_eq!(
16118            iter.next(),
16119            Some(&frame::Frame::ConnectionClose {
16120                error_code: 0x7,
16121                frame_type: 0,
16122                reason: Vec::new(),
16123            })
16124        );
16125    }
16126
16127    #[test]
16128    /// Tests that NEW_CONNECTION_ID with too long CID are rejected.
16129    fn connection_id_invalid_max_len() {
16130        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
16131
16132        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16133        config
16134            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16135            .unwrap();
16136        config
16137            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16138            .unwrap();
16139        config
16140            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16141            .unwrap();
16142        config.verify_peer(false);
16143        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
16144
16145        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
16146        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
16147
16148        let mut frames = Vec::new();
16149
16150        // Client adds a CID that is too long.
16151        let (scid, reset_token) =
16152            testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(MAX_CONN_ID_LEN + 1);
16153
16154        frames.push(frame::Frame::NewConnectionId {
16155            seq_num: 1,
16156            retire_prior_to: 0,
16157            conn_id: scid.to_vec(),
16158            reset_token: reset_token.to_be_bytes(),
16159        });
16160
16161        let pkt_type = packet::Type::Short;
16162
16163        let written =
16164            testing::encode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf)
16165                .unwrap();
16166
16167        let active_path = pipe.server.paths.get_active().unwrap();
16168        let info = RecvInfo {
16169            to: active_path.local_addr(),
16170            from: active_path.peer_addr(),
16171        };
16172
16173        assert_eq!(
16174            pipe.server.recv(&mut buf[..written], info),
16175            Err(Error::InvalidFrame)
16176        );
16177
16178        let written = match pipe.server.send(&mut buf) {
16179            Ok((write, _)) => write,
16180
16181            Err(_) => unreachable!(),
16182        };
16183
16184        let frames =
16185            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut buf[..written]).unwrap();
16186        let mut iter = frames.iter();
16187
16188        assert_eq!(
16189            iter.next(),
16190            Some(&frame::Frame::ConnectionClose {
16191                error_code: 0x7,
16192                frame_type: 0,
16193                reason: Vec::new(),
16194            })
16195        );
16196    }
16197
16198    #[test]
16199    /// Exercises the handling of NEW_CONNECTION_ID and RETIRE_CONNECTION_ID
16200    /// frames.
16201    fn connection_id_handling() {
16202        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16203        config
16204            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16205            .unwrap();
16206        config
16207            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16208            .unwrap();
16209        config
16210            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16211            .unwrap();
16212        config.verify_peer(false);
16213        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
16214
16215        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
16216        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
16217
16218        // So far, there should not have any QUIC event.
16219        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16220        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16221        assert_eq!(pipe.client.scids_left(), 1);
16222
16223        let scid = pipe.client.source_id().into_owned();
16224
16225        let (scid_1, reset_token_1) = testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(16);
16226        assert_eq!(pipe.client.new_scid(&scid_1, reset_token_1, false), Ok(1));
16227
16228        // Let exchange packets over the connection.
16229        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16230
16231        // At this point, the server should be notified that it has a new CID.
16232        assert_eq!(pipe.server.available_dcids(), 1);
16233        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16234        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16235        assert_eq!(pipe.client.scids_left(), 0);
16236
16237        // Now we assume that the client wants to advertise more source
16238        // Connection IDs than the advertised limit. This is valid if it
16239        // requests its peer to retire enough Connection IDs to fit within the
16240        // limits.
16241
16242        let (scid_2, reset_token_2) = testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(16);
16243        assert_eq!(pipe.client.new_scid(&scid_2, reset_token_2, true), Ok(2));
16244
16245        // Let exchange packets over the connection.
16246        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16247
16248        // At this point, the server still have a spare DCID.
16249        assert_eq!(pipe.server.available_dcids(), 1);
16250        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16251
16252        // Client should have received a retired notification.
16253        assert_eq!(pipe.client.retired_scid_next(), Some(scid));
16254        assert_eq!(pipe.client.retired_scid_next(), None);
16255
16256        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16257        assert_eq!(pipe.client.scids_left(), 0);
16258
16259        // The active Destination Connection ID of the server should now be the
16260        // one with sequence number 1.
16261        assert_eq!(pipe.server.destination_id(), scid_1);
16262
16263        // Now tries to experience CID retirement. If the server tries to remove
16264        // non-existing DCIDs, it fails.
16265        assert_eq!(pipe.server.retire_dcid(0), Err(Error::InvalidState));
16266        assert_eq!(pipe.server.retire_dcid(3), Err(Error::InvalidState));
16267
16268        // Now it removes DCID with sequence 1.
16269        assert_eq!(pipe.server.retire_dcid(1), Ok(()));
16270
16271        // Let exchange packets over the connection.
16272        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16273
16274        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16275        assert_eq!(pipe.client.retired_scid_next(), Some(scid_1));
16276        assert_eq!(pipe.client.retired_scid_next(), None);
16277
16278        assert_eq!(pipe.server.destination_id(), scid_2);
16279        assert_eq!(pipe.server.available_dcids(), 0);
16280
16281        // Trying to remove the last DCID triggers an error.
16282        assert_eq!(pipe.server.retire_dcid(2), Err(Error::OutOfIdentifiers));
16283    }
16284
16285    #[test]
16286    fn lost_connection_id_frames() {
16287        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16288        config
16289            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16290            .unwrap();
16291        config
16292            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16293            .unwrap();
16294        config
16295            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16296            .unwrap();
16297        config.verify_peer(false);
16298        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
16299
16300        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
16301        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
16302
16303        let scid = pipe.client.source_id().into_owned();
16304
16305        let (scid_1, reset_token_1) = testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(16);
16306        assert_eq!(pipe.client.new_scid(&scid_1, reset_token_1, false), Ok(1));
16307
16308        // Packets are sent, but never received.
16309        testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.client).unwrap();
16310
16311        // Wait until timer expires. Since the RTT is very low, wait a bit more.
16312        let timer = pipe.client.timeout().unwrap();
16313        std::thread::sleep(timer + time::Duration::from_millis(1));
16314
16315        pipe.client.on_timeout();
16316
16317        // Let exchange packets over the connection.
16318        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16319
16320        // At this point, the server should be notified that it has a new CID.
16321        assert_eq!(pipe.server.available_dcids(), 1);
16322
16323        // Now the server retires the first Destination CID.
16324        assert_eq!(pipe.server.retire_dcid(0), Ok(()));
16325
16326        // But the packet never reaches the client.
16327        testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.server).unwrap();
16328
16329        // Wait until timer expires. Since the RTT is very low, wait a bit more.
16330        let timer = pipe.server.timeout().unwrap();
16331        std::thread::sleep(timer + time::Duration::from_millis(1));
16332
16333        pipe.server.on_timeout();
16334
16335        // Let exchange packets over the connection.
16336        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16337
16338        assert_eq!(pipe.client.retired_scid_next(), Some(scid));
16339        assert_eq!(pipe.client.retired_scid_next(), None);
16340    }
16341
16342    #[test]
16343    fn sending_duplicate_scids() {
16344        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16345        config
16346            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16347            .unwrap();
16348        config
16349            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16350            .unwrap();
16351        config
16352            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16353            .unwrap();
16354        config.verify_peer(false);
16355        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(3);
16356
16357        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
16358        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
16359
16360        let (scid_1, reset_token_1) = testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(16);
16361        assert_eq!(pipe.client.new_scid(&scid_1, reset_token_1, false), Ok(1));
16362        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16363
16364        // Trying to send the same CID with a different reset token raises an
16365        // InvalidState error.
16366        let reset_token_2 = reset_token_1.wrapping_add(1);
16367        assert_eq!(
16368            pipe.client.new_scid(&scid_1, reset_token_2, false),
16369            Err(Error::InvalidState),
16370        );
16371
16372        // Retrying to send the exact same CID with the same token returns the
16373        // previously assigned CID seq, but without sending anything.
16374        assert_eq!(pipe.client.new_scid(&scid_1, reset_token_1, false), Ok(1));
16375        assert!(!pipe.client.ids.has_new_scids());
16376
16377        // Now retire this new CID.
16378        assert_eq!(pipe.server.retire_dcid(1), Ok(()));
16379        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16380
16381        // It is up to the application to ensure that a given SCID is not reused
16382        // later.
16383        assert_eq!(pipe.client.new_scid(&scid_1, reset_token_1, false), Ok(2));
16384    }
16385
16386    #[test]
16387    /// Tests the limit to retired DCID sequence numbers.
16388    fn connection_id_retire_limit() {
16389        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
16390
16391        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16392        config
16393            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16394            .unwrap();
16395        config
16396            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16397            .unwrap();
16398        config
16399            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16400            .unwrap();
16401        config.verify_peer(false);
16402        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
16403
16404        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
16405        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
16406
16407        // So far, there should not have any QUIC event.
16408        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16409        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16410        assert_eq!(pipe.client.scids_left(), 1);
16411
16412        let (scid_1, reset_token_1) = testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(16);
16413        assert_eq!(pipe.client.new_scid(&scid_1, reset_token_1, false), Ok(1));
16414
16415        // Let exchange packets over the connection.
16416        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16417
16418        // At this point, the server should be notified that it has a new CID.
16419        assert_eq!(pipe.server.available_dcids(), 1);
16420        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16421        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16422        assert_eq!(pipe.client.scids_left(), 0);
16423
16424        let mut frames = Vec::new();
16425
16426        // Client retires more than 3x the number of allowed active CIDs.
16427        for i in 2..=7 {
16428            let (scid, reset_token) = testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(16);
16429
16430            frames.push(frame::Frame::NewConnectionId {
16431                seq_num: i,
16432                retire_prior_to: i,
16433                conn_id: scid.to_vec(),
16434                reset_token: reset_token.to_be_bytes(),
16435            });
16436        }
16437
16438        let pkt_type = packet::Type::Short;
16439
16440        let written =
16441            testing::encode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf)
16442                .unwrap();
16443
16444        let active_path = pipe.server.paths.get_active().unwrap();
16445        let info = RecvInfo {
16446            to: active_path.local_addr(),
16447            from: active_path.peer_addr(),
16448        };
16449
16450        assert_eq!(
16451            pipe.server.recv(&mut buf[..written], info),
16452            Err(Error::IdLimit)
16453        );
16454
16455        let written = match pipe.server.send(&mut buf) {
16456            Ok((write, _)) => write,
16457
16458            Err(_) => unreachable!(),
16459        };
16460
16461        let frames =
16462            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut buf[..written]).unwrap();
16463        let mut iter = frames.iter();
16464
16465        assert_eq!(
16466            iter.next(),
16467            Some(&frame::Frame::ConnectionClose {
16468                error_code: 0x9,
16469                frame_type: 0,
16470                reason: Vec::new(),
16471            })
16472        );
16473    }
16474
16475    // Utility function.
16476    fn pipe_with_exchanged_cids(
16477        config: &mut Config, client_scid_len: usize, server_scid_len: usize,
16478        additional_cids: usize,
16479    ) -> testing::Pipe {
16480        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config_and_scid_lengths(
16481            config,
16482            client_scid_len,
16483            server_scid_len,
16484        )
16485        .unwrap();
16486        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
16487
16488        let mut c_cids = Vec::new();
16489        let mut c_reset_tokens = Vec::new();
16490        let mut s_cids = Vec::new();
16491        let mut s_reset_tokens = Vec::new();
16492
16493        for i in 0..additional_cids {
16494            if client_scid_len > 0 {
16495                let (c_cid, c_reset_token) =
16496                    testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(client_scid_len);
16497                c_cids.push(c_cid);
16498                c_reset_tokens.push(c_reset_token);
16499
16500                assert_eq!(
16501                    pipe.client.new_scid(&c_cids[i], c_reset_tokens[i], true),
16502                    Ok(i as u64 + 1)
16503                );
16504            }
16505
16506            if server_scid_len > 0 {
16507                let (s_cid, s_reset_token) =
16508                    testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(server_scid_len);
16509                s_cids.push(s_cid);
16510                s_reset_tokens.push(s_reset_token);
16511                assert_eq!(
16512                    pipe.server.new_scid(&s_cids[i], s_reset_tokens[i], true),
16513                    Ok(i as u64 + 1)
16514                );
16515            }
16516        }
16517
16518        // Let exchange packets over the connection.
16519        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16520
16521        if client_scid_len > 0 {
16522            assert_eq!(pipe.server.available_dcids(), additional_cids);
16523        }
16524
16525        if server_scid_len > 0 {
16526            assert_eq!(pipe.client.available_dcids(), additional_cids);
16527        }
16528
16529        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16530        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16531
16532        pipe
16533    }
16534
16535    #[test]
16536    fn path_validation() {
16537        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16538        config
16539            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16540            .unwrap();
16541        config
16542            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16543            .unwrap();
16544        config
16545            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16546            .unwrap();
16547        config.verify_peer(false);
16548        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
16549
16550        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
16551        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
16552
16553        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
16554        let client_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:5678".parse().unwrap();
16555
16556        // We cannot probe a new path if there are not enough identifiers.
16557        assert_eq!(
16558            pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr),
16559            Err(Error::OutOfIdentifiers)
16560        );
16561
16562        let (c_cid, c_reset_token) = testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(16);
16563
16564        assert_eq!(pipe.client.new_scid(&c_cid, c_reset_token, true), Ok(1));
16565
16566        let (s_cid, s_reset_token) = testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(16);
16567        assert_eq!(pipe.server.new_scid(&s_cid, s_reset_token, true), Ok(1));
16568
16569        // We need to exchange the CIDs first.
16570        assert_eq!(
16571            pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr),
16572            Err(Error::OutOfIdentifiers)
16573        );
16574
16575        // Let exchange packets over the connection.
16576        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16577
16578        assert_eq!(pipe.server.available_dcids(), 1);
16579        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16580        assert_eq!(pipe.client.available_dcids(), 1);
16581        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16582
16583        // Now the path probing can work.
16584        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
16585
16586        // But the server cannot probe a yet-unseen path.
16587        assert_eq!(
16588            pipe.server.probe_path(server_addr, client_addr_2),
16589            Err(Error::InvalidState),
16590        );
16591
16592        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16593
16594        // The path should be validated at some point.
16595        assert_eq!(
16596            pipe.client.path_event_next(),
16597            Some(PathEvent::Validated(client_addr_2, server_addr)),
16598        );
16599        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16600
16601        // The server should be notified of this new path.
16602        assert_eq!(
16603            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
16604            Some(PathEvent::New(server_addr, client_addr_2)),
16605        );
16606        assert_eq!(
16607            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
16608            Some(PathEvent::Validated(server_addr, client_addr_2)),
16609        );
16610        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16611
16612        // The server can later probe the path again.
16613        assert_eq!(pipe.server.probe_path(server_addr, client_addr_2), Ok(1));
16614
16615        // This should not trigger any event at client side.
16616        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16617        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16618    }
16619
16620    #[test]
16621    fn losing_probing_packets() {
16622        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16623        config
16624            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16625            .unwrap();
16626        config
16627            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16628            .unwrap();
16629        config
16630            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16631            .unwrap();
16632        config.verify_peer(false);
16633        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
16634
16635        let mut pipe = pipe_with_exchanged_cids(&mut config, 16, 16, 1);
16636
16637        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
16638        let client_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:5678".parse().unwrap();
16639        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
16640
16641        // The client creates the PATH CHALLENGE, but it is lost.
16642        testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.client).unwrap();
16643
16644        // Wait until probing timer expires. Since the RTT is very low,
16645        // wait a bit more.
16646        let probed_pid = pipe
16647            .client
16648            .paths
16649            .path_id_from_addrs(&(client_addr_2, server_addr))
16650            .unwrap();
16651        let probe_instant = pipe
16652            .client
16653            .paths
16654            .get(probed_pid)
16655            .unwrap()
16656            .recovery
16657            .loss_detection_timer()
16658            .unwrap();
16659        let timer = probe_instant.duration_since(time::Instant::now());
16660        std::thread::sleep(timer + time::Duration::from_millis(1));
16661
16662        pipe.client.on_timeout();
16663
16664        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16665
16666        // The path should be validated at some point.
16667        assert_eq!(
16668            pipe.client.path_event_next(),
16669            Some(PathEvent::Validated(client_addr_2, server_addr))
16670        );
16671        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16672
16673        assert_eq!(
16674            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
16675            Some(PathEvent::New(server_addr, client_addr_2))
16676        );
16677        // The path should be validated at some point.
16678        assert_eq!(
16679            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
16680            Some(PathEvent::Validated(server_addr, client_addr_2))
16681        );
16682        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16683    }
16684
16685    #[test]
16686    fn failed_path_validation() {
16687        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16688        config
16689            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16690            .unwrap();
16691        config
16692            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16693            .unwrap();
16694        config
16695            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16696            .unwrap();
16697        config.verify_peer(false);
16698        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
16699
16700        let mut pipe = pipe_with_exchanged_cids(&mut config, 16, 16, 1);
16701
16702        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
16703        let client_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:5678".parse().unwrap();
16704        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
16705
16706        for _ in 0..MAX_PROBING_TIMEOUTS {
16707            // The client creates the PATH CHALLENGE, but it is always lost.
16708            testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.client).unwrap();
16709
16710            // Wait until probing timer expires. Since the RTT is very low,
16711            // wait a bit more.
16712            let probed_pid = pipe
16713                .client
16714                .paths
16715                .path_id_from_addrs(&(client_addr_2, server_addr))
16716                .unwrap();
16717            let probe_instant = pipe
16718                .client
16719                .paths
16720                .get(probed_pid)
16721                .unwrap()
16722                .recovery
16723                .loss_detection_timer()
16724                .unwrap();
16725            let timer = probe_instant.duration_since(time::Instant::now());
16726            std::thread::sleep(timer + time::Duration::from_millis(1));
16727
16728            pipe.client.on_timeout();
16729        }
16730
16731        assert_eq!(
16732            pipe.client.path_event_next(),
16733            Some(PathEvent::FailedValidation(client_addr_2, server_addr)),
16734        );
16735    }
16736
16737    #[test]
16738    fn client_discard_unknown_address() {
16739        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16740        config
16741            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16742            .unwrap();
16743        config
16744            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16745            .unwrap();
16746        config
16747            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16748            .unwrap();
16749        config.verify_peer(false);
16750        config.set_initial_max_data(30);
16751        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_uni(10);
16752        config.set_initial_max_streams_uni(3);
16753
16754        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
16755        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
16756
16757        // Server sends stream data.
16758        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(3, b"a", true), Ok(1));
16759
16760        let mut flight =
16761            testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.server).expect("no packet");
16762        // Let's change the address info.
16763        flight
16764            .iter_mut()
16765            .for_each(|(_, si)| si.from = "127.0.0.1:9292".parse().unwrap());
16766        assert_eq!(testing::process_flight(&mut pipe.client, flight), Ok(()));
16767        assert_eq!(pipe.client.paths.len(), 1);
16768    }
16769
16770    #[test]
16771    fn path_validation_limited_mtu() {
16772        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16773        config
16774            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16775            .unwrap();
16776        config
16777            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16778            .unwrap();
16779        config
16780            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16781            .unwrap();
16782        config.verify_peer(false);
16783        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
16784
16785        let mut pipe = pipe_with_exchanged_cids(&mut config, 16, 16, 1);
16786
16787        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
16788        let client_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:5678".parse().unwrap();
16789        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
16790        // Limited MTU of 1199 bytes for some reason.
16791        testing::process_flight(
16792            &mut pipe.server,
16793            testing::emit_flight_with_max_buffer(
16794                &mut pipe.client,
16795                1199,
16796                None,
16797                None,
16798            )
16799            .expect("no packet"),
16800        )
16801        .expect("error when processing client packets");
16802        testing::process_flight(
16803            &mut pipe.client,
16804            testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.server).expect("no packet"),
16805        )
16806        .expect("error when processing client packets");
16807        let probed_pid = pipe
16808            .client
16809            .paths
16810            .path_id_from_addrs(&(client_addr_2, server_addr))
16811            .unwrap();
16812        assert!(!pipe.client.paths.get(probed_pid).unwrap().validated(),);
16813        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16814        // Now let the client probe at its MTU.
16815        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16816        assert!(pipe.client.paths.get(probed_pid).unwrap().validated());
16817        assert_eq!(
16818            pipe.client.path_event_next(),
16819            Some(PathEvent::Validated(client_addr_2, server_addr))
16820        );
16821    }
16822
16823    #[test]
16824    fn path_probing_dos() {
16825        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16826        config
16827            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16828            .unwrap();
16829        config
16830            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16831            .unwrap();
16832        config
16833            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16834            .unwrap();
16835        config.verify_peer(false);
16836        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
16837
16838        let mut pipe = pipe_with_exchanged_cids(&mut config, 16, 16, 1);
16839
16840        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
16841        let client_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:5678".parse().unwrap();
16842        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
16843
16844        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
16845
16846        // The path should be validated at some point.
16847        assert_eq!(
16848            pipe.client.path_event_next(),
16849            Some(PathEvent::Validated(client_addr_2, server_addr))
16850        );
16851        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
16852
16853        // The server should be notified of this new path.
16854        assert_eq!(
16855            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
16856            Some(PathEvent::New(server_addr, client_addr_2))
16857        );
16858        assert_eq!(
16859            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
16860            Some(PathEvent::Validated(server_addr, client_addr_2))
16861        );
16862        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16863
16864        assert_eq!(pipe.server.paths.len(), 2);
16865
16866        // Now forge a packet reusing the unverified path's CID over another
16867        // 4-tuple.
16868        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
16869        let client_addr_3 = "127.0.0.1:9012".parse().unwrap();
16870        let mut flight =
16871            testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.client).expect("no generated packet");
16872        flight
16873            .iter_mut()
16874            .for_each(|(_, si)| si.from = client_addr_3);
16875        testing::process_flight(&mut pipe.server, flight)
16876            .expect("failed to process");
16877        assert_eq!(pipe.server.paths.len(), 2);
16878        assert_eq!(
16879            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
16880            Some(PathEvent::ReusedSourceConnectionId(
16881                1,
16882                (server_addr, client_addr_2),
16883                (server_addr, client_addr_3)
16884            ))
16885        );
16886        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
16887    }
16888
16889    #[test]
16890    fn retiring_active_path_dcid() {
16891        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16892        config
16893            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16894            .unwrap();
16895        config
16896            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16897            .unwrap();
16898        config
16899            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16900            .unwrap();
16901        config.verify_peer(false);
16902        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
16903
16904        let mut pipe = pipe_with_exchanged_cids(&mut config, 16, 16, 1);
16905        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
16906        let client_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:5678".parse().unwrap();
16907        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
16908
16909        assert_eq!(pipe.client.retire_dcid(0), Err(Error::OutOfIdentifiers));
16910    }
16911
16912    #[test]
16913    fn send_on_path_test() {
16914        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
16915        config
16916            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
16917            .unwrap();
16918        config
16919            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
16920            .unwrap();
16921        config
16922            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
16923            .unwrap();
16924        config.verify_peer(false);
16925        config.set_initial_max_data(100000);
16926        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(100000);
16927        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(100000);
16928        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(2);
16929        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(4);
16930
16931        let mut pipe = pipe_with_exchanged_cids(&mut config, 16, 16, 3);
16932
16933        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
16934        let client_addr = testing::Pipe::client_addr();
16935        let client_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:5678".parse().unwrap();
16936        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
16937
16938        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
16939        // There is nothing to send on the initial path.
16940        assert_eq!(
16941            pipe.client.send_on_path(
16942                &mut buf,
16943                Some(client_addr),
16944                Some(server_addr)
16945            ),
16946            Err(Error::Done)
16947        );
16948
16949        // Client should send padded PATH_CHALLENGE.
16950        let (sent, si) = pipe
16951            .client
16952            .send_on_path(&mut buf, Some(client_addr_2), Some(server_addr))
16953            .expect("No error");
16954        assert_eq!(sent, MIN_CLIENT_INITIAL_LEN);
16955        assert_eq!(si.from, client_addr_2);
16956        assert_eq!(si.to, server_addr);
16957
16958        let ri = RecvInfo {
16959            to: si.to,
16960            from: si.from,
16961        };
16962        assert_eq!(pipe.server.recv(&mut buf[..sent], ri), Ok(sent));
16963
16964        let stats = pipe.server.stats();
16965        assert_eq!(stats.path_challenge_rx_count, 1);
16966
16967        // A non-existing 4-tuple raises an InvalidState.
16968        let client_addr_3 = "127.0.0.1:9012".parse().unwrap();
16969        let server_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:9876".parse().unwrap();
16970        assert_eq!(
16971            pipe.client.send_on_path(
16972                &mut buf,
16973                Some(client_addr_3),
16974                Some(server_addr)
16975            ),
16976            Err(Error::InvalidState)
16977        );
16978        assert_eq!(
16979            pipe.client.send_on_path(
16980                &mut buf,
16981                Some(client_addr),
16982                Some(server_addr_2)
16983            ),
16984            Err(Error::InvalidState)
16985        );
16986
16987        // Let's introduce some additional path challenges and data exchange.
16988        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr, server_addr_2), Ok(2));
16989        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_3, server_addr), Ok(3));
16990        // Just to fit in two packets.
16991        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, &buf[..1201], true), Ok(1201));
16992
16993        // PATH_CHALLENGE
16994        let (sent, si) = pipe
16995            .client
16996            .send_on_path(&mut buf, Some(client_addr), None)
16997            .expect("No error");
16998        assert_eq!(sent, MIN_CLIENT_INITIAL_LEN);
16999        assert_eq!(si.from, client_addr);
17000        assert_eq!(si.to, server_addr_2);
17001
17002        let ri = RecvInfo {
17003            to: si.to,
17004            from: si.from,
17005        };
17006        assert_eq!(pipe.server.recv(&mut buf[..sent], ri), Ok(sent));
17007
17008        let stats = pipe.server.stats();
17009        assert_eq!(stats.path_challenge_rx_count, 2);
17010
17011        // STREAM frame on active path.
17012        let (sent, si) = pipe
17013            .client
17014            .send_on_path(&mut buf, Some(client_addr), None)
17015            .expect("No error");
17016        assert_eq!(si.from, client_addr);
17017        assert_eq!(si.to, server_addr);
17018
17019        let ri = RecvInfo {
17020            to: si.to,
17021            from: si.from,
17022        };
17023        assert_eq!(pipe.server.recv(&mut buf[..sent], ri), Ok(sent));
17024
17025        let stats = pipe.server.stats();
17026        assert_eq!(stats.path_challenge_rx_count, 2);
17027
17028        // PATH_CHALLENGE
17029        let (sent, si) = pipe
17030            .client
17031            .send_on_path(&mut buf, None, Some(server_addr))
17032            .expect("No error");
17033        assert_eq!(sent, MIN_CLIENT_INITIAL_LEN);
17034        assert_eq!(si.from, client_addr_3);
17035        assert_eq!(si.to, server_addr);
17036
17037        let ri = RecvInfo {
17038            to: si.to,
17039            from: si.from,
17040        };
17041        assert_eq!(pipe.server.recv(&mut buf[..sent], ri), Ok(sent));
17042
17043        let stats = pipe.server.stats();
17044        assert_eq!(stats.path_challenge_rx_count, 3);
17045
17046        // STREAM frame on active path.
17047        let (sent, si) = pipe
17048            .client
17049            .send_on_path(&mut buf, None, Some(server_addr))
17050            .expect("No error");
17051        assert_eq!(si.from, client_addr);
17052        assert_eq!(si.to, server_addr);
17053
17054        let ri = RecvInfo {
17055            to: si.to,
17056            from: si.from,
17057        };
17058        assert_eq!(pipe.server.recv(&mut buf[..sent], ri), Ok(sent));
17059
17060        // No more data to exchange leads to Error::Done.
17061        assert_eq!(
17062            pipe.client.send_on_path(&mut buf, Some(client_addr), None),
17063            Err(Error::Done)
17064        );
17065        assert_eq!(
17066            pipe.client.send_on_path(&mut buf, None, Some(server_addr)),
17067            Err(Error::Done)
17068        );
17069
17070        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
17071
17072        let mut v1 = pipe.client.paths_iter(client_addr).collect::<Vec<_>>();
17073        let mut v2 = vec![server_addr, server_addr_2];
17074
17075        v1.sort();
17076        v2.sort();
17077
17078        assert_eq!(v1, v2);
17079
17080        let mut v1 = pipe.client.paths_iter(client_addr_2).collect::<Vec<_>>();
17081        let mut v2 = vec![server_addr];
17082
17083        v1.sort();
17084        v2.sort();
17085
17086        assert_eq!(v1, v2);
17087
17088        let mut v1 = pipe.client.paths_iter(client_addr_3).collect::<Vec<_>>();
17089        let mut v2 = vec![server_addr];
17090
17091        v1.sort();
17092        v2.sort();
17093
17094        assert_eq!(v1, v2);
17095
17096        let stats = pipe.server.stats();
17097        assert_eq!(stats.path_challenge_rx_count, 3);
17098    }
17099
17100    #[test]
17101    fn connection_migration() {
17102        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
17103        config
17104            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
17105            .unwrap();
17106        config
17107            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
17108            .unwrap();
17109        config
17110            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
17111            .unwrap();
17112        config.verify_peer(false);
17113        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(3);
17114        config.set_initial_max_data(30);
17115        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(15);
17116        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(15);
17117        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_uni(10);
17118        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(3);
17119
17120        let mut pipe = pipe_with_exchanged_cids(&mut config, 16, 16, 2);
17121
17122        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
17123        let client_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:5678".parse().unwrap();
17124        let client_addr_3 = "127.0.0.1:9012".parse().unwrap();
17125        let client_addr_4 = "127.0.0.1:8908".parse().unwrap();
17126
17127        // Case 1: the client first probes the new address, the server too, and
17128        // then migrates.
17129        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
17130        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
17131        assert_eq!(
17132            pipe.client.path_event_next(),
17133            Some(PathEvent::Validated(client_addr_2, server_addr))
17134        );
17135        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
17136        assert_eq!(
17137            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17138            Some(PathEvent::New(server_addr, client_addr_2))
17139        );
17140        assert_eq!(
17141            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17142            Some(PathEvent::Validated(server_addr, client_addr_2))
17143        );
17144        assert_eq!(
17145            pipe.client.is_path_validated(client_addr_2, server_addr),
17146            Ok(true)
17147        );
17148        assert_eq!(
17149            pipe.server.is_path_validated(server_addr, client_addr_2),
17150            Ok(true)
17151        );
17152        // The server can never initiates the connection migration.
17153        assert_eq!(
17154            pipe.server.migrate(server_addr, client_addr_2),
17155            Err(Error::InvalidState)
17156        );
17157        assert_eq!(pipe.client.migrate(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
17158        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"data", true), Ok(4));
17159        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
17160        assert_eq!(
17161            pipe.client
17162                .paths
17163                .get_active()
17164                .expect("no active")
17165                .local_addr(),
17166            client_addr_2
17167        );
17168        assert_eq!(
17169            pipe.client
17170                .paths
17171                .get_active()
17172                .expect("no active")
17173                .peer_addr(),
17174            server_addr
17175        );
17176        assert_eq!(
17177            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17178            Some(PathEvent::PeerMigrated(server_addr, client_addr_2))
17179        );
17180        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
17181        assert_eq!(
17182            pipe.server
17183                .paths
17184                .get_active()
17185                .expect("no active")
17186                .local_addr(),
17187            server_addr
17188        );
17189        assert_eq!(
17190            pipe.server
17191                .paths
17192                .get_active()
17193                .expect("no active")
17194                .peer_addr(),
17195            client_addr_2
17196        );
17197
17198        // Case 2: the client migrates on a path that was not previously
17199        // validated, and has spare SCIDs/DCIDs to do so.
17200        assert_eq!(pipe.client.migrate(client_addr_3, server_addr), Ok(2));
17201        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"data", true), Ok(4));
17202        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
17203        assert_eq!(
17204            pipe.client
17205                .paths
17206                .get_active()
17207                .expect("no active")
17208                .local_addr(),
17209            client_addr_3
17210        );
17211        assert_eq!(
17212            pipe.client
17213                .paths
17214                .get_active()
17215                .expect("no active")
17216                .peer_addr(),
17217            server_addr
17218        );
17219        assert_eq!(
17220            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17221            Some(PathEvent::New(server_addr, client_addr_3))
17222        );
17223        assert_eq!(
17224            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17225            Some(PathEvent::Validated(server_addr, client_addr_3))
17226        );
17227        assert_eq!(
17228            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17229            Some(PathEvent::PeerMigrated(server_addr, client_addr_3))
17230        );
17231        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
17232        assert_eq!(
17233            pipe.server
17234                .paths
17235                .get_active()
17236                .expect("no active")
17237                .local_addr(),
17238            server_addr
17239        );
17240        assert_eq!(
17241            pipe.server
17242                .paths
17243                .get_active()
17244                .expect("no active")
17245                .peer_addr(),
17246            client_addr_3
17247        );
17248
17249        // Case 3: the client tries to migrate on the current active path.
17250        // This is not an error, but it triggers nothing.
17251        assert_eq!(pipe.client.migrate(client_addr_3, server_addr), Ok(2));
17252        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(8, b"data", true), Ok(4));
17253        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
17254        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
17255        assert_eq!(
17256            pipe.client
17257                .paths
17258                .get_active()
17259                .expect("no active")
17260                .local_addr(),
17261            client_addr_3
17262        );
17263        assert_eq!(
17264            pipe.client
17265                .paths
17266                .get_active()
17267                .expect("no active")
17268                .peer_addr(),
17269            server_addr
17270        );
17271        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
17272        assert_eq!(
17273            pipe.server
17274                .paths
17275                .get_active()
17276                .expect("no active")
17277                .local_addr(),
17278            server_addr
17279        );
17280        assert_eq!(
17281            pipe.server
17282                .paths
17283                .get_active()
17284                .expect("no active")
17285                .peer_addr(),
17286            client_addr_3
17287        );
17288
17289        // Case 4: the client tries to migrate on a path that was not previously
17290        // validated, and has no spare SCIDs/DCIDs. Prevent active migration.
17291        assert_eq!(
17292            pipe.client.migrate(client_addr_4, server_addr),
17293            Err(Error::OutOfIdentifiers)
17294        );
17295        assert_eq!(
17296            pipe.client
17297                .paths
17298                .get_active()
17299                .expect("no active")
17300                .local_addr(),
17301            client_addr_3
17302        );
17303        assert_eq!(
17304            pipe.client
17305                .paths
17306                .get_active()
17307                .expect("no active")
17308                .peer_addr(),
17309            server_addr
17310        );
17311    }
17312
17313    #[test]
17314    fn connection_migration_zero_length_cid() {
17315        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
17316        config
17317            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
17318            .unwrap();
17319        config
17320            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
17321            .unwrap();
17322        config
17323            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
17324            .unwrap();
17325        config.verify_peer(false);
17326        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
17327        config.set_initial_max_data(30);
17328        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(15);
17329        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(15);
17330        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_uni(10);
17331        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(3);
17332
17333        let mut pipe = pipe_with_exchanged_cids(&mut config, 0, 16, 1);
17334
17335        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
17336        let client_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:5678".parse().unwrap();
17337
17338        // The client migrates on a path that was not previously
17339        // validated, and has spare SCIDs/DCIDs to do so.
17340        assert_eq!(pipe.client.migrate(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
17341        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"data", true), Ok(4));
17342        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
17343        assert_eq!(
17344            pipe.client
17345                .paths
17346                .get_active()
17347                .expect("no active")
17348                .local_addr(),
17349            client_addr_2
17350        );
17351        assert_eq!(
17352            pipe.client
17353                .paths
17354                .get_active()
17355                .expect("no active")
17356                .peer_addr(),
17357            server_addr
17358        );
17359        assert_eq!(
17360            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17361            Some(PathEvent::New(server_addr, client_addr_2))
17362        );
17363        assert_eq!(
17364            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17365            Some(PathEvent::Validated(server_addr, client_addr_2))
17366        );
17367        assert_eq!(
17368            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17369            Some(PathEvent::PeerMigrated(server_addr, client_addr_2))
17370        );
17371        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
17372        assert_eq!(
17373            pipe.server
17374                .paths
17375                .get_active()
17376                .expect("no active")
17377                .local_addr(),
17378            server_addr
17379        );
17380        assert_eq!(
17381            pipe.server
17382                .paths
17383                .get_active()
17384                .expect("no active")
17385                .peer_addr(),
17386            client_addr_2
17387        );
17388    }
17389
17390    #[test]
17391    fn connection_migration_reordered_non_probing() {
17392        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
17393        config
17394            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
17395            .unwrap();
17396        config
17397            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
17398            .unwrap();
17399        config
17400            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
17401            .unwrap();
17402        config.verify_peer(false);
17403        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(2);
17404        config.set_initial_max_data(30);
17405        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(15);
17406        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(15);
17407        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_uni(10);
17408        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(3);
17409
17410        let mut pipe = pipe_with_exchanged_cids(&mut config, 16, 16, 1);
17411
17412        let client_addr = testing::Pipe::client_addr();
17413        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
17414        let client_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:5678".parse().unwrap();
17415
17416        assert_eq!(pipe.client.probe_path(client_addr_2, server_addr), Ok(1));
17417        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
17418        assert_eq!(
17419            pipe.client.path_event_next(),
17420            Some(PathEvent::Validated(client_addr_2, server_addr))
17421        );
17422        assert_eq!(pipe.client.path_event_next(), None);
17423        assert_eq!(
17424            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17425            Some(PathEvent::New(server_addr, client_addr_2))
17426        );
17427        assert_eq!(
17428            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17429            Some(PathEvent::Validated(server_addr, client_addr_2))
17430        );
17431        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
17432
17433        // A first flight sent from secondary address.
17434        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"data", true), Ok(4));
17435        let mut first = testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.client).unwrap();
17436        first.iter_mut().for_each(|(_, si)| si.from = client_addr_2);
17437        // A second one, but sent from the original one.
17438        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"data", true), Ok(4));
17439        let second = testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.client).unwrap();
17440        // Second flight is received before first one.
17441        assert_eq!(testing::process_flight(&mut pipe.server, second), Ok(()));
17442        assert_eq!(testing::process_flight(&mut pipe.server, first), Ok(()));
17443
17444        // Server does not perform connection migration because of packet
17445        // reordering.
17446        assert_eq!(pipe.server.path_event_next(), None);
17447        assert_eq!(
17448            pipe.server
17449                .paths
17450                .get_active()
17451                .expect("no active")
17452                .peer_addr(),
17453            client_addr
17454        );
17455    }
17456
17457    #[test]
17458    fn resilience_against_migration_attack() {
17459        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
17460        config
17461            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
17462            .unwrap();
17463        config
17464            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
17465            .unwrap();
17466        config
17467            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
17468            .unwrap();
17469        config.verify_peer(false);
17470        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(3);
17471        config.set_initial_max_data(100000);
17472        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(100000);
17473        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(100000);
17474        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(2);
17475
17476        let mut pipe = pipe_with_exchanged_cids(&mut config, 16, 16, 1);
17477
17478        let client_addr = testing::Pipe::client_addr();
17479        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
17480        let spoofed_client_addr = "127.0.0.1:6666".parse().unwrap();
17481
17482        const DATA_BYTES: usize = 24000;
17483        let buf = [42; DATA_BYTES];
17484        let mut recv_buf = [0; DATA_BYTES];
17485        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(1, &buf, true), Ok(12000));
17486        assert_eq!(
17487            testing::process_flight(
17488                &mut pipe.client,
17489                testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.server).unwrap()
17490            ),
17491            Ok(())
17492        );
17493        let (rcv_data_1, _) = pipe.client.stream_recv(1, &mut recv_buf).unwrap();
17494
17495        // Fake the source address of client.
17496        let mut faked_addr_flight =
17497            testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.client).unwrap();
17498        faked_addr_flight
17499            .iter_mut()
17500            .for_each(|(_, si)| si.from = spoofed_client_addr);
17501        assert_eq!(
17502            testing::process_flight(&mut pipe.server, faked_addr_flight),
17503            Ok(())
17504        );
17505        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(1, &buf[12000..], true), Ok(12000));
17506        assert_eq!(
17507            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17508            Some(PathEvent::ReusedSourceConnectionId(
17509                0,
17510                (server_addr, client_addr),
17511                (server_addr, spoofed_client_addr)
17512            ))
17513        );
17514        assert_eq!(
17515            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17516            Some(PathEvent::New(server_addr, spoofed_client_addr))
17517        );
17518
17519        assert_eq!(
17520            pipe.server.is_path_validated(server_addr, client_addr),
17521            Ok(true)
17522        );
17523        assert_eq!(
17524            pipe.server
17525                .is_path_validated(server_addr, spoofed_client_addr),
17526            Ok(false)
17527        );
17528
17529        // The client creates the PATH CHALLENGE, but it is always lost.
17530        testing::emit_flight(&mut pipe.server).unwrap();
17531
17532        // Wait until probing timer expires. Since the RTT is very low,
17533        // wait a bit more.
17534        let probed_pid = pipe
17535            .server
17536            .paths
17537            .path_id_from_addrs(&(server_addr, spoofed_client_addr))
17538            .unwrap();
17539        let probe_instant = pipe
17540            .server
17541            .paths
17542            .get(probed_pid)
17543            .unwrap()
17544            .recovery
17545            .loss_detection_timer()
17546            .unwrap();
17547        let timer = probe_instant.duration_since(time::Instant::now());
17548        std::thread::sleep(timer + time::Duration::from_millis(1));
17549
17550        pipe.server.on_timeout();
17551
17552        // Because of the small ACK size, the server cannot send more to the
17553        // client. Fallback on the previous active path.
17554        assert_eq!(
17555            pipe.server.path_event_next(),
17556            Some(PathEvent::FailedValidation(
17557                server_addr,
17558                spoofed_client_addr
17559            ))
17560        );
17561
17562        assert_eq!(
17563            pipe.server.is_path_validated(server_addr, client_addr),
17564            Ok(true)
17565        );
17566        assert_eq!(
17567            pipe.server
17568                .is_path_validated(server_addr, spoofed_client_addr),
17569            Ok(false)
17570        );
17571
17572        let server_active_path = pipe.server.paths.get_active().unwrap();
17573        assert_eq!(server_active_path.local_addr(), server_addr);
17574        assert_eq!(server_active_path.peer_addr(), client_addr);
17575        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
17576        let (rcv_data_2, fin) =
17577            pipe.client.stream_recv(1, &mut recv_buf).unwrap();
17578        assert!(fin);
17579        assert_eq!(rcv_data_1 + rcv_data_2, DATA_BYTES);
17580    }
17581
17582    #[test]
17583    fn consecutive_non_ack_eliciting() {
17584        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
17585
17586        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
17587        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
17588
17589        // Client sends a bunch of PING frames, causing server to ACK (ACKs aren't
17590        // ack-eliciting)
17591        let frames = [frame::Frame::Ping { mtu_probe: None }];
17592        let pkt_type = packet::Type::Short;
17593        for _ in 0..24 {
17594            let len = pipe
17595                .send_pkt_to_server(pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf)
17596                .unwrap();
17597            assert!(len > 0);
17598
17599            let frames =
17600                testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
17601            assert!(
17602                frames
17603                    .iter()
17604                    .all(|frame| matches!(frame, frame::Frame::ACK { .. })),
17605                "ACK only"
17606            );
17607        }
17608
17609        // After 24 non-ack-eliciting, an ACK is explicitly elicited with a PING
17610        let len = pipe
17611            .send_pkt_to_server(pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf)
17612            .unwrap();
17613        assert!(len > 0);
17614
17615        let frames =
17616            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
17617        assert!(
17618            frames
17619                .iter()
17620                .any(|frame| matches!(frame, frame::Frame::Ping {
17621                    mtu_probe: None
17622                })),
17623            "found a PING"
17624        );
17625    }
17626
17627    #[test]
17628    fn send_ack_eliciting_causes_ping() {
17629        // First establish a connection
17630        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
17631        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
17632
17633        // Queue a PING frame
17634        pipe.server.send_ack_eliciting().unwrap();
17635
17636        // Make sure ping is sent
17637        let mut buf = [0; 1500];
17638        let (len, _) = pipe.server.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
17639
17640        let frames =
17641            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
17642        let mut iter = frames.iter();
17643
17644        assert_eq!(iter.next(), Some(&frame::Frame::Ping { mtu_probe: None }));
17645    }
17646
17647    #[test]
17648    fn send_ack_eliciting_no_ping() {
17649        // First establish a connection
17650        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::new().unwrap();
17651        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
17652
17653        // Queue a PING frame
17654        pipe.server.send_ack_eliciting().unwrap();
17655
17656        // Send a stream frame, which is ACK-eliciting to make sure the ping is
17657        // not sent
17658        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(1, b"a", false), Ok(1));
17659
17660        // Make sure ping is not sent
17661        let mut buf = [0; 1500];
17662        let (len, _) = pipe.server.send(&mut buf).unwrap();
17663
17664        let frames =
17665            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
17666        let mut iter = frames.iter();
17667
17668        assert!(matches!(
17669            iter.next(),
17670            Some(&frame::Frame::Stream {
17671                stream_id: 1,
17672                data: _
17673            })
17674        ));
17675        assert!(iter.next().is_none());
17676    }
17677
17678    /// Tests that streams do not keep being "writable" after being collected
17679    /// on reset.
17680    #[test]
17681    fn stop_sending_stream_send_after_reset_stream_ack() {
17682        let mut b = [0; 15];
17683
17684        let mut buf = [0; 65535];
17685
17686        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
17687        config
17688            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
17689            .unwrap();
17690        config
17691            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
17692            .unwrap();
17693        config
17694            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
17695            .unwrap();
17696        config.set_initial_max_data(999999999);
17697        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(30);
17698        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(30);
17699        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_uni(30);
17700        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(1000);
17701        config.set_initial_max_streams_uni(0);
17702        config.verify_peer(false);
17703
17704        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
17705        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
17706
17707        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 0);
17708        assert_eq!(pipe.server.readable().len(), 0);
17709        assert_eq!(pipe.server.writable().len(), 0);
17710
17711        // Client opens a load of streams
17712        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(0, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
17713        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(4, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
17714        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(8, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
17715        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(12, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
17716        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(16, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
17717        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(20, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
17718        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(24, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
17719        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(28, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
17720        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(32, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
17721        assert_eq!(pipe.client.stream_send(36, b"hello", true), Ok(5));
17722        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
17723
17724        // Server iterators are populated
17725        let mut r = pipe.server.readable();
17726        assert_eq!(r.len(), 10);
17727        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(0));
17728        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(4));
17729        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(8));
17730        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(12));
17731        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(16));
17732        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(20));
17733        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(24));
17734        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(28));
17735        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(32));
17736        assert_eq!(r.next(), Some(36));
17737
17738        assert_eq!(r.next(), None);
17739
17740        let mut w = pipe.server.writable();
17741        assert_eq!(w.len(), 10);
17742        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(0));
17743        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(4));
17744        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(8));
17745        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(12));
17746        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(16));
17747        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(20));
17748        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(24));
17749        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(28));
17750        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(32));
17751        assert_eq!(w.next(), Some(36));
17752        assert_eq!(w.next(), None);
17753
17754        // Read one stream
17755        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_recv(0, &mut b), Ok((5, true)));
17756        assert!(pipe.server.stream_finished(0));
17757
17758        assert_eq!(pipe.server.readable().len(), 9);
17759        assert_eq!(pipe.server.writable().len(), 10);
17760
17761        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_writable(0, 0), Ok(true));
17762
17763        // Server sends data on stream 0, until blocked.
17764        while pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"world", false) != Err(Error::Done) {
17765            assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
17766        }
17767
17768        assert_eq!(pipe.server.writable().len(), 9);
17769        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_writable(0, 0), Ok(true));
17770
17771        // Client sends STOP_SENDING.
17772        let frames = [frame::Frame::StopSending {
17773            stream_id: 0,
17774            error_code: 42,
17775        }];
17776
17777        let pkt_type = packet::Type::Short;
17778        let len = pipe
17779            .send_pkt_to_server(pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf)
17780            .unwrap();
17781
17782        // Server sent a RESET_STREAM frame in response.
17783        let frames =
17784            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
17785
17786        let mut iter = frames.iter();
17787
17788        // Skip ACK frame.
17789        iter.next();
17790
17791        assert_eq!(
17792            iter.next(),
17793            Some(&frame::Frame::ResetStream {
17794                stream_id: 0,
17795                error_code: 42,
17796                final_size: 30,
17797            })
17798        );
17799
17800        // Stream 0 is now writable in order to make apps aware of STOP_SENDING
17801        // via returning an error.
17802        let mut w = pipe.server.writable();
17803        assert_eq!(w.len(), 10);
17804
17805        assert!(w.any(|s| s == 0));
17806        assert_eq!(
17807            pipe.server.stream_writable(0, 1),
17808            Err(Error::StreamStopped(42))
17809        );
17810
17811        assert_eq!(pipe.server.writable().len(), 10);
17812        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 10);
17813
17814        // Client acks RESET_STREAM frame.
17815        let mut ranges = ranges::RangeSet::default();
17816        ranges.insert(0..12);
17817
17818        let frames = [frame::Frame::ACK {
17819            ack_delay: 15,
17820            ranges,
17821            ecn_counts: None,
17822        }];
17823
17824        assert_eq!(pipe.send_pkt_to_server(pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf), Ok(0));
17825
17826        // Stream is collected on the server after RESET_STREAM is acked.
17827        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 9);
17828
17829        // Sending STOP_SENDING again shouldn't trigger RESET_STREAM again.
17830        let frames = [frame::Frame::StopSending {
17831            stream_id: 0,
17832            error_code: 42,
17833        }];
17834
17835        let len = pipe
17836            .send_pkt_to_server(pkt_type, &frames, &mut buf)
17837            .unwrap();
17838
17839        let frames =
17840            testing::decode_pkt(&mut pipe.client, &mut buf[..len]).unwrap();
17841
17842        assert_eq!(frames.len(), 1);
17843
17844        match frames.first() {
17845            Some(frame::Frame::ACK { .. }) => (),
17846
17847            f => panic!("expected ACK frame, got {:?}", f),
17848        };
17849
17850        assert_eq!(pipe.server.streams.len(), 9);
17851
17852        // Stream 0 has been collected and must not be writable anymore.
17853        let mut w = pipe.server.writable();
17854        assert_eq!(w.len(), 9);
17855        assert!(!w.any(|s| s == 0));
17856
17857        // If we called send before the client ACK of reset stream, it would
17858        // have failed with StreamStopped.
17859        assert_eq!(pipe.server.stream_send(0, b"world", true), Err(Error::Done),);
17860
17861        // Stream 0 is still not writable.
17862        let mut w = pipe.server.writable();
17863        assert_eq!(w.len(), 9);
17864        assert!(!w.any(|s| s == 0));
17865    }
17866
17867    #[test]
17868    fn challenge_no_cids() {
17869        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
17870        config
17871            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
17872            .unwrap();
17873        config
17874            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
17875            .unwrap();
17876        config
17877            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
17878            .unwrap();
17879        config.verify_peer(false);
17880        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(4);
17881        config.set_initial_max_data(30);
17882        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(15);
17883        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(15);
17884        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_uni(10);
17885        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(3);
17886
17887        let mut pipe =
17888            testing::Pipe::with_config_and_scid_lengths(&mut config, 16, 16)
17889                .unwrap();
17890        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
17891
17892        // Server send CIDs to client
17893        let mut server_cids = Vec::new();
17894        for _ in 0..2 {
17895            let (cid, reset_token) = testing::create_cid_and_reset_token(16);
17896            pipe.server
17897                .new_scid(&cid, reset_token, true)
17898                .expect("server issue cid");
17899            server_cids.push(cid);
17900        }
17901        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
17902
17903        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
17904        let client_addr_2 = "127.0.0.1:5678".parse().unwrap();
17905
17906        // Client probes path before sending CIDs (simulating race condition)
17907        let frames = [frame::Frame::PathChallenge {
17908            data: [0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7],
17909        }];
17910        let mut pkt_buf = [0u8; 1500];
17911        let mut b = octets::OctetsMut::with_slice(&mut pkt_buf);
17912        let epoch = packet::Type::Short.to_epoch().unwrap();
17913        let space = &mut pipe.client.pkt_num_spaces[epoch];
17914        let pn = pipe.client.next_pkt_num;
17915        let pn_len = 4;
17916
17917        let hdr = Header {
17918            ty: packet::Type::Short,
17919            version: pipe.client.version,
17920            dcid: server_cids[0].clone(),
17921            scid: ConnectionId::from_ref(&[5, 4, 3, 2, 1]),
17922            pkt_num: 0,
17923            pkt_num_len: pn_len,
17924            token: pipe.client.token.clone(),
17925            versions: None,
17926            key_phase: pipe.client.key_phase,
17927        };
17928        hdr.to_bytes(&mut b).expect("encode header");
17929        let payload_len = frames.iter().fold(0, |acc, x| acc + x.wire_len());
17930        b.put_u32(pn as u32).expect("put pn");
17931
17932        let payload_offset = b.off();
17933
17934        for frame in frames {
17935            frame.to_bytes(&mut b).expect("encode frames");
17936        }
17937
17938        let aead = space.crypto_seal.as_ref().expect("crypto seal");
17939
17940        let written = packet::encrypt_pkt(
17941            &mut b,
17942            pn,
17943            pn_len,
17944            payload_len,
17945            payload_offset,
17946            None,
17947            aead,
17948        )
17949        .expect("packet encrypt");
17950        pipe.client.next_pkt_num += 1;
17951
17952        pipe.server
17953            .recv(&mut pkt_buf[..written], RecvInfo {
17954                to: server_addr,
17955                from: client_addr_2,
17956            })
17957            .expect("server receive path challenge");
17958
17959        // Show that the new path is not considered a destination path by quiche
17960        assert!(!pipe
17961            .server
17962            .paths_iter(server_addr)
17963            .any(|path| path == client_addr_2));
17964    }
17965
17966    #[test]
17967    fn successful_probe_pmtud() {
17968        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
17969        config
17970            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
17971            .unwrap();
17972        config
17973            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
17974            .unwrap();
17975        config
17976            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
17977            .unwrap();
17978        config.verify_peer(false);
17979        config.set_initial_max_data(100000);
17980        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(100000);
17981        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(100000);
17982        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(2);
17983        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(4);
17984        config.set_max_send_udp_payload_size(1350);
17985        config.set_max_recv_udp_payload_size(1350);
17986        config.discover_pmtu(true);
17987
17988        // Perform initial handshake.
17989        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
17990        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
17991
17992        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
17993        let client_addr = testing::Pipe::client_addr();
17994        let pid_1 = pipe
17995            .server
17996            .paths
17997            .path_id_from_addrs(&(server_addr, client_addr))
17998            .expect("no such path");
17999
18000        // Check that PMTU params are configured correctly
18001        let pmtu_param = &mut pipe.server.paths.get_mut(pid_1).unwrap().pmtud;
18002        assert!(pmtu_param.get_probe_status());
18003        assert_eq!(pmtu_param.get_probe_size(), 1350);
18004        assert_eq!(pipe.advance(), Ok(()));
18005
18006        for (_, p) in pipe.server.paths.iter_mut() {
18007            assert_eq!(p.pmtud.get_current(), 1350);
18008            assert!(!p.pmtud.get_probe_status());
18009        }
18010    }
18011
18012    #[test]
18013    fn pmtud_probe_loss() {
18014        let mut config = Config::new(crate::PROTOCOL_VERSION).unwrap();
18015        config
18016            .load_cert_chain_from_pem_file("examples/cert.crt")
18017            .unwrap();
18018        config
18019            .load_priv_key_from_pem_file("examples/cert.key")
18020            .unwrap();
18021        config
18022            .set_application_protos(&[b"proto1", b"proto2"])
18023            .unwrap();
18024        config.verify_peer(false);
18025        config.set_initial_max_data(100000);
18026        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local(100000);
18027        config.set_initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote(100000);
18028        config.set_initial_max_streams_bidi(2);
18029        config.set_active_connection_id_limit(4);
18030        config.set_max_send_udp_payload_size(1350);
18031        config.set_max_recv_udp_payload_size(1250);
18032        config.discover_pmtu(true);
18033
18034        // Perform initial handshake.
18035        let mut pipe = testing::Pipe::with_config(&mut config).unwrap();
18036        assert_eq!(pipe.handshake(), Ok(()));
18037
18038        let server_addr = testing::Pipe::server_addr();
18039        let client_addr = testing::Pipe::client_addr();
18040        let pid_1 = pipe
18041            .server
18042            .paths
18043            .path_id_from_addrs(&(server_addr, client_addr))
18044            .expect("no such path");
18045
18046        // Check that PMTU params are configured correctly
18047        let pmtu_param = &mut pipe.server.paths.get_mut(pid_1).unwrap().pmtud;
18048        assert!(pmtu_param.get_probe_status());
18049        assert_eq!(pmtu_param.get_probe_size(), 1350);
18050        std::thread::sleep(
18051            pipe.server.paths.get_mut(pid_1).unwrap().recovery.rtt() +
18052                time::Duration::from_millis(1),
18053        );
18054
18055        let active_server_path = pipe.server.paths.get_active_mut().unwrap();
18056        let pmtu_param = &mut active_server_path.pmtud;
18057
18058        // PMTU not updated since probe is not ACKed
18059        assert_eq!(pmtu_param.get_current(), 1200);
18060
18061        // Continue searching for PMTU
18062        assert!(pmtu_param.get_probe_status());
18063    }
18064}
